text.NRSV: King David was old and advanced in years; and although they covered him with clothes, he could not get warm.
text.Rotherham: Now, King David, was old, advanced in days,—and they covered him with clothes, but he gat no heat.
text.KJV: Now king David was old and stricken in years; and they covered him with clothes, but he gat no heat.
text.NABRE: I. The Reign of Solomon Chapter 1 David’s Old Age. When King David was old and advanced in years, though they covered him with blankets he could not get warm.
text.DRB: And king David was old, and had many days of age: & when he was covered with cloths, he was not warmed.

text.NRSV: So his servants said to him, “Let a young virgin be sought for my lord the king, and let her wait on the king, and be her attendant; let her lie in your bosom, so that my lord the king may be warm.”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore his servants said unto him—Let there be sought for my lord the king, a young woman—a virgin, so shall she stand before the king, that she may become his companion,—and she shall lie in thy bosom, and my lord the king shall get, heat.
text.KJV: Wherefore his servants said unto him, Let there be sought for my lord the king a young virgin: and let her stand before the king, and let her cherish him, and let her lie in thy bosom, that my lord the king may get heat.
text.NABRE: His servants therefore said to him, “Let a young virgin be sought to attend my lord the king, and to nurse him. If she sleeps with you, my lord the king will be warm.”
text.DRB: His servants therefore said to him: Let us seek for our lord the king a young woman a virgin, and let her stand before the king, and cherish him, and sleep in his bosom, and warm our lord the king.

text.NRSV: So they searched for a beautiful girl throughout all the territory of Israel, and found Abishag the Shunammite, and brought her to the king.
text.Rotherham: So they sought a fair young woman, throughout all the bounds of Israel,—and found Abishag, the Shunammite, and brought her in, unto the king.
text.KJV: So they sought for a fair damsel throughout all the coasts of Israel, and found Abishag a Shunammite, and brought her to the king.
text.NABRE: So they sought for a beautiful girl throughout the territory of Israel, and found Abishag the Shunamite. So they brought her to the king.
text.DRB: They sought therefore a beautiful young woman in all the coasts of Israel, and they found Abisag a Sunamite, & brought her to the king.

text.NRSV: The girl was very beautiful. She became the king’s attendant and served him, but the king did not know her sexually.
text.Rotherham: Now, the young woman, was exceeding fair,—so she became unto the king a companion, and ministered unto him, but, the king, knew her not.
text.KJV: And the damsel was very fair, and cherished the king, and ministered to him: but the king knew her not.
text.NABRE: The girl was very beautiful indeed, and she nursed the king and took care of him. But the king did not have relations with her.
text.DRB: And the damsel was exceeding beautiful, and she slept with the king, and served him, but the king did not know her.

text.NRSV: Now Adonijah son of Haggith exalted himself, saying, “I will be king”; he prepared for himself chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
text.Rotherham: And, Adonijah son of Haggith, exalted himself, saying—I, will be king. Therefore prepared he for himself chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
text.KJV: Then Adonijah the son of Haggith exalted himself, saying, I will be king: and he prepared him chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
text.NABRE: Adonijah’s Ambition. Adonijah, son of Haggith, boasted, “I shall be king!” and he provided himself with chariots, horses, and a retinue of fifty to go before him.
text.DRB: And Adonias the son of Haggith was elevated, saying: I will reign. And he made himself chariots & horsemen, & fifty men, that should run before him.

text.NRSV: His father had never at any time displeased him by asking, “Why have you done thus and so?” He was also a very handsome man, and he was born next after Absalom.
text.Rotherham: Now his father had not displeased him all his days, by saying—Why, thus, hast thou done? Moreover also, he, was of exceeding handsome appearance, and, him, did his mother bear, after Absolom.
text.KJV: And his father had not displeased him at any time in saying, Why hast thou done so? and he also was a very goodly man; and his mother bare him after Absalom.
text.NABRE: Yet his father would never antagonize him by asking, “Why are you doing this?” Adonijah was also very handsome, and next in age to Absalom by the same mother.
text.DRB: Neither did his father control him at any time, saying: Why didst thou this? And he also was very beautiful, the second born after Absalom.

text.NRSV: He conferred with Joab son of Zeruiah and with the priest Abiathar, and they supported Adonijah.
text.Rotherham: So then he had speech with Joab, son of Zeruiah, and with Abiathar the priest,—and they gave help, following Adonijah.
text.KJV: And he conferred with Joab the son of Zeruiah, and with Abiathar the priest: and they following Adonijah helped him.
text.NABRE: He consulted with Joab, son of Zeruiah, and with Abiathar the priest, and they became Adonijah’s supporters.
text.DRB: And he had talk with Joab the son of Sarvia, and with Abiathar the priest, who furthered Adonias' side.

text.NRSV: But the priest Zadok, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and the prophet Nathan, and Shimei, and Rei, and David’s own warriors did not side with Adonijah.
text.Rotherham: But, Zadok the priest, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and Nathan the prophet, and Shimei, and Rei, and the mighty men who belonged to David, were not with Adonijah.
text.KJV: But Zadok the priest, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and Nathan the prophet, and Shimei, and Rei, and the mighty men which belonged to David, were not with Adonijah.
text.NABRE: However, Zadok the priest, Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, Nathan the prophet, Shimei and Rei, and David’s warriors did not support Adonijah.
text.DRB: But Sadoc the Priest, and Banaias the son of Joiada, and Nathan the Prophet, and Semei, and Rei, and the strength of David's army was not with Adonias.

text.NRSV: Adonijah sacrificed sheep, oxen, and fatted cattle by the stone Zoheleth, which is beside En-rogel, and he invited all his brothers, the king’s sons, and all the royal officials of Judah,
text.Rotherham: And Adonijah sacrificed sheep and oxen and heifers, near the stone of Zoheleth, which is beside En-rogel,—and invited all his brethren, sons of the king, and all the men of Judah, servants of the king;
text.KJV: And Adonijah slew sheep and oxen and fat cattle by the stone of Zoheleth, which is by En–rogel, and called all his brethren the king’s sons, and all the men of Judah the king’s servants:
text.NABRE: Adonijah slaughtered sheep, oxen, and fatlings at the stone Zoheleth near En-rogel and invited all his brothers, the king’s sons, and all the royal officials of Judah;
text.DRB: Adonias therefore having immolated rams and calves, and all fat beasts beside the Stone zoheleth, which was nigh to the Fountain Rogel, called all his brethren the sons of the king, and all the men of Juda the servants of the king:

text.NRSV: but he did not invite the prophet Nathan or Benaiah or the warriors or his brother Solomon.
text.Rotherham: but, Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah, and the mighty men, and Solomon his brother, he invited not.
text.KJV: But Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah, and the mighty men, and Solomon his brother, he called not.
text.NABRE: but he did not invite Nathan the prophet, or Benaiah, or the warriors, or Solomon his brother.
text.DRB: But Nathan the Prophet, and Banaias, and all the strong ones, and Salomon his brother he called not.

text.NRSV: Then Nathan said to Bathsheba, Solomon’s mother, “Have you not heard that Adonijah son of Haggith has become king and our lord David does not know it?
text.Rotherham: Then spake Nathan unto Bath-sheba mother of Solomon, saying—Hast thou not heard that Adonijah son of Haggith hath become king, and, our lord David, knoweth it not?
text.KJV: Wherefore Nathan spake unto Bath–sheba the mother of Solomon, saying, Hast thou not heard that Adonijah the son of Haggith doth reign, and David our lord knoweth it not?
text.NABRE: Solomon Proclaimed King. Then Nathan said to Bathsheba, Solomon’s mother: “Have you not heard that Adonijah, son of Haggith, has become king, and our lord David does not know?
text.DRB: Nathan therefore said to Bethsabee the mother of Salomon: Hast thou not heard that Adonias the son of Haggith hath reigned, & our lord David is ignorant thereof?

text.NRSV: Now therefore come, let me give you advice, so that you may save your own life and the life of your son Solomon.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, come; let me give thee counsel, I pray thee,—so shalt thou rescue thine own life, and the life of thy son, Solomon.
text.KJV: Now therefore come, let me, I pray thee, give thee counsel, that thou mayest save thine own life, and the life of thy son Solomon.
text.NABRE: Come now, let me advise you so that you may save your life and the life of your son Solomon.
text.DRB: Now therefore come, take counsel of me, & save thy life, & thy son Salomon's.

text.NRSV: Go in at once to King David, and say to him, ‘Did you not, my lord the king, swear to your servant, saying: Your son Solomon shall succeed me as king, and he shall sit on my throne? Why then is Adonijah king?’
text.Rotherham: Go, and get thee in unto King David, and thou shalt say unto him—Didst not, thou thyself, my lord O king, swear unto thy handmaid, saying—Solomon thy son, shall become king after me, yea, he, shall sit upon my throne? Why, then, hath, Adonijah, become king?
text.KJV: Go and get thee in unto king David, and say unto him, Didst not thou, my lord, O king, swear unto thine handmaid, saying, Assuredly Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne? why then doth Adonijah reign?
text.NABRE: Go, visit King David, and say to him, ‘Did you not, my lord king, swear to your handmaid: Your son Solomon shall be king after me; it is he who shall sit upon my throne? Why, then, has Adonijah become king?’
text.DRB: Go, & enter in to king David, & say to him: Didst not thou my lord king swear to me thy handmaid, saying: Salomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit in my throne? Why then reigneth Adonias?

text.NRSV: Before you are finished speaking to the king, I will come in after you and confirm your words.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! while thou art yet speaking there with the king, I also, will come in after thee, and confirm thy words.
text.KJV: Behold, while thou yet talkest there with the king, I also will come in after thee, and confirm thy words.
text.NABRE: And while you are still there speaking to the king, I will come in after you and confirm your words.”
text.DRB: And whiles thou art yet speaking there with the king, I will come after thee, & make up thy words.

text.NRSV: So Bathsheba went to the king in his room. The king was very old; Abishag the Shunammite was attending the king.
text.Rotherham: So Bath-sheba went in unto the king, into the chamber, now, the king, was very old,—and Abishag the Shunammite, was ministering unto the king.
text.KJV: And Bath–sheba went in unto the king into the chamber: and the king was very old; and Abishag the Shunammite ministered unto the king.
text.NABRE: So Bathsheba visited the king in his room. The king was very old, and Abishag the Shunamite was caring for the king.
text.DRB: Bethsabee therefore went in to the king in the chamber: and the king was exceeding old, and Abisag the Sunamite ministered to him.

text.NRSV: Bathsheba bowed and did obeisance to the king, and the king said, “What do you wish?”
text.Rotherham: And Bath-sheba bowed, and did homage unto the king,—and the king said—What aileth thee?
text.KJV: And Bath–sheba bowed, and did obeisance unto the king. And the king said, What wouldest thou?
text.NABRE: Bathsheba bowed in homage to the king. The king said to her, “What do you wish?”
text.DRB: Bethsabee bowed herself, and adored the king. To whom the king said: What is thy will? Quoth he.

text.NRSV: She said to him, “My lord, you swore to your servant by the Lord your God, saying: Your son Solomon shall succeed me as king, and he shall sit on my throne.
text.Rotherham: And she said unto him—My lord, thou thyself, didst swear by Yahweh thy God, unto thy handmaid, Assuredly, Solomon thy son, shall become king after me,—yea, he, shall sit upon my throne.
text.KJV: And she said unto him, My lord, thou swarest by the Lord thy God unto thine handmaid, saying, Assuredly Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne.
text.NABRE: She answered him: “My lord, you swore to your servant by the Lord, your God, ‘Solomon your son will be king after me; it is he who shall sit upon my throne.’
text.DRB: Who answering said: My lord, thou didst swear to thy handmaid by our Lord thy God, that Salomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit in my throne.

text.NRSV: But now suddenly Adonijah has become king, though you, my lord the king, do not know it.
text.Rotherham: But, now, lo! Adonijah, hath become king; now, therefore, my lord, O king, thou knowest it not:
text.KJV: And now, behold, Adonijah reigneth; and now, my lord the king, thou knowest it not:
text.NABRE: But now Adonijah has become king, and you, my lord king, do not know it.
text.DRB: And behold now Adonias reigneth, thou my lord the king not knowing thereof.

text.NRSV: He has sacrificed oxen, fatted cattle, and sheep in abundance, and has invited all the children of the king, the priest Abiathar, and Joab the commander of the army; but your servant Solomon he has not invited.
text.Rotherham: and he hath sacrificed oxen and heifers and sheep, in abundance, and hath invited all the sons of the king, and Abiathar the priest, and Joab general of the army,—but, Solomon thy servant, hath he not invited.
text.KJV: And he hath slain oxen and fat cattle and sheep in abundance, and hath called all the sons of the king, and Abiathar the priest, and Joab the captain of the host: but Solomon thy servant hath he not called.
text.NABRE: He has sacrificed bulls, fatlings, and sheep in great numbers; he has invited all the king’s sons, Abiathar the priest, and Joab, the commander of the army, but not your servant Solomon.
text.DRB: He hath killed oxen, and all fat things, and many rams, and called all the king's sons. Abiathar also the Priest, and Joab the General of the warfare: but Salomon thy servant he called not.

text.NRSV: But you, my lord the king—the eyes of all Israel are on you to tell them who shall sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, my lord, O king, the eyes of all Israel, are upon thee,—to tell them, who shall sit upon the throne of my lord the king, after him.
text.KJV: And thou, my lord, O king, the eyes of all Israel are upon thee, that thou shouldest tell them who shall sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.
text.NABRE: Now, my lord king, all Israel is looking to you to declare to them who is to sit upon the throne of my lord the king after him.
text.DRB: Notwithstanding my lord king, the eyes of all Israel look upon thee, that thou wouldest shew them, who shall sit in thy throne my lord king after thee.

text.NRSV: Otherwise it will come to pass, when my lord the king sleeps with his ancestors, that my son Solomon and I will be counted offenders.”
text.Rotherham: Otherwise it shall come to pass, when my lord the king shall sleep with his fathers, then shall I and my son Solomon be counted, offenders.
text.KJV: Otherwise it shall come to pass, when my lord the king shall sleep with his fathers, that I and my son Solomon shall be counted offenders.
text.NABRE: If this is not done, when my lord the king rests with his ancestors, I and my son Solomon will be considered criminals.”
text.DRB: And it shall be when my lord king sleepeth with his fathers, I & my son Salomon shall be sinners.

text.NRSV: While she was still speaking with the king, the prophet Nathan came in.
text.Rotherham: And lo! while yet she was speaking with the king, Nathan the prophet, came in.
text.KJV: And, lo, while she yet talked with the king, Nathan the prophet also came in.
text.NABRE: While she was still speaking to the king, Nathan the prophet came in.
text.DRB: As she was yet speaking with the king, Nathan the Prophet came.

text.NRSV: The king was told, “Here is the prophet Nathan.” When he came in before the king, he did obeisance to the king, with his face to the ground.
text.Rotherham: So they told the king, saying, Lo! Nathan the prophet! And, when he had come in before the king, he bowed himself down to the king with his face to the ground.
text.KJV: And they told the king, saying, Behold Nathan the prophet. And when he was come in before the king, he bowed himself before the king with his face to the ground.
text.NABRE: They told the king, “Nathan the prophet is here.” He entered the king’s presence and did him homage, bowing to the floor.
text.DRB: And they told the king, saying: Nathan the Prophet is here. And when he was gone in to the king, and had adored bowing to the earth,

text.NRSV: Nathan said, “My lord the king, have you said, ‘Adonijah shall succeed me as king, and he shall sit on my throne’?
text.Rotherham: Then said Nathan—My lord, O king, hast, thou, said, Adonijah, shall become king after me,—yea, he, shall sit upon my throne?
text.KJV: And Nathan said, My lord, O king, hast thou said, Adonijah shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne?
text.NABRE: Then Nathan said: “My lord king, did you say, ‘Adonijah shall be king after me and shall sit upon my throne’?
text.DRB: Nathan said: My lord king, didst thou say: Let Adonias reign after me, and let him sit upon my throne?

text.NRSV: For today he has gone down and has sacrificed oxen, fatted cattle, and sheep in abundance, and has invited all the king’s children, Joab the commander of the army, and the priest Abiathar; and now they are eating and drinking before him, and saying, ‘Long live King Adonijah!’
text.Rotherham: For he hath gone down to-day, and hath sacrificed oxen and heifers and sheep, in abundance, and hath invited all the sons of the king, and the generals of the army, and Abiathar the priest, and, there they are—eating and drinking before him,—and they have said—Long live King Adonijah!
text.KJV: For he is gone down this day, and hath slain oxen and fat cattle and sheep in abundance, and hath called all the king’s sons, and the captains of the host, and Abiathar the priest; and, behold, they eat and drink before him, and say, God save king Adonijah.
text.NABRE: For today he went down and sacrificed bulls, fatlings, and sheep in great numbers; he invited all the king’s sons, the commanders of the army, and Abiathar the priest, and even now they are eating and drinking in his company and saying, ‘Long live King Adonijah!’
text.DRB: Because he is gone down today, and hath immolated oxen, and fatlings, and many rams, and called all the king's sons, and the captains of the army, Abiathar also the Priest: and they eating and drinking before him, and saying: God save the king Adonias:

text.NRSV: But me, your servant, and the priest Zadok, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and your servant Solomon he did not invite.
text.Rotherham: But, me, even me thy servant, and Zadok the priest, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and Solomon thy servant, hath he not invited.
text.KJV: But me, even me thy servant, and Zadok the priest, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and thy servant Solomon, hath he not called.
text.NABRE: But me, your servant, he did not invite; nor Zadok the priest, nor Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, nor your servant Solomon.
text.DRB: me thy servant, and Sadoc the Priest, and Banaias the son of Joiada, and Salomon thy servant he called not.

text.NRSV: Has this thing been brought about by my lord the king and you have not let your servants know who should sit on the throne of my lord the king after him?”
text.Rotherham: If, of my lord the king, this thing had been brought to pass, then wouldest thou not have made known unto thy servant, who should sit upon the throne of my lord the king, after him?
text.KJV: Is this thing done by my lord the king, and thou hast not shewed it unto thy servant, who should sit on the throne of my lord the king after him?
text.NABRE: If this was done by order of my lord the king, you did not tell me, your servant, who is to sit upon the throne of my lord the king after him.”
text.DRB: Is this word proceeding from my lord the king, and hast thou not told me thy servant who should sit upon the throne of my lord the king after him?

text.NRSV: King David answered, “Summon Bathsheba to me.” So she came into the king’s presence, and stood before the king.
text.Rotherham: Then responded King David, and said, Call me Bath-sheba. So she came in before the king, and stood before the king.
text.KJV: Then king David answered and said, Call me Bath–sheba. And she came into the king’s presence, and stood before the king.
text.NABRE: King David answered, “Call Bathsheba here.” When she entered the king’s presence and stood before him,
text.DRB: And king David answered, saying: Call unto me Bethsabee. Who when she was entered in to the king, and stood before him,

text.NRSV: The king swore, saying, “As the Lord lives, who has saved my life from every adversity,
text.Rotherham: And the king sware, and said,—By the life of Yahweh, who hath redeemed my life out of every strait,
text.KJV: And the king sware, and said, As the Lord liveth, that hath redeemed my soul out of all distress,
text.NABRE: the king swore, “As the Lord lives, who has redeemed my life from all distress,
text.DRB: the king sware, and said: Our Lord liveth, which hath delivered my soul from all distress,

text.NRSV: as I swore to you by the Lord, the God of Israel, ‘Your son Solomon shall succeed me as king, and he shall sit on my throne in my place,’ so will I do this day.”
text.Rotherham: even as I sware unto thee by Yahweh, God of Israel, saying, Assuredly, Solomon thy son, shall become king after me, yea, he, shall sit upon my throne, in my stead, even so, will I do this day.
text.KJV: Even as I sware unto thee by the Lord God of Israel, saying, Assuredly Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne in my stead; even so will I certainly do this day.
text.NABRE: this very day I will fulfill the oath I swore to you by the Lord, the God of Israel, ‘Your son Solomon shall be king after me and shall sit upon my throne in my place.’”
text.DRB: that as I sware to thee by our Lord the God of Israel, saying: Salomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne for me, so will I do this day.

text.NRSV: Then Bathsheba bowed with her face to the ground, and did obeisance to the king, and said, “May my lord King David live forever!”
text.Rotherham: Then Bath-sheba bowed with her face to the ground, and did homage unto the king,—and said—Let my lord, King David, live to times age-abiding!
text.KJV: Then Bath–sheba bowed with her face to the earth, and did reverence to the king, and said, Let my lord king David live for ever.
text.NABRE: Bowing to the floor in homage to the king, Bathsheba said, “May my lord, King David, live forever!”
text.DRB: And Bethsabee bowing her countenance unto the earth adored the king, saying: God save my lord for ever.

text.NRSV: King David said, “Summon to me the priest Zadok, the prophet Nathan, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada.” When they came before the king,
text.Rotherham: Then said King David—Call me Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada. And they came in before the king.
text.KJV: And king David said, Call me Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada. And they came before the king.
text.NABRE: Then King David said, “Call Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah, son of Jehoiada.” When they had entered the king’s presence,
text.DRB: King David also said: Call me Sadoc the priest, and Nathan the prophet, & Banaias the son of Joiada. Who when they were entered in before the king,

text.NRSV: the king said to them, “Take with you the servants of your lord, and have my son Solomon ride on my own mule, and bring him down to Gihon.
text.Rotherham: Then said the king unto them—Take ye with you the servants of your lord, and cause Solomon my son to ride upon the mule which pertaineth unto myself,—and bring him down unto Gihon;
text.KJV: The king also said unto them, Take with you the servants of your lord, and cause Solomon my son to ride upon mine own mule, and bring him down to Gihon:
text.NABRE: he said to them: “Take with you the royal officials. Mount my son Solomon upon my own mule and escort him down to Gihon.
text.DRB: he said to them: Take with you the servants of your lord, and set Salomon my son upon my mule: and bring him into Gihon.

text.NRSV: There let the priest Zadok and the prophet Nathan anoint him king over Israel; then blow the trumpet, and say, ‘Long live King Solomon!’
text.Rotherham: then shall Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anoint him there as king over Israel,—and ye shall blow with the horn, and say—Long live King Solomon!
text.KJV: And let Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anoint him there king over Israel: and blow ye with the trumpet, and say, God save king Solomon.
text.NABRE: There Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet shall anoint him king over Israel, and you shall blow the ram’s horn and cry, ‘Long live King Solomon!’
text.DRB: And let Sadoc the priest anoint him there, and Nathan the prophet to be king over Israel: and you shall sound the trumpet, and shall say: God save king Salomon.

text.NRSV: You shall go up after him, and he shall come and sit on my throne; he shall be king in my place; for I have appointed him to be ruler over Israel and over Judah.”
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye come up after him, and he shall enter, and sit upon my throne, yea, he, shall become king in my stead; even, him, have I charged to become leader, over Israel, and over Judah.
text.KJV: Then ye shall come up after him, that he may come and sit upon my throne; for he shall be king in my stead: and I have appointed him to be ruler over Israel and over Judah.
text.NABRE: When you come back up with him, he is to go in and sit upon my throne. It is he that shall be king in my place: him I designate ruler of Israel and of Judah.”
text.DRB: And you shall go up after him, and he shall come, and shall sit upon my throne, and he shall reign for me: and I will ordain him that he be Prince over Israel, and over Juda.

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada answered the king, “Amen! May the Lord, the God of my lord the king, so ordain.
text.Rotherham: Then did Benaiah son of Jehoiada make response unto the king, and say—Amen! so, say Yahweh, God of my lord the king.
text.KJV: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada answered the king, and said, Amen: the Lord God of my lord the king say so too.
text.NABRE: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, answered the king: “So be it! May the Lord, the God of my lord the king, so decree!
text.DRB: And Banaias the son of Joiada answered the king, saying: Amen: so speak our Lord the God of my lord the king.

text.NRSV: As the Lord has been with my lord the king, so may he be with Solomon, and make his throne greater than the throne of my lord King David.”
text.Rotherham: As Yahweh hath been with my lord the king, so, let him he with Solomon,—and make his throne greater than the throne of my lord, King David.
text.KJV: As the Lord hath been with my lord the king, even so be he with Solomon, and make his throne greater than the throne of my lord king David.
text.NABRE: As the Lord has been with my lord the king, so may he be with Solomon, and make his throne even greater than that of my lord, King David!”
text.DRB: As our Lord hath been with my lord the king, so be he with Salomon, and make his throne higher than the throne of my lord king David.

text.NRSV: So the priest Zadok, the prophet Nathan, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and the Pelethites, went down and had Solomon ride on King David’s mule, and led him to Gihon.
text.Rotherham: So Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, with the Cherethites and the Pelethites, went down, and caused Solomon to ride upon the mule of King David,—and they escorted him to Gihon.
text.KJV: So Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites, and the Pelethites, went down, and caused Solomon to ride upon king David’s mule, and brought him to Gihon.
text.NABRE: So Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and Pelethites went down, and mounting Solomon on King David’s mule, escorted him to Gihon.
text.DRB: Sadoc therefore the Priest, and Nathan the Prophet went down, and Banaias the son of Joiada, and Cerethi, and Phelethi: and they set Salomon upon the mule of king David, and brought him into Gihon.

text.NRSV: There the priest Zadok took the horn of oil from the tent and anointed Solomon. Then they blew the trumpet, and all the people said, “Long live King Solomon!”
text.Rotherham: Then took Zadok the priest, the horn of oil out of the Tent, and anointed Solomon,—and they blew with a [ram’s] horn, and all the people said, Long live King Solomon!
text.KJV: And Zadok the priest took an horn of oil out of the tabernacle, and anointed Solomon. And they blew the trumpet; and all the people said, God save king Solomon.
text.NABRE: Then Zadok the priest took the horn of oil from the tent and anointed Solomon. They blew the ram’s horn and all the people shouted, “Long live King Solomon!”
text.DRB: And Sadoc the priest took a horn of oil out of the tabernacle, and anointed Salomon: and they sounded the trumpet, and all the people said: God save king Salomon.

text.NRSV: And all the people went up after him, playing on flutes and rejoicing with great joy, so that the earth quaked at their noise.
text.Rotherham: And all the people came up after him, the people themselves also, playing with flutes, and rejoicing with great joy,—so that the earth rent with the sound of them.
text.KJV: And all the people came up after him, and the people piped with pipes, and rejoiced with great joy, so that the earth rent with the sound of them.
text.NABRE: Then all the people went up after him, playing flutes and rejoicing so much the earth split with their shouting.
text.DRB: And all the multitude went up after him, and the people singing on shalms, and rejoicing with great gladness, and the earth sounded of their cry.

text.NRSV: Adonijah and all the guests who were with him heard it as they finished feasting. When Joab heard the sound of the trumpet, he said, “Why is this city in an uproar?”
text.Rotherham: And Adonijah, and all the guests who were with him, heard it, just as, they, had made an end of eating,—and, when Joab heard the sound of a horn, he said—Wherefore the noise of the city, in tumult?
text.KJV: And Adonijah and all the guests that were with him heard it as they had made an end of eating. And when Joab heard the sound of the trumpet, he said, Wherefore is this noise of the city being in an uproar?
text.NABRE: Adonijah Submits to Solomon. Adonijah and all the guests who were with him heard it, just as they ended their banquet. When Joab heard the sound of the ram’s horn, he asked, “Why this uproar in the city?”
text.DRB: And Adonias heard, and all that were invited of him, and the feast was ended: yea & Joab hearing the voice of the trumpet, said: What meaneth the cry of the city making a tumult?

text.NRSV: While he was still speaking, Jonathan son of the priest Abiathar arrived. Adonijah said, “Come in, for you are a worthy man and surely you bring good news.”
text.Rotherham: While yet he was speaking, lo! Jonathan son of Abiathar the priest, came in,—and Adonijah said—Come in, for, a worthy man, thou art, and, good tidings, dost thou bring.
text.KJV: And while he yet spake, behold, Jonathan the son of Abiathar the priest came: and Adonijah said unto him, Come in; for thou art a valiant man, and bringest good tidings.
text.NABRE: As he was speaking, Jonathan, son of Abiathar the priest, arrived. Adonijah said, “Come, you are a man of worth and must bring good news.”
text.DRB: As he yet spake, came Jonathas the son of Abiathar the Priest: to whom Adonias said: Come in, because thou art a stout man, and bringest good news.

text.NRSV: Jonathan answered Adonijah, “No, for our lord King David has made Solomon king;
text.Rotherham: But Jonathan responded and said to Adonijah,—Of a truth, our lord, King David, hath made, Solomon, king.
text.KJV: And Jonathan answered and said to Adonijah, Verily our lord king David hath made Solomon king.
text.NABRE: Jonathan answered Adonijah, “Hardly! Our lord, King David, has made Solomon king.
text.DRB: And Jonathas answered Adonias: Not so: for our lord king David hath appointed Salomon king,

text.NRSV: the king has sent with him the priest Zadok, the prophet Nathan, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and they had him ride on the king’s mule;
text.Rotherham: And the king hath sent with him—Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, with the Cherethites, and the Pelethites,—and they have caused him to ride upon the mule of the king;
text.KJV: And the king hath sent with him Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites, and the Pelethites, and they have caused him to ride upon the king’s mule:
text.NABRE: The king sent with him Zadok the priest, Nathan the prophet, Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and Pelethites, and they mounted him upon the king’s own mule.
text.DRB: and hath sent with him Sadoc the Priest, and Nathan the Prophet, and Banaias the son of Joiada, and Cerethi, and Phelethi, and they have set him upon the king's mule.

text.NRSV: the priest Zadok and the prophet Nathan have anointed him king at Gihon; and they have gone up from there rejoicing, so that the city is in an uproar. This is the noise that you heard.
text.Rotherham: and Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet have anointed him king, in Gihon, and they went up from thence rejoicing, so that the city rang again,—That, is the noise ye have heard.
text.KJV: And Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet have anointed him king in Gihon: and they are come up from thence rejoicing, so that the city rang again. This is the noise that ye have heard.
text.NABRE: Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anointed him king at Gihon, and they went up from there rejoicing, so that the city is in an uproar. That is the noise you hear.
text.DRB: And Sadoc the Priest, and Nathan the Prophet have anointed him king in Gihon: & they are gone up thence rejoicing, and the city sounded: this is the voice that you heard.

text.NRSV: Solomon now sits on the royal throne.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, Solomon hath taken his seat upon the throne of the kingdom.
text.KJV: And also Solomon sitteth on the throne of the kingdom.
text.NABRE: Moreover, Solomon has taken his seat on the royal throne,
text.DRB: Yea and Salomon sitteth upon the throne of the Kingdom.

text.NRSV: Moreover the king’s servants came to congratulate our lord King David, saying, “May your God make the name of Solomon more famous than yours, and make his throne greater than your throne.” The king bowed in worship on the bed
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, the servants of the king have been in, to bless our lord King David, saying—Thy God make the name of Solomon better than thy name, and make his throne greater than thy throne,—And the king bowed himself upon his bed.
text.KJV: And moreover the king’s servants came to bless our lord king David, saying, God make the name of Solomon better than thy name, and make his throne greater than thy throne. And the king bowed himself upon the bed.
text.NABRE: and moreover the king’s servants have come to pay their respects to our lord, King David, saying, ‘May your God make Solomon’s name more famous than your name, his throne greater than your throne!’ And the king in his bed did homage.
text.DRB: And the king's servants entering in, have blessed our lord king David, saying: God amplify the name of Salomon above thy name, and magnify his throne above thy throne. And the king adored in his bed:

text.NRSV: and went on to pray thus, “Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, who today has granted one of my offspring to sit on my throne and permitted me to witness it.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, thus and thus, hath said the king,—Blessed be Yahweh, God of Israel, who hath given, to-day, one to sit upon my throne, mine own eyes also beholding it.
text.KJV: And also thus said the king, Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, which hath given one to sit on my throne this day, mine eyes even seeing it.
text.NABRE: This is what the king said: ‘Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, who has this day provided one to sit upon my throne, so that I see it with my own eyes.’”
text.DRB: and he hath thus spoken: Blessed be our Lord the God of Israel, who hath given this day one sitting in my throne, mine eyes seeing it.

text.NRSV: Then all the guests of Adonijah got up trembling and went their own ways.
text.Rotherham: Then trembled and rose up, all Adonijah’s guests,—and departed, every man his own way.
text.KJV: And all the guests that were with Adonijah were afraid, and rose up, and went every man his way.
text.NABRE: All the guests of Adonijah got up trembling, and went each their way,
text.DRB: They therefore were terrified, and they all arose, that had been invited of Adonias, and every man went his way.

text.NRSV: Adonijah, fearing Solomon, got up and went to grasp the horns of the altar.
text.Rotherham: Adonijah also, feared because of Solomon,—and rose and went, and caught hold of the horns of the altar.
text.KJV: And Adonijah feared because of Solomon, and arose, and went, and caught hold on the horns of the altar.
text.NABRE: but Adonijah, in fear of Solomon, got up and went to grasp the horns of the altar.
text.DRB: And Adonias fearing Salomon arose, & went, & held the horn of the altar.

text.NRSV: Solomon was informed, “See, Adonijah fears King Solomon; for he has laid hold of the horns of the altar, saying, ‘Let King Solomon swear to me first that he will not kill his servant with the sword.’”
text.Rotherham: And it was told Solomon, saying, Lo! Adonijah, feareth King Solomon,—lo! therefore, he hath laid hold of the horns of the altar, saying, Let King Solomon swear unto me at once that he will not put his servant to death with the sword.
text.KJV: And it was told Solomon, saying, Behold, Adonijah feareth king Solomon: for, lo, he hath caught hold on the horns of the altar, saying, Let king Solomon swear unto me to day that he will not slay his servant with the sword.
text.NABRE: It was reported to Solomon: “Adonijah, in fear of King Solomon, is clinging to the horns of the altar and saying, ‘Let King Solomon first swear that he will not kill me, his servant, with the sword.’”
text.DRB: And they told Salomon, saying: Behold Adonias fearing king Salomon, hath taken hold of the horn of the altar, saying: Let king Salomon swear to me this day, that he will not kill his servant with the sword.

text.NRSV: Solomon replied, “If he proves to be a worthy man, not one of his hairs shall fall to the ground; but if wickedness is found in him, he shall die.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Solomon, If he will be a man of worth, there shall not one hair of him fall to the earth,—but, if mischief be found in him, then shall he die.
text.KJV: And Solomon said, If he will shew himself a worthy man, there shall not an hair of him fall to the earth: but if wickedness shall be found in him, he shall die.
text.NABRE: Solomon answered, “If he proves worthy, not a hair of his shall fall to the ground. But if evil is found in him, he shall die.”
text.DRB: And Salomon said: If he be a good man, there shall not so much as one hair of his fall upon the ground: but if evil shall be found in him, he shall die.

text.NRSV: So King Solomon sent to have him brought down from the altar. He came to do obeisance to King Solomon; and Solomon said to him, “Go home.”
text.Rotherham: So King Solomon sent, and brought him down from the altar, and he came in, and bowed himself down to King Solomon,—and Solomon said unto him—Go to thine own house.
text.KJV: So king Solomon sent, and they brought him down from the altar. And he came and bowed himself to king Solomon: and Solomon said unto him, Go to thine house.
text.NABRE: King Solomon sent to have him brought down from the altar, and he came and paid homage to King Solomon. Solomon then said to him, “Go to your house.”
text.DRB: King Salomon therefore sent, and brought him out from the altar: and going in he adored king Salomon: and Salomon said to him: Go to thy house.

text.NRSV: When David’s time to die drew near, he commanded his son Solomon, saying,
text.Rotherham: And, when the days of David drew near that he must die, he charged Solomon his son, saying:—
text.KJV: Now the days of David drew nigh that he should die; and he charged Solomon his son, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 David’s Last Instructions and Death. When the time of David’s death drew near, he gave these instructions to Solomon his son:
text.DRB: And the days of David approached that he should die, and he commanded his son Salomon, saying:

text.NRSV: “I am about to go the way of all the earth. Be strong, and show yourself a man,
text.Rotherham: I, am going the way of all the earth,—thou must be strong, therefore, and show thyself a man;
text.KJV: I go the way of all the earth: be thou strong therefore, and shew thyself a man;
text.NABRE: “I am going the way of all the earth. Be strong and be a man!
text.DRB: I enter into the way of all flesh: take courage, and play the man.

text.NRSV: and keep the charge of the Lord your God, walking in his ways and keeping his statutes, his commandments, his ordinances, and his testimonies, as it is written in the law of Moses, so that you may prosper in all that you do and wherever you turn.
text.Rotherham: and keep the observances of Yahweh thy God, by walking in his ways, by keeping his statutes, his commandments, and his regulations and his testimonies, as written in the law of Moses,—to the end thou mayest prosper in all that thou doest, and whithersoever thou turnest thyself;
text.KJV: And keep the charge of the Lord thy God, to walk in his ways, to keep his statutes, and his commandments, and his judgments, and his testimonies, as it is written in the law of Moses, that thou mayest prosper in all that thou doest, and whithersoever thou turnest thyself:
text.NABRE: Keep the mandate of the Lord, your God, walking in his ways and keeping his statutes, commands, ordinances, and decrees as they are written in the law of Moses, that you may succeed in whatever you do, and wherever you turn,
text.DRB: And observe the watches of our Lord thy God, that thou walk in his ways, and keep his ceremonies, and his precepts & judgments, and testimonies as is written in the law of Moyses: that thou mayst understand all things which thou doest, and whithersoever thou shalt turn thyself:

text.NRSV: Then the Lord will establish his word that he spoke concerning me: ‘If your heirs take heed to their way, to walk before me in faithfulness with all their heart and with all their soul, there shall not fail you a successor on the throne of Israel.’
text.Rotherham: to the end that Yahweh may establish his word which he spake concerning me, saying, If thy sons will take heed to their way, by walking before me, in truth, with all their heart, and with all their soul, (then, said he) there shall not be cut off to thee a man, from off the throne of Israel.
text.KJV: That the Lord may continue his word which he spake concerning me, saying, If thy children take heed to their way, to walk before me in truth with all their heart and with all their soul, there shall not fail thee (said he) a man on the throne of Israel.
text.NABRE: and that the Lord may fulfill the word he spoke concerning me: If your sons so conduct themselves that they walk before me in faithfulness with their whole heart and soul, there shall never be wanting someone of your line on the throne of Israel.
text.DRB: that our Lord may confirm his words, which he hath spoken of me, saying: If thy children shall keep their ways, and shall walk before me in truth, in all their heart, and in all their soul, there shall not altogether be taken from thee a man out of the throne of Israel.

text.NRSV: “Moreover you know also what Joab son of Zeruiah did to me, how he dealt with the two commanders of the armies of Israel, Abner son of Ner, and Amasa son of Jether, whom he murdered, retaliating in time of peace for blood that had been shed in war, and putting the blood of war on the belt around his waist, and on the sandals on his feet.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, thou, knowest what Joab son of Zeruiah did to me, how he dealt with two generals of the armies of Israel—with Abner son of Ner, and with Amasa son of Jether—both of whom he slew, shedding the blood of war in peace,—and putting the blood of war upon his girdle that was on his loins, and upon his sandals, that were on his feet.
text.KJV: Moreover thou knowest also what Joab the son of Zeruiah did to me, and what he did to the two captains of the hosts of Israel, unto Abner the son of Ner, and unto Amasa the son of Jether, whom he slew, and shed the blood of war in peace, and put the blood of war upon his girdle that was about his loins, and in his shoes that were on his feet.
text.NABRE: “You yourself know what Joab, son of Zeruiah, did to me—what he did to the two commanders of Israel’s armies, Abner, son of Ner, and Amasa, son of Jether: he killed them and brought the blood of war into a time of peace, and put the blood of war on the belt about his waist and the sandal on his foot.
text.DRB: Thou knowest also what Joab the son of Sarvia hath done to me, what he did to the two Princes of the army of Israel, to Abner the son of Ner, & Amasa the son of Jether: whom he slew, & shed the blood of war in peace, and put the blood of battle in his belt, which was about his loins, and in his shoe, which was on his feet.

text.NRSV: Act therefore according to your wisdom, but do not let his gray head go down to Sheol in peace.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, must do according to thy wisdom,—but will not let his grey hair go down in peace, to hades.
text.KJV: Do therefore according to thy wisdom, and let not his hoar head go down to the grave in peace.
text.NABRE: Act with all the wisdom you possess; do not let his gray head go down to Sheol in peace.
text.DRB: Thou shalt do therefore according to thy wisdom, and shalt not bring his hoar head peaceably unto hell.

text.NRSV: Deal loyally, however, with the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite, and let them be among those who eat at your table; for with such loyalty they met me when I fled from your brother Absalom.
text.Rotherham: But, with the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite, thou wilt deal in lovingkindness, and they will be among them who eat at thy table,—for, so, drew they near unto me, when I fled from Absolom thy brother.
text.KJV: But shew kindness unto the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite, and let them be of those that eat at thy table: for so they came to me when I fled because of Absalom thy brother.
text.NABRE: But be true to the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite, and have them among those who eat at your table. For they were loyal to me when I was fleeing from your brother Absalom.
text.DRB: But to the sons also of Berzellai the Galaadite thou shalt render kindness, and they shall eat on thy table: for they met me when I fled from the face of Absalom thy brother.

text.NRSV: There is also with you Shimei son of Gera, the Benjaminite from Bahurim, who cursed me with a terrible curse on the day when I went to Mahanaim; but when he came down to meet me at the Jordan, I swore to him by the Lord, ‘I will not put you to death with the sword.’
text.Rotherham: Lo! also, there is with thee—Shimei son of Gera a Benjamite, of Behurim, well, he, it was who cursed me with a grievous curse; on the day I journeyed to Mahanaim,—but he, came down to meet me, at the Jordan, and so I sware to him by Yahweh, saying—I will not put thee to death, with the sword.
text.KJV: And, behold, thou hast with thee Shimei the son of Gera, a Benjamite of Bahurim, which cursed me with a grievous curse in the day when I went to Mahanaim: but he came down to meet me at Jordan, and I sware to him by the Lord, saying, I will not put thee to death with the sword.
text.NABRE: You also have with you Shimei, son of Gera, the Benjaminite of Bahurim, who cursed me bitterly the day I was going to Mahanaim. When he came down to meet me at the Jordan, I swore to him by the Lord: ‘I will not kill you by the sword.’
text.DRB: Thou shalt also with thee Semei the son of Gera the son of Jemini of Bahurim, who cursed me with a wicked curse, when I went to the Camp; but because he came down to meet me when I passed Jordan, and I sware to him by our Lord, saying: I will not kill thee with the sword;

text.NRSV: Now therefore do not hold him guiltless, for you are a wise man; you will know what you ought to do to him, and you must bring his gray head down with blood to Sheol.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore do not hold him guiltless, for, a wise man, thou art, and wilt know how thou oughtest to deal with him, and wilt suffer his grey hairs to go down with blood, to hades.
text.KJV: Now therefore hold him not guiltless: for thou art a wise man, and knowest what thou oughtest to do unto him; but his hoar head bring thou down to the grave with blood.
text.NABRE: But you must not let him go unpunished. You are wise; you will know what to do to send his gray head down to Sheol in blood.”
text.DRB: do not thou suffer him to be guiltless. But thou art a wise man, so that thou knowest what thou shalt do to him, and thou shalt bring his hoar hair with blood unto hell.

text.NRSV: Then David slept with his ancestors, and was buried in the city of David.
text.Rotherham: So then David slept with his fathers,—and was buried in the city of David.
text.KJV: So David slept with his fathers, and was buried in the city of David.
text.NABRE: David rested with his ancestors and was buried in the City of David.
text.DRB: David therefore slept with his fathers, & was buried in the city of David.

text.NRSV: The time that David reigned over Israel was forty years; he reigned seven years in Hebron, and thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Now, the days that David reigned over Israel, were forty years,—in Hebron, reigned he seven years, and, in Jerusalem, reigned he thirty and three years.
text.KJV: And the days that David reigned over Israel were forty years: seven years reigned he in Hebron, and thirty and three years reigned he in Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: David was king over Israel for forty years: he was king seven years in Hebron and thirty-three years in Jerusalem. The Kingdom Made Secure.
text.DRB: And the days that David reigned in Israel, are forty years: in Hebron he reigned seven years, in Jerusalem thirty three.

text.NRSV: So Solomon sat on the throne of his father David; and his kingdom was firmly established.
text.Rotherham: But, when, Solomon, took his seat upon the throne of David his father, then was the kingdom firmly established.
text.KJV: Then sat Solomon upon the throne of David his father; and his kingdom was established greatly.
text.NABRE: Then Solomon sat on the throne of David his father, and his kingship was established.
text.DRB: And Salomon sat upon the throne of David his father, & his Kingdom was confirmed exceedingly.

text.NRSV: Then Adonijah son of Haggith came to Bathsheba, Solomon’s mother. She asked, “Do you come peaceably?” He said, “Peaceably.”
text.Rotherham: And Adonijah son of Haggith came in unto Bath-sheba, mother of Solomon: And she said—Peaceably, comest thou? And he said—Peaceably.
text.KJV: And Adonijah the son of Haggith came to Bath–sheba the mother of Solomon. And she said, Comest thou peaceably? And he said, Peaceably.
text.NABRE: Adonijah, son of Haggith, came to Bathsheba, the mother of Solomon. “Do you come in peace?” she asked. “In peace,” he answered,
text.DRB: And Adonias the son of Haggith entered in to Bethsabee the mother of Salomon. Who said to him: Is thy coming peaceable? Who answered: Peaceable.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “I have a word to say to you.” She said, “Go on.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he—I have somewhat to say unto thee. And she said—Say on.
text.KJV: He said moreover, I have somewhat to say unto thee. And she said, Say on.
text.NABRE: and he added, “I have something to say to you.” She replied, “Speak.”
text.DRB: And he added: I have a word to speak with thee. To whom she said: Speak. And he:

text.NRSV: He said, “You know that the kingdom was mine, and that all Israel expected me to reign; however, the kingdom has turned about and become my brother’s, for it was his from the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And he said—Thou, knowest that, mine, was the kingdom, and, on me, had all Israel set their faces, that I should become king,—howbeit the kingdom, hath turned about, and become my brother’s, for, from Yahweh, became it, his.
text.KJV: And he said, Thou knowest that the kingdom was mine, and that all Israel set their faces on me, that I should reign: howbeit the kingdom is turned about, and is become my brother’s: for it was his from the Lord.
text.NABRE: So he said: “You know that the kingship was mine, and all Israel expected me to be king. But the kingship passed me by and went to my brother; by the Lord ’s will it went to him.
text.DRB: Thou knowest, quoth he, that the kingdom was mine, & all Israel had purposed to make me over them to be their king: but the kingdom is transposed, & is made my brother's: for it was appointed him of our Lord.

text.NRSV: And now I have one request to make of you; do not refuse me.” She said to him, “Go on.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, one request, have I to ask of thee, do not turn away my face.
text.KJV: And now I ask one petition of thee, deny me not. And she said unto him, Say on.
text.NABRE: But now there is one favor I would ask of you. Do not refuse me.” And she said, “Speak on.”
text.DRB: Now therefore I desire one petition of thee; Confound not my face. Who said to him: Speak.

text.NRSV: He said, “Please ask King Solomon—he will not refuse you—to give me Abishag the Shunammite as my wife.”
text.Rotherham: And she said unto him—Say on. And he said—Speak, I pray thee, unto Solomon the king, for he will not turn away thy face,—that he give me Abishag the Shunammite, to wife.
text.KJV: And he said, Speak, I pray thee, unto Solomon the king, (for he will not say thee nay,) that he give me Abishag the Shunammite to wife.
text.NABRE: He said, “Please ask King Solomon, who will not refuse you, to give me Abishag the Shunamite to be my wife.”
text.DRB: And he said: I pray speak to Salomon the king (for he cannot deny thee any thing) that he give me Abisag the Sunamite to wife.

text.NRSV: Bathsheba said, “Very well; I will speak to the king on your behalf.”
text.Rotherham: And Bath-sheba said: Good! I myself, will speak for thee, unto the king.
text.KJV: And Bath–sheba said, Well; I will speak for thee unto the king.
text.NABRE: Bathsheba replied, “Very well, I will speak to the king for you.”
text.DRB: And Bethsabee said: Well, I will speak for thee to the king.

text.NRSV: So Bathsheba went to King Solomon, to speak to him on behalf of Adonijah. The king rose to meet her, and bowed down to her; then he sat on his throne, and had a throne brought for the king’s mother, and she sat on his right.
text.Rotherham: So Bath-sheba went in unto King Solomon, to speak to him for Adonijah,—and the king rose up to meet her, and bowed himself down to her, and sat down upon his throne, and caused a throne to be set for the mother of the king, and she sat on his right hand.
text.KJV: Bath–sheba therefore went unto king Solomon, to speak unto him for Adonijah. And the king rose up to meet her, and bowed himself unto her, and sat down on his throne, and caused a seat to be set for the king’s mother; and she sat on his right hand.
text.NABRE: Then Bathsheba went to King Solomon to speak to him for Adonijah, and the king stood up to meet her and paid her homage. Then he sat down upon his throne, and a throne was provided for the king’s mother, who sat at his right.
text.DRB: Bethsabee therefore came to king Salomon, to speak unto him for Adonias: & the king arose to meet her, & adored her, & sat down upon his throne: & a throne was set for the king's mother, who sat on his right hand.

text.NRSV: Then she said, “I have one small request to make of you; do not refuse me.” And the king said to her, “Make your request, my mother; for I will not refuse you.”
text.Rotherham: Then said she—One small request, am I asking of thee, do not turn away my face. And the king said to her—Ask on, my mother, for I will not turn away thy face.
text.KJV: Then she said, I desire one small petition of thee; I pray thee, say me not nay. And the king said unto her, Ask on, my mother: for I will not say thee nay.
text.NABRE: She said, “There is one small favor I would ask of you. Do not refuse me.” The king said to her, “Ask it, my mother, for I will not refuse you.”
text.DRB: And she said to him: One little petition I desire of thee, confound not my face. And the king said to her: My mother ask: for it behoveth not that I turn away thy face.

text.NRSV: She said, “Let Abishag the Shunammite be given to your brother Adonijah as his wife.”
text.Rotherham: And she said, Let Abishag the Shunammite be given to Adonijah thy brother, to wife.
text.KJV: And she said, Let Abishag the Shunammite be given to Adonijah thy brother to wife.
text.NABRE: So she said, “Let Abishag the Shunamite be given to your brother Adonijah to be his wife.”
text.DRB: Who said: Let Abisag the Sunamite be given to Adonias thy brother to wife.

text.NRSV: King Solomon answered his mother, “And why do you ask Abishag the Shunammite for Adonijah? Ask for him the kingdom as well! For he is my elder brother; ask not only for him but also for the priest Abiathar and for Joab son of Zeruiah!”
text.Rotherham: Then answered King Solomon, and said to his mother—Wherefore, then, art thou asking Abishag the Shunammite, for Adonijah? ask, then, for him the kingdom, because he is mine elder brother,—even for him, and for Abiathar the priest, and for Joab, son of Zeruiah.
text.KJV: And king Solomon answered and said unto his mother, And why dost thou ask Abishag the Shunammite for Adonijah? ask for him the kingdom also; for he is mine elder brother; even for him, and for Abiathar the priest, and for Joab the son of Zeruiah.
text.NABRE: King Solomon answered his mother, “And why do you ask that Abishag the Shunamite be given to Adonijah? Ask the kingship for him as well, for he is my older brother! Ask for him, for Abiathar the priest, for Joab, son of Zeruiah!”
text.DRB: And king Salomon answered, and said to his mother: Why doest thou ask Abisag the Sunamite for Adonias? Ask for him also the Kingdom: for he is my brother elder than I, & hath Abiathar the priest, & Joab the son of Sarvia.

text.NRSV: Then King Solomon swore by the Lord, “So may God do to me, and more also, for Adonijah has devised this scheme at the cost of his life!
text.Rotherham: Then swore King Solomon by Yahweh, saying,—So, let God do to me, and, so, let him add, if, at the cost of his life, Adonijah hath not spoken this word.
text.KJV: Then king Solomon sware by the Lord, saying, God do so to me, and more also, if Adonijah have not spoken this word against his own life.
text.NABRE: And King Solomon swore by the Lord: “May God do thus to me and more, if Adonijah has not spoken this word at the cost of his life.
text.DRB: Therefore king Salomon sware by our Lord, saying: These things do God to me, & these add he, because Adonias hath spoken this word against his life.

text.NRSV: Now therefore as the Lord lives, who has established me and placed me on the throne of my father David, and who has made me a house as he promised, today Adonijah shall be put to death.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, by the life of Yahweh, who hath established me, and seated me upon the throne of David my father, and who hath made for me a household, as he had spoken, surely, to-day, shall Adonijah be put to death!
text.KJV: Now therefore, as the Lord liveth, which hath established me, and set me on the throne of David my father, and who hath made me an house, as he promised, Adonijah shall be put to death this day.
text.NABRE: And now, as the Lord lives, who has established me and set me on the throne of David my father and made for me a house as he promised, this day shall Adonijah be put to death.”
text.DRB: And now, our Lord liveth which hath established me, & placed me upon the throne of David my father, and which hath made me a house, as he spake, this day shall Adonias be slain.

text.NRSV: So King Solomon sent Benaiah son of Jehoiada; he struck him down, and he died.
text.Rotherham: So King Solomon sent by the hand of Benaiah son of Jehoiada,—and he fell upon him, that he died.
text.KJV: And king Solomon sent by the hand of Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; and he fell upon him that he died.
text.NABRE: Then King Solomon sent Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, who struck him dead.
text.DRB: And king Salomon sent by the hand of Banaias the son of Joiada, who slew him, & he died.

text.NRSV: The king said to the priest Abiathar, “Go to Anathoth, to your estate; for you deserve death. But I will not at this time put you to death, because you carried the ark of the Lord God before my father David, and because you shared in all the hardships my father endured.”
text.Rotherham: Also, unto Abiathar the priest, said the king—To Anathoth, get thee unto thine own fields, for, death-doomed, thou art,—but, this day, will I not put thee to death, because thou didst bear the ark of My Lord, Yahweh, before David my father, and because thou wast afflicted in all wherein my father was afflicted.
text.KJV: And unto Abiathar the priest said the king, Get thee to Anathoth, unto thine own fields; for thou art worthy of death: but I will not at this time put thee to death, because thou barest the ark of the Lord God before David my father, and because thou hast been afflicted in all wherein my father was afflicted.
text.NABRE: The king said to Abiathar the priest: “Go to your estate in Anathoth. Though you deserve to die, I will not put you to death at this time, because you carried the ark of the Lord God before David my father and shared in all the hardships my father endured.”
text.DRB: To Abiathar also the priest the king said: Go into Anathoth to thy field, and thou indeed art a man of death: but today I will not kill thee, because thou didst carry the ark of our Lord God before David my father, & hast sustained labour in all things, wherein my father laboured.

text.NRSV: So Solomon dismissed Abiathar from being priest to the Lord, thus fulfilling the word of the Lord that he had spoken concerning the house of Eli in Shiloh.
text.Rotherham: So Solomon thrust out Abiathar from being priest unto Yahweh,—to fulfil the word of Yahweh which he spake concerning the household of Eli, in Shiloh.
text.KJV: So Solomon thrust out Abiathar from being priest unto the Lord; that he might fulfil the word of the Lord, which he spake concerning the house of Eli in Shiloh.
text.NABRE: So Solomon dismissed Abiathar from the office of priest of the Lord, thus fulfilling the word the Lord had spoken in Shiloh against the house of Eli.
text.DRB: Salomon therefore cast out Abiathar, that he should not be the priest of our Lord, that the word of our Lord might be fulfilled, which he spake concerning the house of Heli in Silo.

text.NRSV: When the news came to Joab—for Joab had supported Adonijah though he had not supported Absalom—Joab fled to the tent of the Lord and grasped the horns of the altar.
text.Rotherham: And, the report, came unto Joab, for, Joab, had inclined after Adonijah, although, after Solomon, he had not inclined,—so then Joab fled into the Tent of Yahweh, and laid hold of the horns of the altar.
text.KJV: Then tidings came to Joab: for Joab had turned after Adonijah, though he turned not after Absalom. And Joab fled unto the tabernacle of the Lord, and caught hold on the horns of the altar.
text.NABRE: When the news came to Joab, since he had sided with Adonijah, though not with Absalom, he fled to the tent of the Lord and clung to the horns of the altar.
text.DRB: And then a messenger came to Joab, for that Joab had turned after Adonias, and after Salomon had not turned: Joab therefore fled into the tabernacle of our Lord, & caught the horn of the altar.

text.NRSV: When it was told King Solomon, “Joab has fled to the tent of the Lord and now is beside the altar,” Solomon sent Benaiah son of Jehoiada, saying, “Go, strike him down.”
text.Rotherham: And it was told King Solomon—Joab hath fled into the Tent of Yahweh, and, there he is, beside the altar. So Solomon sent Benaiah son of Jehoiada, saying—Go fall upon him!
text.KJV: And it was told king Solomon that Joab was fled unto the tabernacle of the Lord; and, behold, he is by the altar. Then Solomon sent Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, saying, Go, fall upon him.
text.NABRE: King Solomon was told, “Joab has fled to the tent of the Lord and is by the altar.” He sent Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, with the order, “Go, strike him down.”
text.DRB: And it was told king Salomon, that Joab was fled into the tabernacle of our Lord, and was beside the altar: and Salomon sent Banaias the son of Joiada, saying: Go, kill him

text.NRSV: So Benaiah came to the tent of the Lord and said to him, “The king commands, ‘Come out.’” But he said, “No, I will die here.” Then Benaiah brought the king word again, saying, “Thus said Joab, and thus he answered me.”
text.Rotherham: And Benaiah came into the Tent of Yahweh, and said unto him—Thus, saith the king, Come forth! And he said—Nay! but, here, will I die! So Benaiah brought the king word again, saying, Thus, spake Joab, and, thus, he answered me.
text.KJV: And Benaiah came to the tabernacle of the Lord, and said unto him, Thus saith the king, Come forth. And he said, Nay; but I will die here. And Benaiah brought the king word again, saying, Thus said Joab, and thus he answered me.
text.NABRE: Benaiah went to the tent of the Lord and said to him, “The king says, ‘Come out.’” But he answered, “No! I will die here.” Benaiah reported to the king, “This is what Joab said to me in reply.”
text.DRB: And Banaias came to the tabernacle of our Lord, and said to him: Thus saith the king: Come forth. Who said: I will not come forth, but here will I die. Banaias reported his word to the king, saying: Thus spake Joab, and thus he answered me.

text.NRSV: The king replied to him, “Do as he has said, strike him down and bury him; and so take away from me and from my father’s house the guilt for the blood that Joab shed without cause.
text.Rotherham: And the king said to him—Do as he hath spoken, fall, then, upon him, and bury him,—and so put away the innocent blood, which Joab shed, from off me, and from off the house of my father;
text.KJV: And the king said unto him, Do as he hath said, and fall upon him, and bury him; that thou mayest take away the innocent blood, which Joab shed, from me, and from the house of my father.
text.NABRE: The king answered him: “Do as he has said. Strike him down and bury him, and remove from me and from my father’s house the blood which Joab shed without provocation.
text.DRB: And the king said to him: Do as he hath spoken: and kill him, and bury him, and thou shalt remove the innocent blood, which hath been shed of Joab, from me, and from the house of my father.

text.NRSV: The Lord will bring back his bloody deeds on his own head, because, without the knowledge of my father David, he struck down with the sword two men more righteous and better than himself, Abner son of Ner, commander of the army of Israel, and Amasa son of Jether, commander of the army of Judah.
text.Rotherham: thus will Yahweh bring back his blood upon his own head, in that he fell upon two men more righteous and better than he, and slew them with the sword, my father David not knowing it,—even Abner son of Ner, general of the army of Israel, and Amasa son of Jether, general of the army of Judah;
text.KJV: And the Lord shall return his blood upon his own head, who fell upon two men more righteous and better than he, and slew them with the sword, my father David not knowing thereof, to wit, Abner the son of Ner, captain of the host of Israel, and Amasa the son of Jether, captain of the host of Judah.
text.NABRE: The Lord will bring blood upon his own head, because he struck down two men better and more just than himself, and slew them with the sword without my father David’s knowledge: Abner, son of Ner, commander of Israel’s army, and Amasa, son of Jether, commander of Judah’s army.
text.DRB: And our Lord shall render his blood upon his head, because he murdered two just men, and better than himself: and slew them with his sword, my father David not knowing, Abner the son of Ner general of the warfare of Israel, and Amasa the son of Jether general of the army of Juda:

text.NRSV: Their blood shall come back on the head of Joab and on the head of his descendants forever; but to David, and to his descendants, and to his house, and to his throne, there shall be peace from the Lord forevermore.”
text.Rotherham: thus shall their blood come back upon the head of Joab, and upon the head of his seed unto times age-abiding,—but, David and his seed, and his house, and his throne, shall have peace, unto times age-abiding, from Yahweh.
text.KJV: Their blood shall therefore return upon the head of Joab, and upon the head of his seed for ever: but upon David, and upon his seed, and upon his house, and upon his throne, shall there be peace for ever from the Lord.
text.NABRE: Their blood will be upon the head of Joab and his descendants. But upon David and his descendants, upon his house and his throne, there shall be peace forever from the Lord.”
text.DRB: and their blood shall return upon the head of Joab, and upon the head of his seed for ever. But to David & his seed & his house, & to his throne be peace for ever from our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Benaiah son of Jehoiada went up and struck him down and killed him; and he was buried at his own house in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: So Benaiah son of Jehoiada went up, and fell upon him, and put him to death,—and he was buried in his own house, in the wilderness.
text.KJV: So Benaiah the son of Jehoiada went up, and fell upon him, and slew him: and he was buried in his own house in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, went back, struck him down and killed him; he was buried in his house in the wilderness.
text.DRB: Banaias therefore the son of Joiada went up, and setting upon him slew him: and he was buried in his house in the desert.

text.NRSV: The king put Benaiah son of Jehoiada over the army in his place, and the king put the priest Zadok in the place of Abiathar.
text.Rotherham: And the king put Benaiah son of Jehoiada, in his stead, over the army,—and, Zadok the priest, did the king put in the stead of Abiathar.
text.KJV: And the king put Benaiah the son of Jehoiada in his room over the host: and Zadok the priest did the king put in the room of Abiathar.
text.NABRE: The king appointed Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, over the army in his place; Zadok the priest the king put in place of Abiathar.
text.DRB: And the king appointed Banaias the son of Joiada for him over the army, and Sadoc the priest he placed for Abiathar.

text.NRSV: Then the king sent and summoned Shimei, and said to him, “Build yourself a house in Jerusalem, and live there, and do not go out from there to any place whatever.
text.Rotherham: And the king sent, and called for Shimei, and said to him—Build thee a house, in Jerusalem, so shalt thou dwell there,—and shalt not go forth from thence, hither or thither;
text.KJV: And the king sent and called for Shimei, and said unto him, Build thee an house in Jerusalem, and dwell there, and go not forth thence any whither.
text.NABRE: Then the king summoned Shimei and said to him: “Build yourself a house in Jerusalem and stay there. Do not go anywhere else.
text.DRB: The king also sent, and called Semei, and said to him: Build thee a house in Jerusalem, and dwell there: and thou shalt not go out thence hither and thither.

text.NRSV: For on the day you go out, and cross the Wadi Kidron, know for certain that you shall die; your blood shall be on your own head.”
text.Rotherham: but it shall be that, on the day thou goest forth, and passest over the Kidron ravine, know, that thou shalt, die,—thy blood, shall be, upon thine own head.
text.KJV: For it shall be, that on the day thou goest out, and passest over the brook Kidron, thou shalt know for certain that thou shalt surely die: thy blood shall be upon thine own head.
text.NABRE: For the day you leave, and cross the Wadi Kidron, be certain you shall surely die. Your blood shall be upon your own head.”
text.DRB: But what day soever thou shalt go out, and shalt pass the Torrent Cedron, know that thou art to be slain: thy blood shall be upon thy head.

text.NRSV: Shimei said to the king, “The sentence is fair; as my lord the king has said, so will your servant do.” So Shimei lived in Jerusalem many days.
text.Rotherham: And Shimei said to the king—Good, is the word, as my lord the king hath spoken, so, will thy servant do. And Shimei dwelt in Jerusalem many days.
text.KJV: And Shimei said unto the king, The saying is good: as my lord the king hath said, so will thy servant do. And Shimei dwelt in Jerusalem many days.
text.NABRE: Shimei answered the king: “I accept. Your servant will do just as my lord the king has said.” So Shimei stayed in Jerusalem for a long time.
text.DRB: And Semei said to the king: The saying is good: as my lord the king hath spoken, so will thy servant do. Semei therefore dwelt in Jerusalem many days.

text.NRSV: But it happened at the end of three years that two of Shimei’s slaves ran away to King Achish son of Maacah of Gath. When it was told Shimei, “Your slaves are in Gath,”
text.Rotherham: But it came to pass, at the end of three years, that two of the servants of Shimei fled unto Achish son of Maachah king of Gath,—and they told Shimei, saying—Lo! thy servants are in Gath.
text.KJV: And it came to pass at the end of three years, that two of the servants of Shimei ran away unto Achish son of Maachah king of Gath. And they told Shimei, saying, Behold, thy servants be in Gath.
text.NABRE: But three years later, two of Shimei’s servants ran away to Achish, son of Maacah, king of Gath, and Shimei was told, “Your servants are in Gath.”
text.DRB: And it came to pass after three years, that the servants of Semei fled to Achis the son of Maacha the king of Geth: and it was told Semei that his servants were gone into Geth.

text.NRSV: Shimei arose and saddled a donkey, and went to Gath in search of the slaves, to Achish; Shimei went and brought the slaves from Gath.
text.Rotherham: So Shimei arose, and saddled his ass, and went to Gath, unto Achish, to seek his servants—and Shimei went and brought in his servants, from Gath.
text.KJV: And Shimei arose, and saddled his ass, and went to Gath to Achish to seek his servants: and Shimei went, and brought his servants from Gath.
text.NABRE: So Shimei rose, saddled his donkey, and went to Achish in Gath in search of his servants; and Shimei returned from Gath with his servants.
text.DRB: And Semei arose, and saddled his ass, & went to Achis into Geth to require his servants, & he brought them out of Geth.

text.NRSV: When Solomon was told that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath and returned,
text.Rotherham: And it was told Solomon—Shimei hath been out of Jerusalem to Gath, and returned.
text.KJV: And it was told Solomon that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath, and was come again.
text.NABRE: When Solomon was told that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath, and had returned,
text.DRB: And it was told Salomon that Semei went into Geth out of Jerusalem, & was returned.

text.NRSV: the king sent and summoned Shimei, and said to him, “Did I not make you swear by the Lord, and solemnly adjure you, saying, ‘Know for certain that on the day you go out and go anywhere whatever, you shall die’? And you said to me, ‘The sentence is fair; I accept it.’
text.Rotherham: Then sent the king and called for Shimei, and said unto him—Did I not put thee on oath by Yahweh, and adjure thee, saying—On the day thou goest forth and takest thy journey hither or thither, know, that thou shalt, die. Then saidst thou unto me—Good, is the word I have heard.
text.KJV: And the king sent and called for Shimei, and said unto him, Did I not make thee to swear by the Lord, and protested unto thee, saying, Know for a certain, on the day thou goest out, and walkest abroad any whither, that thou shalt surely die? and thou saidst unto me, The word that I have heard is good.
text.NABRE: the king summoned Shimei and said to him: “Did I not have you swear by the Lord and warn you clearly, ‘The day you leave and go anywhere else, be certain you shall surely die’? And you answered, ‘I accept and obey.’
text.DRB: And sending he called him, & said to him: Did I not testify to thee by our Lord, & told thee before: What day soever thou going out shalt pass hither and thither, know that thou shalt die? And thou didst answer me: The saying is good, which I have heard.

text.NRSV: Why then have you not kept your oath to the Lord and the commandment with which I charged you?”
text.Rotherham: Why then, hast thou not kept the oath of Yahweh,—and the charge which I laid upon thee?
text.KJV: Why then hast thou not kept the oath of the Lord, and the commandment that I have charged thee with?
text.NABRE: Why, then, have you not kept the oath of the Lord and the command that I gave you?”
text.DRB: Why then hast thou not kept the oath of our Lord, and the precept that I commanded thee?

text.NRSV: The king also said to Shimei, “You know in your own heart all the mischief that you did to my father David; so the Lord will bring back your mischief on your own head.
text.Rotherham: Then said the king unto Shimei—Thou, knowest all the wickedness which thy heart is privy to, which thou didst unto David my father,—therefore shall Yahweh bring back thy wickedness, upon thine own head.
text.KJV: The king said moreover to Shimei, Thou knowest all the wickedness which thine heart is privy to, that thou didst to David my father: therefore the Lord shall return thy wickedness upon thine own head;
text.NABRE: And the king said to Shimei: “In your heart you know very well the evil that you did to David my father. Now the Lord is bringing your own evil upon your head.
text.DRB: And the king said to Semei: Thou knowest all the evil, whereof thy heart is privy to thyself, which thou didst to David my father: our Lord hath rendered thy malice upon thy head.
