text.NRSV: But King Solomon shall be blessed, and the throne of David shall be established before the Lord forever.”
text.Rotherham: But, King Solomon, shall be blessed,—and, the throne of David, shall be established before Yahweh, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: And king Solomon shall be blessed, and the throne of David shall be established before the Lord for ever.
text.NABRE: But King Solomon shall be blessed, and David’s throne shall be established before the Lord forever.”
text.DRB: And king Salomon be blessed, and the throne of David shall be stable before our Lord for ever.

text.NRSV: Then the king commanded Benaiah son of Jehoiada; and he went out and struck him down, and he died. So the kingdom was established in the hand of Solomon.
text.Rotherham: So the king charged Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and he went out and fell upon him, that he died,—and, the kingdom, was established in the hand of Solomon.
text.KJV: So the king commanded Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; which went out, and fell upon him, that he died. And the kingdom was established in the hand of Solomon.
text.NABRE: The king then gave the order to Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, who went out and struck him dead. And the royal power was established in Solomon’s hand.
text.DRB: The king therefore commanded Banaias the son of Joiada: who going out, struck him, and he died.

text.NRSV: Solomon made an alliance with Pharaoh king of Egypt and married his daughter. He brought her to the city of David until he finished building his palace and the temple of the Lord, and the wall around Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And Solomon contracted an alliance with Pharaoh king of Egypt,—and took the daughter of Pharaoh, and brought her into the city of David, until he had made an end of building his own house, and the house of Yahweh, and the wall of Jerusalem, round about.
text.KJV: And Solomon made affinity with Pharaoh king of Egypt, and took Pharaoh’s daughter, and brought her into the city of David, until he had made an end of building his own house, and the house of the Lord, and the wall of Jerusalem round about.
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 Early Promise of Solomon’s Reign. Solomon allied himself by marriage with Pharaoh, king of Egypt. He married the daughter of Pharaoh and brought her to the City of David, until he should finish building his own house, and the house of the Lord, and the wall around Jerusalem.
text.DRB: The kingdom therefore was established in the hand of Salomon, and he was joined in affinity to Pharao the king of Ægypt: for he took his daughter, and brought her into the city of David, until he accomplished building his own house, and the house of our Lord, and the wall of Jerusalem round about.

text.NRSV: The people, however, were still sacrificing at the high places, because a temple had not yet been built for the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Save only, that the people were sacrificing in the high places,—because there had not been built a house unto the Name of Yahweh, until those days,
text.KJV: Only the people sacrificed in high places, because there was no house built unto the name of the Lord, until those days.
text.NABRE: The people were sacrificing on the high places, however, for up to that time no house had been built for the name of the Lord.
text.DRB: But yet the people immolated in the excelses: for there was no temple built to the name of our Lord until that day.

text.NRSV: Solomon showed his love for the Lord by walking according to the statutes of his father David; only he sacrificed and offered incense at the high places.
text.Rotherham: Solomon loved Yahweh, by walking in the statutes of David his father,—save only, that, in the high places, he himself, was sacrificing and offering incense.
text.KJV: And Solomon loved the Lord, walking in the statutes of David his father: only he sacrificed and burnt incense in high places.
text.NABRE: Although Solomon loved the Lord, walking in the statutes of David his father, he offered sacrifice and burned incense on the high places.
text.DRB: And Salomon loved our Lord, walking in the precepts of David his father, saving that he immolated in the excelses, and burnt incense.

text.NRSV: The king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there, for that was the principal high place; Solomon used to offer a thousand burnt offerings on that altar.
text.Rotherham: So then the king went to Gibeon, to sacrifice there, for, that, was the great high place,—a thousand ascending-sacrifices, did Solomon offer up on that altar.
text.KJV: And the king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there; for that was the great high place: a thousand burnt offerings did Solomon offer upon that altar.
text.NABRE: The king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there, because that was the great high place. Upon its altar Solomon sacrificed a thousand burnt offerings.
text.DRB: He went therefore into Gabaon, to immolate there: for that was a very great excelse: a thousand hosts for holocaust did Salomon offer upon that altar in Gabaon.

text.NRSV: At Gibeon the Lord appeared to Solomon in a dream by night; and God said, “Ask what I should give you.”
text.Rotherham: In Gibeon, Yahweh appeared unto Solomon, in a dream by night,—and God said, Ask, what I shall give thee.
text.KJV: In Gibeon the Lord appeared to Solomon in a dream by night: and God said, Ask what I shall give thee.
text.NABRE: In Gibeon the Lord appeared to Solomon in a dream at night. God said: Whatever you ask I shall give you.
text.DRB: And our Lord appeared to Salomon in a dream by night, saying: Ask what thou wilt that I may give it thee.

text.NRSV: And Solomon said, “You have shown great and steadfast love to your servant my father David, because he walked before you in faithfulness, in righteousness, and in uprightness of heart toward you; and you have kept for him this great and steadfast love, and have given him a son to sit on his throne today.
text.Rotherham: Then said Solomon—Thou, didst deal, with thy servant David my father, in great lovingkindness, according as he walked before thee, in truth and in righteousness and in uprightness of heart, with thee,—and thou hast kept, for him, this great lovingkindness, that thou hast given unto him a son, to sit upon his throne, as it is this day.
text.KJV: And Solomon said, Thou hast shewed unto thy servant David my father great mercy, according as he walked before thee in truth, and in righteousness, and in uprightness of heart with thee; and thou hast kept for him this great kindness, that thou hast given him a son to sit on his throne, as it is this day.
text.NABRE: Solomon answered: “You have shown great kindness to your servant, David my father, because he walked before you with fidelity, justice, and an upright heart; and you have continued this great kindness toward him today, giving him a son to sit upon his throne.
text.DRB: And Salomon said: Thou hast done great mercy with thy servant David my father, even as he walked in thy sight in truth, and justice, and a right heart with thee: for thou hast kept thy great mercy, and hast given him a son sitting upon his throne, as it is this day.

text.NRSV: And now, O Lord my God, you have made your servant king in place of my father David, although I am only a little child; I do not know how to go out or come in.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh my God, thou, hast made thy servant king, instead of David my father,—and, I, am but a little child, I know not how to go out and come in.
text.KJV: And now, O Lord my God, thou hast made thy servant king instead of David my father: and I am but a little child: I know not how to go out or come in.
text.NABRE: Now, Lord, my God, you have made me, your servant, king to succeed David my father; but I am a mere youth, not knowing at all how to act—
text.DRB: And now Lord God, thou hast made thy servant to reign for David my father, but I am a little child, and ignorant of my going out and coming in.

text.NRSV: And your servant is in the midst of the people whom you have chosen, a numerous people, so many that they cannot be numbered or counted.
text.Rotherham: And, thy servant, is in the midst of thy people, whom thou hast chosen,—a numerous people, that cannot be numbered or summed up, for multitude.
text.KJV: And thy servant is in the midst of thy people which thou hast chosen, a great people, that cannot be numbered nor counted for multitude.
text.NABRE: I, your servant, among the people you have chosen, a people so vast that it cannot be numbered or counted.
text.DRB: And thy servant is in the midst of the people, which thou hast chosen, a people infinite, which cannot be numbered and counted for the multitude.

text.NRSV: Give your servant therefore an understanding mind to govern your people, able to discern between good and evil; for who can govern this your great people?”
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou, then, give unto thy servant, a heart that hearkeneth, to judge thy people, to discern between good and bad,—for who is able to judge this thine honoured people?
text.KJV: Give therefore thy servant an understanding heart to judge thy people, that I may discern between good and bad: for who is able to judge this thy so great a people?
text.NABRE: Give your servant, therefore, a listening heart to judge your people and to distinguish between good and evil. For who is able to give judgment for this vast people of yours?”
text.DRB: Thou shalt therefore give to thy servant a docible heart, that he may judge the people, and discern between good & evil. For who shall be able to judge this people, this thy people great in number?

text.NRSV: It pleased the Lord that Solomon had asked this.
text.Rotherham: And the thing was good in the eyes of the Lord,—that Solomon had asked this thing;
text.KJV: And the speech pleased the Lord, that Solomon had asked this thing.
text.NABRE: The Lord was pleased by Solomon’s request.
text.DRB: The word therefore was liked before our Lord, that Salomon had asked such a thing.

text.NRSV: God said to him, “Because you have asked this, and have not asked for yourself long life or riches, or for the life of your enemies, but have asked for yourself understanding to discern what is right,
text.Rotherham: And God said unto him—Because thou hast asked this thing, and hast not asked for thyself many days, nor asked for thyself riches, nor asked the lives of thine enemies,—but hast asked for thyself discernment, in hearing a cause,
text.KJV: And God said unto him, Because thou hast asked this thing, and hast not asked for thyself long life; neither hast asked riches for thyself, nor hast asked the life of thine enemies; but hast asked for thyself understanding to discern judgment;
text.NABRE: So God said to him: Because you asked for this—you did not ask for a long life for yourself, nor for riches, nor for the life of your enemies—but you asked for discernment to know what is right—
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Salomon: Because thou hast asked this thing, and hast not desired for thyself many days, nor riches, nor the lives of thine enemies, but hast desired wisdom for thyself to discern judgment:

text.NRSV: I now do according to your word. Indeed I give you a wise and discerning mind; no one like you has been before you, and no one like you shall arise after you.
text.Rotherham: lo! I have done according to thy word,—lo! I have given unto thee a wise and discerning heart, so that, like thee, hath been none, before thee, and, after thee, shall rise up none, like thee.
text.KJV: Behold, I have done according to thy words: lo, I have given thee a wise and an understanding heart; so that there was none like thee before thee, neither after thee shall any arise like unto thee.
text.NABRE: I now do as you request. I give you a heart so wise and discerning that there has never been anyone like you until now, nor after you will there be anyone to equal you.
text.DRB: behold I have done unto thee according to thy words, and have given thee a wise heart and intelligent, in so much that none before thee hath been like thee, nor shall arise after thee.

text.NRSV: I give you also what you have not asked, both riches and honor all your life; no other king shall compare with you.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, what thou hast not asked, have I given unto thee, both riches and honour,—so that there hath been none, like thee, among the kings, all thy days.
text.KJV: And I have also given thee that which thou hast not asked, both riches, and honour: so that there shall not be any among the kings like unto thee all thy days.
text.NABRE: In addition, I give you what you have not asked for: I give you such riches and glory that among kings there will be no one like you all your days.
text.DRB: Yea and these things, which thou didst not ask, I have given thee; to wit, riches, and glory, so that none hath been like thee among the kings all days heretofore.

text.NRSV: If you will walk in my ways, keeping my statutes and my commandments, as your father David walked, then I will lengthen your life.”
text.Rotherham: And, if thou wilt walk in my ways, by keeping my statutes, and my commandments, as, David thy father, walked, then will I lengthen out thy days.
text.KJV: And if thou wilt walk in my ways, to keep my statutes and my commandments, as thy father David did walk, then I will lengthen thy days.
text.NABRE: And if you walk in my ways, keeping my statutes and commandments, as David your father did, I will give you a long life.
text.DRB: And if thou wilt walk in my ways, and keep my precepts, & my commandments, as thy father walked, I will make thy days long.

text.NRSV: Then Solomon awoke; it had been a dream. He came to Jerusalem and stood before the ark of the covenant of the Lord. He offered up burnt offerings and offerings of well-being, and provided a feast for all his servants.
text.Rotherham: And Solomon awoke, and lo! it was a dream. So he entered into Jerusalem, and stood before the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and caused to go up ascending-sacrifices and offered peace-offerings, and made a banquet for all his servants.
text.KJV: And Solomon awoke; and, behold, it was a dream. And he came to Jerusalem, and stood before the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and offered up burnt offerings, and offered peace offerings, and made a feast to all his servants.
text.NABRE: Solomon awoke; it was a dream! He went to Jerusalem, stood before the ark of the covenant of the Lord, sacrificed burnt offerings and communion offerings, and gave a feast for all his servants. Solomon’s Listening Heart.
text.DRB: Therefore Salomon awaked, and perceived that it was a dream: and when he was come to Jerusalem, he stood before the ark of covenant of our Lord, and offered holocausts, and made pacific victims, and a great feast to all his servants.

text.NRSV: Later, two women who were prostitutes came to the king and stood before him.
text.Rotherham: Then, came there in two unchaste women, unto the king,—and stood before him.
text.KJV: Then came there two women, that were harlots, unto the king, and stood before him.
text.NABRE: Later, two prostitutes came to the king and stood before him.
text.DRB: Then came there two women harlots to the king, and stood before him:

text.NRSV: One woman said, “Please, my lord, this woman and I live in the same house; and I gave birth while she was in the house.
text.Rotherham: And the one woman said—Pardon, my lord! I and this woman, dwell in one house,—and I gave birth to a child near her, in the house.
text.KJV: And the one woman said, O my lord, I and this woman dwell in one house; and I was delivered of a child with her in the house.
text.NABRE: One woman said: “By your leave, my lord, this woman and I live in the same house, and I gave birth in the house while she was present.
text.DRB: of the which one said: I beseech thee, my lord, I and this woman dwelt in one house, and I was delivered of a child beside her in the chamber.

text.NRSV: Then on the third day after I gave birth, this woman also gave birth. We were together; there was no one else with us in the house, only the two of us were in the house.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, the third day after I bare, that, this woman also, gave birth to a child,—we two, being together, there was no stranger with us in the house, none but we two in the house.
text.KJV: And it came to pass the third day after that I was delivered, that this woman was delivered also: and we were together; there was no stranger with us in the house, save we two in the house.
text.NABRE: On the third day after I gave birth, this woman also gave birth. We were alone; no one else was in the house with us; only the two of us were in the house.
text.DRB: And the third day, after that I was delivered, she also was delivered, and we were together, and no other person with us in the house, except we two.

text.NRSV: Then this woman’s son died in the night, because she lay on him.
text.Rotherham: And this woman’s son died in the night,—because she overlaid it.
text.KJV: And this woman’s child died in the night; because she overlaid it.
text.NABRE: This woman’s son died during the night when she lay on top of him.
text.DRB: And this woman's child died in the night. For sleeping she oppressed him.

text.NRSV: She got up in the middle of the night and took my son from beside me while your servant slept. She laid him at her breast, and laid her dead son at my breast.
text.Rotherham: Then arose she, in the middle of the night, and took my son from beside me, while thy handmaid was sleeping, and laid it in her own besom,—but, her dead son, laid she in, my, bosom.
text.KJV: And she arose at midnight, and took my son from beside me, while thine handmaid slept, and laid it in her bosom, and laid her dead child in my bosom.
text.NABRE: So in the middle of the night she got up and took my son from my side, as your servant was sleeping. Then she laid him in her bosom and laid her dead son in my bosom.
text.DRB: And rising in the dead time of the night, she took my child from the side of me thy handmaid being asleep, and laid it in her bosom: and her child that was dead, she put in my bosom.

text.NRSV: When I rose in the morning to nurse my son, I saw that he was dead; but when I looked at him closely in the morning, clearly it was not the son I had borne.”
text.Rotherham: And, when I arose in the morning, to give suck unto my child, lo! it was dead! but, when I looked at it narrowly in the morning, lo! it was not, my son that I had borne.
text.KJV: And when I rose in the morning to give my child suck, behold, it was dead: but when I had considered it in the morning, behold, it was not my son, which I did bear.
text.NABRE: I rose in the morning to nurse my son, and he was dead! But when I examined him in the morning light, I saw it was not the son I had borne.”
text.DRB: And when I was risen in the morning to give my child milk, he appeared dead: whom more diligently beholding when it was clear day, I found that it was not mine which I bare.

text.NRSV: But the other woman said, “No, the living son is mine, and the dead son is yours.” The first said, “No, the dead son is yours, and the living son is mine.” So they argued before the king.
text.Rotherham: Then said the other woman—Nay! but, my son, is the, living, and, thy son, the, dead. But, this, one kept on saying—Nay, verily! but, thy son, is the, dead, and, my son, the, living. Thus spake they before the king.
text.KJV: And the other woman said, Nay; but the living is my son, and the dead is thy son. And this said, No; but the dead is thy son, and the living is my son. Thus they spake before the king.
text.NABRE: The other woman answered, “No! The living one is my son, the dead one is yours.” But the first kept saying, “No! the dead one is your son, the living one is mine!” Thus they argued before the king.
text.DRB: And the other woman answered: It is not so as thou sayst, but thy child is dead, and mine liveth. On the contrary part she said: Thou liest: for my child liveth, and thy child is dead. And in this manner they strove before the king.

text.NRSV: Then the king said, “The one says, ‘This is my son that is alive, and your son is dead’; while the other says, ‘No, your son is dead, and my son is the living one.’”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king, The one woman, is saying, This, is, my son, the one that liveth, and, thy son, is, the one that is dead,—and, the other, is saying, Nay! but, thy son, is, the dead one, and, my son, the, living.
text.KJV: Then said the king, The one saith, This is my son that liveth, and thy son is the dead: and the other saith, Nay; but thy son is the dead, and my son is the living.
text.NABRE: Then the king said: “One woman claims, ‘This, the living one, is my son, the dead one is yours.’ The other answers, ‘No! The dead one is your son, the living one is mine.’”
text.DRB: Then said the king: This woman saith: My child liveth, and thy child is dead. And this hath answered, No, but thy child is dead, and mine liveth.

text.NRSV: So the king said, “Bring me a sword,” and they brought a sword before the king.
text.Rotherham: And the king said—Bring me a sword! So they brought a sword before the king.
text.KJV: And the king said, Bring me a sword. And they brought a sword before the king.
text.NABRE: The king continued, “Get me a sword.” When they brought the sword before the king,
text.DRB: The king therefore said: Bring me a sword. And when they had brought a sword before the king,

text.NRSV: The king said, “Divide the living boy in two; then give half to the one, and half to the other.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king, Divide the living child, in twain,—and give half to the one, and half to the other.
text.KJV: And the king said, Divide the living child in two, and give half to the one, and half to the other.
text.NABRE: he said, “Cut the living child in two, and give half to one woman and half to the other.”
text.DRB: Divide, quoth he, the living child into two parts, and give the half part to one, and half to the other.

text.NRSV: But the woman whose son was alive said to the king—because compassion for her son burned within her—“Please, my lord, give her the living boy; certainly do not kill him!” The other said, “It shall be neither mine nor yours; divide it.”
text.Rotherham: Then spake the woman, whose was the living child, unto the king—for tender became her compassions over her son—so she said—Pardon, my lord! Give, her, the living child, and do not, kill, it. But the other kept on saying—Neither mine, nor thine, shall it be, divide it.
text.KJV: Then spake the woman whose the living child was unto the king, for her bowels yearned upon her son, and she said, O my lord, give her the living child, and in no wise slay it. But the other said, Let it be neither mine nor thine, but divide it.
text.NABRE: The woman whose son was alive, because she was stirred with compassion for her son, said to the king, “Please, my lord, give her the living baby—do not kill it!” But the other said, “It shall be neither mine nor yours. Cut it in two!”
text.DRB: But the woman, whose child was alive, said to the king (for her bowels were moved upon her child) I beseech thee my lord, give her the child alive, and kill it not. On the contrary part she said: be it neither mine, nor thine, but let it be divided.

text.NRSV: Then the king responded: “Give the first woman the living boy; do not kill him. She is his mother.”
text.Rotherham: Then responded the king, and said—Give, her, the living child, ye shall not, kill, it,—she, is its mother.
text.KJV: Then the king answered and said, Give her the living child, and in no wise slay it: she is the mother thereof.
text.NABRE: The king then answered, “Give her the living baby! Do not kill it! She is the mother.”
text.DRB: The king answered, and said: Give unto this woman the infant alive, and let it not be killed, for this is the mother thereof.

text.NRSV: All Israel heard of the judgment that the king had rendered; and they stood in awe of the king, because they perceived that the wisdom of God was in him, to execute justice.
text.Rotherham: And all Israel heard of the judgment which the king had delivered, and they stood in awe before the king,—for they saw, that, the wisdom of God, was in him, to execute justice.
text.KJV: And all Israel heard of the judgment which the king had judged; and they feared the king: for they saw that the wisdom of God was in him, to do judgment.
text.NABRE: When all Israel heard the judgment the king had given, they were in awe of him, because they saw that the king had in him the wisdom of God for giving right judgment.
text.DRB: All Israel therefore heard the judgment that the king had judged, and they feared the king, seeing the wisdom of God to be in him to do judgment.

text.NRSV: King Solomon was king over all Israel,
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass, that, King Solomon, was king, over all Israel.
text.KJV: So king Solomon was king over all Israel.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 Solomon’s Riches: Domestic Affairs. Solomon was king over all Israel,
text.DRB: And king Salomon was reigning over all Israel:

text.NRSV: and these were his high officials: Azariah son of Zadok was the priest;
text.Rotherham: Now, these, were his chief officers of state,—Azariah son of Zadok, the priest;
text.KJV: And these were the princes which he had; Azariah the son of Zadok the priest,
text.NABRE: and these were the officials he had in his service: Azariah, son of Zadok, the priest;
text.DRB: and these were the princes which he had: Azarias the son of Sadoc the priest:

text.NRSV: Elihoreph and Ahijah sons of Shisha were secretaries; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder;
text.Rotherham: Elihoreph and Ahijah, sons of Shisha, scribes,—Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud, the recorder;
text.KJV: Elihoreph and Ahiah, the sons of Shisha, scribes; Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud, the recorder.
text.NABRE: Elihoreph and Ahijah, sons of Shisha, scribes; Jehoshaphat, son of Ahilud, the chancellor;
text.DRB: Elihoreph, and Ahia the sons of Sisa Scribes: Josaphat the son of Ahilud, register:

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was in command of the army; Zadok and Abiathar were priests;
text.Rotherham: And Benaiah son of Jehoiada, over the army,—and Zadok and Abiathar priests;
text.KJV: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the host: and Zadok and Abiathar were the priests:
text.NABRE: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, in charge of the army; Zadok and Abiathar, priests;
text.DRB: Banaias the son of Joiada, over the army: and Sadoc, and Abiathar priests.

text.NRSV: Azariah son of Nathan was over the officials; Zabud son of Nathan was priest and king’s friend;
text.Rotherham: And Azariah son of Nathan, over the governors,—and Zabud son of Nathan, minister, king’s friend;
text.KJV: And Azariah the son of Nathan was over the officers: and Zabud the son of Nathan was principal officer, and the king’s friend:
text.NABRE: Azariah, son of Nathan, in charge of the governors; Zabud, son of Nathan, priest and companion to the king;
text.DRB: Azarias the son of Nathan, over them that assisted the king: Zabud the son of Nathan priest, the king's friend:

text.NRSV: Ahishar was in charge of the palace; and Adoniram son of Abda was in charge of the forced labor.
text.Rotherham: And Ahishar, over the household,—and Adoniram son of Abda, over the tribute.
text.KJV: And Ahishar was over the household: and Adoniram the son of Abda was over the tribute.
text.NABRE: Ahishar, master of the palace; and Adoniram, son of Abda, in charge of the forced labor.
text.DRB: and Ahizar governor of the house: and Adoniram the son of Abda over the tributes.

text.NRSV: Solomon had twelve officials over all Israel, who provided food for the king and his household; each one had to make provision for one month in the year.
text.Rotherham: And, Solomon, had twelve governors over all Israel, who used to sustain the king and his household,—for a month in the year, was it appointed unto each one, to find sustenance.
text.KJV: And Solomon had twelve officers over all Israel, which provided victuals for the king and his household: each man his month in a year made provision.
text.NABRE: Solomon had twelve governors over all Israel who supplied food for the king and his household, each having to provide for one month in the year.
text.DRB: And Salomon had twelve governors over all Israel, which served out victuals for the king and for his house: for every one ministered necessaries, each man his month in the year.

text.NRSV: These were their names: Ben-hur, in the hill country of Ephraim;
text.Rotherham: And, these, are their names, Ben-hur, in the hill country of Ephraim;
text.KJV: And these are their names: The son of Hur, in mount Ephraim:
text.NABRE: Their names were: the son of Hur in the hill country of Ephraim;
text.DRB: And these are their names: Benhur in mount Ephraim.

text.NRSV: Ben-deker, in Makaz, Shaalbim, Beth-shemesh, and Elon-beth-hanan;
text.Rotherham: Ben-deker, in Makaz and in Shaalbim and Beth-shemesh,—and Elon-beth-hanan;
text.KJV: The son of Dekar, in Makaz, and in Shaalbim, and Beth–shemesh, and Elon–beth–hanan:
text.NABRE: the son of Deker in Makaz, Shaalbim, Beth-shemesh, and Elon Beth-hanan;
text.DRB: Bendecar, in Macces, and in Salebim, and in Bethsames, and in Elon, and in Bethhanan.

text.NRSV: Ben-hesed, in Arubboth (to him belonged Socoh and all the land of Hepher);
text.Rotherham: Ben-hesed, in Arubboth,—to him, pertaineth Socoh, and all the land of Hepher;
text.KJV: The son of Hesed, in Aruboth; to him pertained Sochoh, and all the land of Hepher:
text.NABRE: the son of Hesed in Arubboth, as well as in Socoh and the whole region of Hepher;
text.DRB: Benhesed in Aruboth: his was Socho, and all the land Epher.

text.NRSV: Ben-abinadab, in all Naphath-dor (he had Taphath, Solomon’s daughter, as his wife);
text.Rotherham: Ben-abinadab, all the height of Dor,—Taphath daughter of Solomon, had he to wife;
text.KJV: The son of Abinadab, in all the region of Dor; which had Taphath the daughter of Solomon to wife:
text.NABRE: the son of Abinadab, in all Naphath-dor; he was married to Taphath, Solomon’s daughter;
text.DRB: Benabinadab, whose was all Nepha Dor, had Tapheth the daughter of Salomon to wife.

text.NRSV: Baana son of Ahilud, in Taanach, Megiddo, and all Beth-shean, which is beside Zarethan below Jezreel, and from Beth-shean to Abel-meholah, as far as the other side of Jokmeam;
text.Rotherham: Baana son of Ahilud, in Taanach, and Megiddo,—and all Beth-shean which is beside Zarethan, beneath Jezreel, from Beth-shean as far as Abel-meholah, as far as over against Jokmeam;
text.KJV: Baana the son of Ahilud; to him pertained Taanach and Megiddo, and all Beth–shean, which is by Zartanah beneath Jezreel, from Beth–shean to Abel–meholah, even unto the place that is beyond Jokneam:
text.NABRE: Baana, son of Ahilud, in Taanach and Megiddo and all Beth-shean near Zarethan below Jezreel, from Beth-shean to Abel-meholah to beyond Jokmeam;
text.DRB: Bana the son of Ahilud governed Thanach and Mageddo, and all Bethsan, which is beside Sarthana under Jezrahel, from Bethsan unto Abelmehula over against Jecmaan.

text.NRSV: Ben-geber, in Ramoth-gilead (he had the villages of Jair son of Manasseh, which are in Gilead, and he had the region of Argob, which is in Bashan, sixty great cities with walls and bronze bars);
text.Rotherham: Ben-geber, in Ramoth-gilead,—to him, belonged the towns of Jair, son of Manasseh, which are in Gilead, his, was the region of Argob which is in Bashan, sixty great cities, with walls and with bars of bronze;
text.KJV: The son of Geber, in Ramoth–gilead; to him pertained the towns of Jair the son of Manasseh, which are in Gilead; to him also pertained the region of Argob, which is in Bashan, threescore great cities with walls and brasen bars:
text.NABRE: the son of Geber in Ramoth-gilead, having charge of the villages of Jair, son of Manasseh, in Gilead; and of the district of Argob in Bashan—sixty large walled cities with gates barred with bronze;
text.DRB: Bengaber in Ramoth Galaad: had Avothjair the son of Manasses in Galaad, he was chief in all the country of Argob, which is in Basan, three score cities great and walled, which had brazen locks.

text.NRSV: Ahinadab son of Iddo, in Mahanaim;
text.Rotherham: Ahinadab son of Iddo, towards Mahanaim;
text.KJV: Ahinadab the son of Iddo had Mahanaim:
text.NABRE: Ahinadab, son of Iddo, in Mahanaim;
text.DRB: Ahinadab the son of Addo was chief in Manaim.

text.NRSV: Ahimaaz, in Naphtali (he had taken Basemath, Solomon’s daughter, as his wife);
text.Rotherham: Ahimaaz, in Naphtali,—moreover, he, took Basemath daughter of Solomon, to wife;
text.KJV: Ahimaaz was in Naphtali; he also took Basmath the daughter of Solomon to wife:
text.NABRE: Ahimaaz, in Naphtali; he was married to Basemath, another daughter of Solomon;
text.DRB: Achimaas in Nephthali: yea he also had Basemath the daughter of Salomon in marriage.

text.NRSV: Baana son of Hushai, in Asher and Bealoth;
text.Rotherham: Banna son of Hushai, in Asher and in Aloth;
text.KJV: Baanah the son of Hushai was in Asher and in Aloth:
text.NABRE: Baana, son of Hushai, in Asher and Aloth;
text.DRB: Baana the son of Husi, in Aser, and in Baloth.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat son of Paruah, in Issachar;
text.Rotherham: Jehoshaphat son of Paruah, in Issachar;
text.KJV: Jehoshaphat the son of Paruah, in Issachar:
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat, son of Paruah, in Issachar;
text.DRB: Josaphat the son of Pharue, in Issachar.

text.NRSV: Shimei son of Ela, in Benjamin;
text.Rotherham: Shimei son of Ela, in Benjamin;
text.KJV: Shimei the son of Elah, in Benjamin:
text.NABRE: Shimei, son of Ela, in Benjamin;
text.DRB: Semei the son of Ela, in Benjamin.

text.NRSV: Geber son of Uri, in the land of Gilead, the country of King Sihon of the Amorites and of King Og of Bashan. And there was one official in the land of Judah.
text.Rotherham: Geber son of Uri, in the land of Gilead,—the land of Sihon king of the Amorites, and Og king of Bashan, being, the one governor, that was in the land.
text.KJV: Geber the son of Uri was in the country of Gilead, in the country of Sihon king of the Amorites, and of Og king of Bashan; and he was the only officer which was in the land.
text.NABRE: Geber, son of Uri, in the land of Gilead, the land of Sihon, king of the Amorites, and of Og, king of Bashan. There was one governor besides, in the land of Judah.
text.DRB: Gaber the son of Uri, in the land of Galaad in the land of Sehon the king of the Ammorrheite, & of Og the king of Basan, over all things that were in that Land.

text.NRSV: Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sand by the sea; they ate and drank and were happy.
text.Rotherham: Judah and Israel, were many, as the sand that is by the sea, for multitude,—eating and drinking, and rejoicing.
text.KJV: Judah and Israel were many, as the sand which is by the sea in multitude, eating and drinking, and making merry.
text.NABRE: Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sands by the sea; they ate and drank and rejoiced.
text.DRB: Juda and Israel innumerable, as the sand of the sea in multitude: eating, and drinking, and rejoicing.

text.NRSV: Solomon was sovereign over all the kingdoms from the Euphrates to the land of the Philistines, even to the border of Egypt; they brought tribute and served Solomon all the days of his life.
text.Rotherham: And, Solomon, became ruler over all the kingdoms, from the River [even unto] the land of the Philistines, and as far as the boundary of Egypt,—and these were bringing presents, and serving Solomon, all the days of his life.
text.KJV: And Solomon reigned over all kingdoms from the river unto the land of the Philistines, and unto the border of Egypt: they brought presents, and served Solomon all the days of his life.
text.NABRE: Solomon ruled over all the kingdoms from the River to the land of the Philistines, down to the border of Egypt; they paid Solomon tribute and served him as long as he lived.
text.DRB: And Salomon was in his dominion, having all the Kingdoms with him from the river of the land of the Philisthiims unto the border of Ægypt: of them that offered him presents, & served him all the days of his life.

text.NRSV: Solomon’s provision for one day was thirty cors of choice flour, and sixty cors of meal,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that Solomon’s provision for one day was,—thirty measures of fine flour, and sixty measures of meal;
text.KJV: And Solomon’s provision for one day was thirty measures of fine flour, and threescore measures of meal,
text.DRB: And the provision of Salomon was every day thirty measures of flour, & threescore measures of meal,

text.NRSV: ten fatted oxen, and twenty pasture-fed cattle, and one hundred sheep, besides deer, gazelles, roebucks, and fatted fowl.
text.Rotherham: ten fat oxen, and twenty oxen out of the pastures, and a hundred sheep,—besides harts, and gazelles, and roebucks, and fatted fowl.
text.KJV: Ten fat oxen, and twenty oxen out of the pastures, and an hundred sheep, beside harts, and roebucks, and fallowdeer, and fatted fowl.
text.DRB: ten fat oxen and twenty pasture-fed, & a hundred rams, beside the venison of harts, roes, and buffles, and fatted fowl.

text.NRSV: For he had dominion over all the region west of the Euphrates from Tiphsah to Gaza, over all the kings west of the Euphrates; and he had peace on all sides fore and aft.
text.Rotherham: For, he, had dominion over all on this side the River, from Tiphsah even as far as Gaza, over all the kings on this side the River,—and he had, peace, on all sides of him, round about.
text.KJV: For he had dominion over all the region on this side the river, from Tiphsah even to Azzah, over all the kings on this side the river: and he had peace on all sides round about him.
text.DRB: For he possessed all the country, which was beyond the river, from Thapsa unto Gaza, and all the kings of those countries: and he had peace on every side round about.

text.NRSV: During Solomon’s lifetime Judah and Israel lived in safety, from Dan even to Beer-sheba, all of them under their vines and fig trees.
text.Rotherham: And Judah and Israel dwelt securely, every man under his own vine, and under his own fig-tree, from Dan even unto Beer-sheba,—all the days of Solomon.
text.KJV: And Judah and Israel dwelt safely, every man under his vine and under his fig tree, from Dan even to Beer–sheba, all the days of Solomon.
text.DRB: And Juda and Israel dwelt without any fear, every one under his vine, and under his fig tree, from Dan unto Bersabee all the days of Salomon.

text.NRSV: Solomon also had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots, and twelve thousand horsemen.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, Solomon, had forty thousand stalls of horses, for his chariots,—and twelve thousand horsemen.
text.KJV: And Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots, and twelve thousand horsemen.
text.NABRE: Solomon had forty thousand stalls for horses for chariots and twelve thousand horsemen.
text.DRB: And Salomon had forty thousand stalls of chariot-horses, and twelve thousand for the saddle.

text.NRSV: Those officials supplied provisions for King Solomon and for all who came to King Solomon’s table, each one in his month; they let nothing be lacking.
text.Rotherham: And these governors provided sustenance for King Solomon, and for all that drew near unto the table of King Solomon, every man in his month,—they let, nought, be lacking.
text.KJV: And those officers provided victual for king Solomon, and for all that came unto king Solomon’s table, every man in his month: they lacked nothing.
text.DRB: And the foresaid governors of the king fed them: yea and the necessaries of king Salomon's table they gave forth with great care in their time.

text.NRSV: They also brought to the required place barley and straw for the horses and swift steeds, each according to his charge.
text.Rotherham: Barley also and crushed straw, for the horses and for the swift beasts, brought they in unto the place where it should be, every man according to his charge.
text.KJV: Barley also and straw for the horses and dromedaries brought they unto the place where the officers were, every man according to his charge.
text.DRB: Barley also and straw for the horses, and beasts, they brought to the place, where the king was, according as it was appointed them.

text.NRSV: God gave Solomon very great wisdom, discernment, and breadth of understanding as vast as the sand on the seashore,
text.Rotherham: And God gave wisdom unto Solomon, and discernment, and very great largeness of heart,—like the sand that is on the shore of the sea:
text.KJV: And God gave Solomon wisdom and understanding exceeding much, and largeness of heart, even as the sand that is on the sea shore.
text.NABRE: Moreover, God gave Solomon wisdom, exceptional understanding, and knowledge, as vast as the sand on the seashore.
text.DRB: God also gave wisdom to Salomon and prudence exceeding much, and latitude of heart as the sand that is in the sea shore.

text.NRSV: so that Solomon’s wisdom surpassed the wisdom of all the people of the east, and all the wisdom of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: so that, the wisdom of Solomon, excelled, the wisdom of all the sons of the East,—and all the wisdom of the Egyptians,
text.KJV: And Solomon’s wisdom excelled the wisdom of all the children of the east country, and all the wisdom of Egypt.
text.DRB: And the wisdom of Salomon passed the wisdom of all them in the east, and of the Ægyptians,

text.NRSV: He was wiser than anyone else, wiser than Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman, Calcol, and Darda, children of Mahol; his fame spread throughout all the surrounding nations.
text.Rotherham: so that he was wiser than any man—than Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman and Calcol and Darda, sons of Mahol,—and it came to pass that, his name, was throughout all the nations round about.
text.KJV: For he was wiser than all men; than Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman, and Chalcol, and Darda, the sons of Mahol: and his fame was in all nations round about.
text.DRB: and he was wiser than all men: wiser than Ethan the Ezralite, and Heman, and Chalcol, & Dorda the sons of Mahol, and he was renowned in all nations round about.

text.NRSV: He composed three thousand proverbs, and his songs numbered a thousand and five.
text.Rotherham: And he spake three thousand proverbs,—and it came to pass that, his songs, were a thousand and five.
text.KJV: And he spake three thousand proverbs: and his songs were a thousand and five.
text.DRB: Salomon also spake three thousand parables: and his songs were a thousand & five.

text.NRSV: He would speak of trees, from the cedar that is in the Lebanon to the hyssop that grows in the wall; he would speak of animals, and birds, and reptiles, and fish.
text.Rotherham: And he discoursed of trees—from the cedar that is in Lebanon, even unto the hyssop, that springeth out in the wall,—and he discoursed of beasts and of birds, and of creeping things, and of fishes.
text.KJV: And he spake of trees, from the cedar tree that is in Lebanon even unto the hyssop that springeth out of the wall: he spake also of beasts, and of fowl, and of creeping things, and of fishes.
text.DRB: And he disputed of trees from the cedar that is in Libanus, unto the hyssop which cometh out of the wall: & he discoursed of beasts, and fowls, and creeping worms, and fishes.

text.NRSV: People came from all the nations to hear the wisdom of Solomon; they came from all the kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom.
text.Rotherham: And there came in, of all the peoples, to hear the wisdom of Solomon—of all the kings of the earth, who heard his wisdom.
text.KJV: And there came of all people to hear the wisdom of Solomon, from all kings of the earth, which had heard of his wisdom.
text.NABRE: People from all nations came to hear Solomon’s wisdom, sent by all the kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom.
text.DRB: And there came from all people to hear the wisdom of Salomon, and from all the kings of the earth, which heard his wisdom.

text.NRSV: Now King Hiram of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon, when he heard that they had anointed him king in place of his father; for Hiram had always been a friend to David.
text.Rotherham: And Hiram King of Tyre sent his servants unto Solomon, for he had heard that, him, had they anointed king, in the room of his father,—for, Hiram, was, a lover, of, David continually.
text.KJV: And Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants unto Solomon; for he had heard that they had anointed him king in the room of his father: for Hiram was ever a lover of David.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 Solomon’s Riches: International Affairs. Solomon ruled over all the kingdoms from the River to the land of the Philistines, down to the border of Egypt; they paid Solomon tribute and served him as long as he lived.
text.DRB: Hiram also the king of Tyre sent his servants to Salomon: for he heard that they had anointed him king for his father: because Hiram had been David's friend at all time.

text.NRSV: Solomon sent word to Hiram, saying,
text.Rotherham: So Solomon sent unto Hiram, saying:—
text.KJV: And Solomon sent to Hiram, saying,
text.NABRE: Solomon’s provisions for each day were thirty kors of fine flour, sixty kors of meal,
text.DRB: And Salomon sent to Hiram, saying:

text.NRSV: “You know that my father David could not build a house for the name of the Lord his God because of the warfare with which his enemies surrounded him, until the Lord put them under the soles of his feet.
text.Rotherham: Thou, knewest David my father, how that he could not build a house unto the Name of Yahweh his God, because of the wars that were about him on every side,—until Yahweh should put them under the soles of his feet;
text.KJV: Thou knowest how that David my father could not build an house unto the name of the Lord his God for the wars which were about him on every side, until the Lord put them under the soles of his feet.
text.NABRE: ten fatted oxen, twenty pasture-fed oxen, and a hundred sheep, not counting harts, gazelles, roebucks, and fatted fowl.
text.DRB: Thou knowest the will of David my father, and that he could not build a house to the name of our Lord his God, because of wars imminent round about, until our Lord put them under the sole of his feet.

text.NRSV: But now the Lord my God has given me rest on every side; there is neither adversary nor misfortune.
text.Rotherham: but, now, Yahweh my God hath given me rest on every side,—there is neither adversary nor incident of evil.
text.KJV: But now the Lord my God hath given me rest on every side, so that there is neither adversary nor evil occurrent.
text.NABRE: He had dominion over all the land west of the River, from Tiphsah to Gaza, and all its kings, and he had peace on all his borders round about.
text.DRB: But now our Lord my God hath given me rest round about: and there is no satan, nor ill rencounter.

text.NRSV: So I intend to build a house for the name of the Lord my God, as the Lord said to my father David, ‘Your son, whom I will set on your throne in your place, shall build the house for my name.’
text.Rotherham: Behold me! then, purposing to build a house for the Name of Yahweh my God—even as Yahweh spake unto David my father, saying—Thy son, whom I will set in thy stead, upon thy throne, he, shall build the house for my Name.
text.KJV: And, behold, I purpose to build an house unto the name of the Lord my God, as the Lord spake unto David my father, saying, Thy son, whom I will set upon thy throne in thy room, he shall build an house unto my name.
text.NABRE: Thus Judah and Israel lived in security, everyone under their own vine and fig tree from Dan to Beer-sheba, as long as Solomon lived.
text.DRB: Wherefore I purpose to build a temple to the name of our Lord my God, as our Lord hath spoken to David my father, saying: Thy son, whom I will give for thee upon thy throne, he shall build a house to my name.

text.NRSV: Therefore command that cedars from the Lebanon be cut for me. My servants will join your servants, and I will give you whatever wages you set for your servants; for you know that there is no one among us who knows how to cut timber like the Sidonians.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, command thou that they fell me cedars, out of Lebanon, and, my servants, shall be, with thy servants, and, the hire of thy servants, will I give thee, according to all that thou shalt say,—for, thou, knowest, that, none amongst us, hath skill to fell timber, like the Zidonians.
text.KJV: Now therefore command thou that they hew me cedar trees out of Lebanon; and my servants shall be with thy servants: and unto thee will I give hire for thy servants according to all that thou shalt appoint: for thou knowest that there is not among us any that can skill to hew timber like unto the Sidonians.
text.NABRE: Solomon’s Riches: Chariots and Horses. Solomon had forty thousand stalls for horses for chariots and twelve thousand horsemen.
text.DRB: Command therefore that thy servants cut me down cedars out of Libanus, and let my servants be with thy servants: and I will give thee the hire of thy servants whatsoever thou wilt ask, for thou knowest how there is not in my people a man that hath skill to hew wood as the Sidonians.

text.NRSV: When Hiram heard the words of Solomon, he rejoiced greatly, and said, “Blessed be the Lord today, who has given to David a wise son to be over this great people.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Hiram heard the words of Solomon, that he rejoiced greatly,—and said: Blessed, be Yahweh, to-day, who hath given unto David a wise son, over this great people!
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Hiram heard the words of Solomon, that he rejoiced greatly, and said, Blessed be the Lord this day, which hath given unto David a wise son over this great people.
text.NABRE: The governors, one for each month, provided food for King Solomon and for all the guests at King Solomon’s table. They left nothing unprovided.
text.DRB: When Hiram therefore had heard the words of Salomon, he rejoiced exceedingly, and said: Blessed be the Lord God this day, who hath given unto David a son most wise over this people so great in number.

text.NRSV: Hiram sent word to Solomon, “I have heard the message that you have sent to me; I will fulfill all your needs in the matter of cedar and cypress timber.
text.Rotherham: And Hiram sent unto Solomon, saying, I have heard that which thou hast sent unto me,—I, will do all thy desire, as to timber of cedar, and timber of fir.
text.KJV: And Hiram sent to Solomon, saying, I have considered the things which thou sentest to me for: and I will do all thy desire concerning timber of cedar, and concerning timber of fir.
text.NABRE: For the chariot horses and draft animals also, each brought his quota of barley and straw to the required place.
text.DRB: And Hiram sent to Salomon, saying: I have heard whatsoever thou hast willed me: I will do all thy will in cedar trees, and fir trees.

text.NRSV: My servants shall bring it down to the sea from the Lebanon; I will make it into rafts to go by sea to the place you indicate. I will have them broken up there for you to take away. And you shall meet my needs by providing food for my household.”
text.Rotherham: My servants, shall bring them down out of Lebanon unto the sea, and, I, will put them in rafts on the sea, unto the place of which thou shalt send me word, and will cause them to be discharged there, and, thou, shalt receive them, and, thou, shalt do my desire, by giving food for my household.
text.KJV: My servants shall bring them down from Lebanon unto the sea: and I will convey them by sea in floats unto the place that thou shalt appoint me, and will cause them to be discharged there, and thou shalt receive them: and thou shalt accomplish my desire, in giving food for my household.
text.NABRE: Solomon’s Renown. Moreover, God gave Solomon wisdom, exceptional understanding, and knowledge, as vast as the sand on the seashore.
text.DRB: My servants shall bring them down from Libanus to the sea: and I will put them in boats in the sea, unto the place which thou shalt signify to me; & will land them there, and thou shalt take them: and thou shalt allow me necessaries, that there be meat given for my house.

text.NRSV: So Hiram supplied Solomon’s every need for cedar and cypress timber.
text.Rotherham: So then Hiram began giving unto Solomon timber of cedar, and timber of fir, even all his desire,
text.KJV: So Hiram gave Solomon cedar trees and fir trees according to all his desire.
text.NABRE: Solomon’s wisdom surpassed that of all the peoples of the East and all the wisdom of Egypt.
text.DRB: Therefore Hiram gave Salomon cedar trees, and fir trees, according to all his will.

text.NRSV: Solomon in turn gave Hiram twenty thousand cors of wheat as food for his household, and twenty cors of fine oil. Solomon gave this to Hiram year by year.
text.Rotherham: and, Solomon, gave unto Hiram, twenty thousand measures of wheat, as food for his household, and twenty measures of beaten oil,—thus, used Solomon on to give unto Hiram, year by year.
text.KJV: And Solomon gave Hiram twenty thousand measures of wheat for food to his household, and twenty measures of pure oil: thus gave Solomon to Hiram year by year.
text.NABRE: He was wiser than anyone else—wiser than Ethan the Ezrahite, or Heman, Chalcol, and Darda, the musicians—and his fame spread throughout the neighboring peoples.
text.DRB: And Salomon allowed Hiram twenty thousand cores of wheat, for provision for his house, and twenty cores of most pure oil: these things did Salomon give to Hiram every year.

text.NRSV: The Lord gave Solomon wisdom, as he promised him. There was peace between Hiram and Solomon; and the two of them made a treaty.
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, gave wisdom unto Solomon, as he promised him,—and it came to pass that there was peace between Hiram and Solomon, and, they two, solemnised a covenant.
text.KJV: And the Lord gave Solomon wisdom, as he promised him: and there was peace between Hiram and Solomon; and they two made a league together.
text.NABRE: Solomon also uttered three thousand proverbs, and his songs numbered a thousand and five.
text.DRB: Our Lord also gave wisdom to Salomon, as he spake to him: & there was peace between Hiram & Salomon, and both made a league.

text.NRSV: King Solomon conscripted forced labor out of all Israel; the levy numbered thirty thousand men.
text.Rotherham: And King Solomon raised a labour-band, out of all Israel,—and it came to pass that, the labour-band, was thirty thousand men.
text.KJV: And king Solomon raised a levy out of all Israel; and the levy was thirty thousand men.
text.NABRE: He spoke of plants, from the cedar on Lebanon to the hyssop growing out of the wall, and he spoke about beasts, birds, reptiles, and fishes.
text.DRB: And king Salomon chose workmen out of all Israel, and the taxed number was of thirty thousand men.

text.NRSV: He sent them to the Lebanon, ten thousand a month in shifts; they would be a month in the Lebanon and two months at home; Adoniram was in charge of the forced labor.
text.Rotherham: And he sent them to Lebanon, ten thousand a month, by courses, a month, were they in Lebanon, two months, at home,—and, Adoniram, was over the levy.
text.KJV: And he sent them to Lebanon, ten thousand a month by courses: a month they were in Lebanon, and two months at home: and Adoniram was over the levy.
text.NABRE: People from all nations came to hear Solomon’s wisdom, sent by all the kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom. Preparations for the Temple.
text.DRB: And he sent them into Libanus, ten thousand every month by course, so that two months they were in their houses: and Adoniram was over this taxing.

text.NRSV: Solomon also had seventy thousand laborers and eighty thousand stonecutters in the hill country,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, Solomon, had seventy thousand bearers of burdens,—and eighty thousand hewers in the mountain;
text.KJV: And Solomon had threescore and ten thousand that bare burdens, and fourscore thousand hewers in the mountains;
text.NABRE: When Hiram, king of Tyre, heard that Solomon had been anointed king in place of his father, he sent an embassy to him; for Hiram had always been David’s friend.
text.DRB: And Salomon had seventy thousand of them that carried burdens, and eighty thousand hewers of stones in the mountain:

text.NRSV: besides Solomon’s three thousand three hundred supervisors who were over the work, having charge of the people who did the work.
text.Rotherham: besides the chiefs of Solomon’s officers, who were over the work, three thousand and three hundred,—who ruled over the people that wrought in the work.
text.KJV: Beside the chief of Solomon’s officers which were over the work, three thousand and three hundred, which ruled over the people that wrought in the work.
text.NABRE: Solomon sent back this message to Hiram:
text.DRB: besides the overseers which were over every work, in number three thousand, and three hundred that commanded the people, & them that did the work.

text.NRSV: At the king’s command, they quarried out great, costly stones in order to lay the foundation of the house with dressed stones.
text.Rotherham: And the king commanded, and they quarried great stones, costly stones, to found the house with hewn stones.
text.KJV: And the king commanded, and they brought great stones, costly stones, and hewed stones, to lay the foundation of the house.
text.NABRE: “You know that David my father, because of the wars that beset him, could not build a house for the name of the Lord his God until such time as the Lord should put his enemies under the soles of his feet.
text.DRB: And the king commanded, that they should take great stones, chosen stones for the foundation of the temple, and should square them:

text.NRSV: So Solomon’s builders and Hiram’s builders and the Gebalites did the stonecutting and prepared the timber and the stone to build the house.
text.Rotherham: And Solomon’s builders and Hiram’s builders with the Gebalites wrought them,—thus made they ready the timber and the stones, for building the house.
text.KJV: And Solomon’s builders and Hiram’s builders did hew them, and the stonesquarers: so they prepared timber and stones to build the house.
text.NABRE: But now the Lord, my God, has given me rest on all sides, without adversary or misfortune.
text.DRB: which the masons of Salomon, and masons of Hiram hewed: moreover the Giblians prepared timber and stones, to build the house.

text.NRSV: In the four hundred eightieth year after the Israelites came out of the land of Egypt, in the fourth year of Solomon’s reign over Israel, in the month of Ziv, which is the second month, he began to build the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass, in the four hundred and eightieth year, by the coming forth of the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt, in the fourth year (in the month of Zif, the same, being the second month) of the reign of Solomon over Israel, that he began to build the house unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And it came to pass in the four hundred and eightieth year after the children of Israel were come out of the land of Egypt, in the fourth year of Solomon’s reign over Israel, in the month Zif, which is the second month, that he began to build the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 Building of the Temple. In the four hundred and eightieth year after the Israelites went forth from the land of Egypt, in the fourth year of Solomon’s reign over Israel, in the month of Ziv (the second month), he began to build the house of the Lord.
text.DRB: And it came to pass in the four hundred and four score year of the coming forth of the children of Israel out of the Land of Ægypt, in the fourth year, the month Zio (that is the second month) of the reign of Salomon over Israel, he began to build a house to our Lord.

text.NRSV: The house that King Solomon built for the Lord was sixty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and thirty cubits high.
text.Rotherham: Now, the house which King Solomon built unto Yahweh, was sixty cubits in length, and twenty in breadth,—and thirty cubits in height.
text.KJV: And the house which king Solomon built for the Lord, the length thereof was threescore cubits, and the breadth thereof twenty cubits, and the height thereof thirty cubits.
text.NABRE: The house which King Solomon built for the Lord was sixty cubits long, twenty wide, and thirty high.
text.DRB: And the house, which king Salomon built to our Lord, had three score cubits in length, & twenty cubits in breadth, and thirty cubits in height.

text.NRSV: The vestibule in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits wide, corresponding to the width of the house, and its depth was ten cubits in front of the house.
text.Rotherham: And, the porch in front of the temple of the house, was twenty cubits in length, according to the breadth of the house,—ten cubits in breadth, in front of the house.
text.KJV: And the porch before the temple of the house, twenty cubits was the length thereof, according to the breadth of the house; and ten cubits was the breadth thereof before the house.
text.NABRE: The porch in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits from side to side along the width of the house, and ten cubits deep in front of the house.
text.DRB: And there was a porch before the temple of twenty cubits of length, according to the measure of the breadth of the temple: and it had ten cubits of breadth before the face of the temple.

text.NRSV: For the house he made windows with recessed frames.
text.Rotherham: And he made to the house windows, with closed bars.
text.KJV: And for the house he made windows of narrow lights.
text.NABRE: Windows with closed lattices were made for the house,
text.DRB: And he made in the temple oblique windows.

text.NRSV: He also built a structure against the wall of the house, running around the walls of the house, both the nave and the inner sanctuary; and he made side chambers all around.
text.Rotherham: And he built—by the wall of the house—a gallery, round about, against the walls of the house, round about, both to the temple, and to the shrine,—and he made side chambers round about.
text.KJV: And against the wall of the house he built chambers round about, against the walls of the house round about, both of the temple and of the oracle: and he made chambers round about:
text.NABRE: and adjoining the wall of the house he built a substructure around its walls that enclosed the nave and the inner sanctuary, and he made side chambers all around.
text.DRB: And he built upon the wall of the temple lofts round about, in the walls of the house round about the temple and the oracle, and he made sides round about.

text.NRSV: The lowest story was five cubits wide, the middle one was six cubits wide, and the third was seven cubits wide; for around the outside of the house he made offsets on the wall in order that the supporting beams should not be inserted into the walls of the house.
text.Rotherham: The, lowest gallery, was five cubits broad, and, the middle, six cubits broad, and, the third, seven cubits broad,—for he put, ledges, against the house round about, on the outer side, so as not to make fastenings in the walls of the house.
text.KJV: The nethermost chamber was five cubits broad, and the middle was six cubits broad, and the third was seven cubits broad: for without in the wall of the house he made narrowed rests round about, that the beams should not be fastened in the walls of the house.
text.NABRE: The lowest story was five cubits wide, the middle one six cubits wide, the third seven cubits wide, because he put recesses along the outside of the house to avoid fastening anything into the walls of the house.
text.DRB: The loft that was underneath, had five cubits of breadth, & the middle loft was of six cubits in breadth, and the third loft had seven cubits of breadth. And he put beams in the house round about on the outside, that they might not cleave to the walls of the temple.

text.NRSV: The house was built with stone finished at the quarry, so that neither hammer nor ax nor any tool of iron was heard in the house while it was being built.
text.Rotherham: Now, the house, when it was in building, with whole quarry-stones, was built,—neither hammer, nor axe, nor any tools of iron, was heard in the house, when it was in building.
text.KJV: And the house, when it was in building, was built of stone made ready before it was brought thither: so that there was neither hammer nor axe nor any tool of iron heard in the house, while it was in building.
text.NABRE: The house was built of stone dressed at the quarry, so that no hammer or ax, no iron tool, was to be heard in the house during its construction.
text.DRB: And the house when it was built, was built of stones hewed and perfected: and hammer, and hatchet, and all the tool of iron were not heard in the house when it was built.

text.NRSV: The entrance for the middle story was on the south side of the house: one went up by winding stairs to the middle story, and from the middle story to the third.
text.Rotherham: The entrance of the lowest story, was on the right side of the house,—and, by winding stairs, went they up unto the middle [story], and, out of the middle, into, the third.
text.KJV: The door for the middle chamber was in the right side of the house: and they went up with winding stairs into the middle chamber, and out of the middle into the third.
text.NABRE: The entrance to the middle story was on the south side of the house; stairs led up to the middle story and from the middle story to the third.
text.DRB: The door of the middle side was in the wall of the house on the right hand: and by winding stairs they went up into the middle room, and from the middle into the third.

text.NRSV: So he built the house, and finished it; he roofed the house with beams and planks of cedar.
text.Rotherham: So he built the house, and finished it,—and ceiled the house with planks and beams of cedar;
text.KJV: So he built the house, and finished it; and covered the house with beams and boards of cedar.
text.NABRE: When he had finished building the house, it was roofed in with rafters and boards of cedar.
text.DRB: And he built the house, and finished it: he covered also the house with ceilings of cedar trees.

text.NRSV: He built the structure against the whole house, each story five cubits high, and it was joined to the house with timbers of cedar.
text.Rotherham: and built the gallery against all the house, five cubits in height,—and so fastened the house, with timber of cedar.
text.KJV: And then he built chambers against all the house, five cubits high: and they rested on the house with timber of cedar.
text.NABRE: He built the substructure five cubits high all along the outside of the house, to which it was joined by cedar beams.
text.DRB: And he built a loft over all the house five cubits of height, and he covered the house with cedar timber.

text.NRSV: Now the word of the Lord came to Solomon,
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto Solomon, saying:
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord came to Solomon, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to Solomon:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord came to Salomon, saying:

text.NRSV: “Concerning this house that you are building, if you will walk in my statutes, obey my ordinances, and keep all my commandments by walking in them, then I will establish my promise with you, which I made to your father David.
text.Rotherham: Concerning this house, which thou art building, If thou wilt walk in my statutes, and, my regulations, wilt practise, and so observe all my commandments, by walking in them, Then will I establish my word with thee, which I spake unto David thy father;
text.KJV: Concerning this house which thou art in building, if thou wilt walk in my statutes, and execute my judgments, and keep all my commandments to walk in them; then will I perform my word with thee, which I spake unto David thy father:
text.NABRE: As to this house you are building—if you walk in my statutes, carry out my ordinances, and observe all my commands, walking in them, I will fulfill toward you my word which I spoke to David your father.
text.DRB: This house, which thou buildest, if thou wilt walk in my precepts, and to my judgments, and keep all my commandments, going in them, I will establish my word to thee, which I spake to David thy father.

text.NRSV: I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will not forsake my people Israel.”
text.Rotherham: and will make my habitation in the midst of the sons of Israel,—and will not forsake my people Israel.
text.KJV: And I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will not forsake my people Israel.
text.NABRE: I will dwell in the midst of the Israelites and will not forsake my people Israel.
text.DRB: And I will dwell in the midst of the children of Israel, and will not forsake my people Israel.

text.NRSV: So Solomon built the house, and finished it.
text.Rotherham: Thus Solomon built the house, and finished it;
text.KJV: So Solomon built the house, and finished it.
text.NABRE: When Solomon finished building the house,
text.DRB: Salomon therefore built the house, and finished it.

text.NRSV: He lined the walls of the house on the inside with boards of cedar; from the floor of the house to the rafters of the ceiling, he covered them on the inside with wood; and he covered the floor of the house with boards of cypress.
text.Rotherham: and he built the walls of the house on the inside, with boards of cedar, from the floor of the house, unto the cross-beams of the ceiling, he overlaid it with wood, on the inside,—and he overlaid the floor of the house with boards of fir.
text.KJV: And he built the walls of the house within with boards of cedar, both the floor of the house, and the walls of the cieling: and he covered them on the inside with wood, and covered the floor of the house with planks of fir.
text.NABRE: its inside walls were lined with cedar paneling: he covered the interior with wood from floor to ceiling, and he covered its floor with fir planking.
text.DRB: And he built the walls of the house on the inside, with cedar lofts, from the pavement of the house to the top of the walls, and to the roofs, he covered it with cedar trees on the inside: and he covered the floor of the house with boards of fir.

text.NRSV: He built twenty cubits of the rear of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the rafters, and he built this within as an inner sanctuary, as the most holy place.
text.Rotherham: And he built twenty cubits, at the hinder part of the house, with boards of cedar, from the floor as far as the cross-beams,—and he built it within, for a shrine, even for the holy of holies;
text.KJV: And he built twenty cubits on the sides of the house, both the floor and the walls with boards of cedar: he even built them for it within, even for the oracle, even for the most holy place.
text.NABRE: At the rear of the house a space of twenty cubits was set off by cedar panels from the floor to the ceiling, enclosing the inner sanctuary, the holy of holies.
text.DRB: And he built lofts of cedar timber of twenty cubits at the hinder part of the temple, from the pavement to the higher parts: and he made the inner house of the oracle to be Sanctum Sanctorum.

text.NRSV: The house, that is, the nave in front of the inner sanctuary, was forty cubits long.
text.Rotherham: but, forty cubits, was the house itself,—the same, was the temple, before the oracle.
text.KJV: And the house, that is, the temple before it, was forty cubits long.
text.NABRE: The house was forty cubits long, that is, the nave, the part in front.
text.DRB: Moreover the temple itself was forty cubits before the doors of the oracle.

text.NRSV: The cedar within the house was carved with gourds and open flowers; all was cedar, no stone was seen.
text.Rotherham: And, the cedar for the house within, was carved with colocynths, and with festoons of flowers,—the whole, was cedar, there was no stone to be seen.
text.KJV: And the cedar of the house within was carved with knops and open flowers: all was cedar; there was no stone seen.
text.NABRE: The cedar in the interior of the house was carved in the form of gourds and open flowers; all was of cedar, and no stone was to be seen.
text.DRB: And all the house was covered within with cedar, having roundels, and the joints thereof cunningly wrought, and the engravings standing out: all things were covered with boards: neither could there a stone appear in the wall at all.

text.NRSV: The inner sanctuary he prepared in the innermost part of the house, to set there the ark of the covenant of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, the shrine in the midst of the house within, made he ready,—for placing there, the ark of the covenant of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the oracle he prepared in the house within, to set there the ark of the covenant of the Lord.
text.NABRE: In the innermost part of the house he set up the inner sanctuary to house the ark of the Lord ’s covenant.
text.DRB: And he made the oracle in the midst of the house, in the inner part, that he might put the ark of covenant of our Lord there.

text.NRSV: The interior of the inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and twenty cubits high; he overlaid it with pure gold. He also overlaid the altar with cedar.
text.Rotherham: And, the interior of the shrine, was twenty cubits in length, and twenty cubits in breadth, and twenty cubits in height, and he overlaid it, with pure gold,—and overlaid the altar with cedar.
text.KJV: And the oracle in the forepart was twenty cubits in length, and twenty cubits in breadth, and twenty cubits in the height thereof: and he overlaid it with pure gold; and so covered the altar which was of cedar.
text.NABRE: In front of the inner sanctuary (it was twenty cubits long, twenty wide, and twenty high, and he covered it with pure gold), he made an altar of cedar.
text.DRB: Moreover the oracle had twenty cubits in length, and twenty cubits of breadth, and twenty cubits in height. And he covered and sealed it with most pure gold. And the altar also he decked with cedar.

text.NRSV: Solomon overlaid the inside of the house with pure gold, then he drew chains of gold across, in front of the inner sanctuary, and overlaid it with gold.
text.Rotherham: Thus then did Solomon overlay the house within, with pure gold,—and he drew chains of gold across the front of the shrine, and overlaid it with gold;
text.KJV: So Solomon overlaid the house within with pure gold: and he made a partition by the chains of gold before the oracle; and he overlaid it with gold.
text.NABRE: Solomon covered the interior of the house with pure gold, and he drew golden chains across in front of the inner sanctuary, and covered it with gold.
text.DRB: The house also before the oracle he covered with most pure gold, and fastened on plates with nails of gold.

text.NRSV: Next he overlaid the whole house with gold, in order that the whole house might be perfect; even the whole altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary he overlaid with gold.
text.Rotherham: yea, all the house, overlaid he with gold, until he had made all the house into one whole,—even all the altar that was by the shrine, overlaid he with gold.
text.KJV: And the whole house he overlaid with gold, until he had finished all the house: also the whole altar that was by the oracle he overlaid with gold.
text.NABRE: He covered the whole house with gold, until the whole house was done, and the whole altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary he covered with gold.
text.DRB: And there was nothing in the temple that was not covered with gold: yea and all the altar of the oracle he covered with gold.

text.NRSV: In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olivewood, each ten cubits high.
text.Rotherham: And he made, within the shrine, two cherubim of wild olive wood,—ten cubits in height;
text.KJV: And within the oracle he made two cherubims of olive tree, each ten cubits high.
text.NABRE: In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim, each ten cubits high, made of pine.
text.DRB: And he made in the oracle two Cherubs of olive trees, of ten cubits in height.

text.NRSV: Five cubits was the length of one wing of the cherub, and five cubits the length of the other wing of the cherub; it was ten cubits from the tip of one wing to the tip of the other.
text.Rotherham: and, five cubits, was the one wing of the cherub, and, five cubits, the second wing of the cherub,—ten cubits, from the one end of his wings unto the other end of his wings.
text.KJV: And five cubits was the one wing of the cherub, and five cubits the other wing of the cherub: from the uttermost part of the one wing unto the uttermost part of the other were ten cubits.
text.NABRE: Each wing of a cherub was five cubits so that the span from wing tip to wing tip was ten cubits.
text.DRB: One wing of a cherub of five cubits, and the other wing of a cherub five cubits: that is, having ten cubits, from the end of one wing unto the end of the other wing.

text.NRSV: The other cherub also measured ten cubits; both cherubim had the same measure and the same form.
text.Rotherham: Ten cubits, also was the second cherub,—of one size and one shape, were the two cherubim.
text.KJV: And the other cherub was ten cubits: both the cherubims were of one measure and one size.
text.NABRE: The second cherub was also ten cubits: the two cherubim were identical in size and shape;
text.DRB: Of ten cubits also was the second cherub: in like measure, and the work was one in both cherubs,

text.NRSV: The height of one cherub was ten cubits, and so was that of the other cherub.
text.Rotherham: The height of the one cherub, was ten cubits,—and, so, of the second cherub.
text.KJV: The height of the one cherub was ten cubits, and so was it of the other cherub.
text.NABRE: the first cherub was ten cubits high, and so was the second.
text.DRB: that is to say, one cherub had the height of ten cubits, and in like manner the second cherub.

text.NRSV: He put the cherubim in the innermost part of the house; the wings of the cherubim were spread out so that a wing of one was touching one wall, and a wing of the other cherub was touching the other wall; their other wings met in the center of the house, wing to wing.
text.Rotherham: And he placed the cherubim in the midst of the inner house, and the cherubim stretched forth their wings,—so that the wing of the one touched the [one] wall, and the wing of the other cherub, was touching the other wall,—and, their wings in the midst of the house, were touching, wing to wing.
text.KJV: And he set the cherubims within the inner house: and they stretched forth the wings of the cherubims, so that the wing of the one touched the one wall, and the wing of the other cherub touched the other wall; and their wings touched one another in the midst of the house.
text.NABRE: He placed the cherubim in the inmost part of the house; the wings of the cherubim were spread wide, so that one wing of the first touched the side wall and the wing of the second touched the other wall; the wings pointing to the middle of the room touched each other.
text.DRB: And he put the cherubs in the midst of the inner temple: and the cherubs extended their wings, and the one wing touched the wall, and the wing of the second cherub touched the other wall: and the other wings in the middle part of the temple touched each other.

text.NRSV: He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
text.Rotherham: And he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
