text.KJV: And he overlaid the cherubims with gold.
text.NABRE: He overlaid the cherubim with gold.
text.DRB: He covered also the cherubs with gold.

text.NRSV: He carved the walls of the house all around about with carved engravings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, in the inner and outer rooms.
text.Rotherham: And, all the walls of the house round about, carved he with figures of cherubim, and palm-trees, and festoons of flowers,—within and without.
text.KJV: And he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved figures of cherubims and palm trees and open flowers, within and without.
text.NABRE: The walls of the house on all sides of both the inner and the outer rooms had carved figures of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers.
text.DRB: And all the walls of the temple round about he graved with diverse engravings and carving: & he made in them cherubs, and palm trees, and diverse pictures, as it were standing out of the wall, and coming forth.

text.NRSV: The floor of the house he overlaid with gold, in the inner and outer rooms.
text.Rotherham: Even the floor of the house, overlaid he with gold,—within and without.
text.KJV: And the floor of the house he overlaid with gold, within and without.
text.NABRE: The floor of the house of both the inner and the outer rooms was overlaid with gold.
text.DRB: Yea the pavement also of the house he covered with gold within and without.

text.NRSV: For the entrance to the inner sanctuary he made doors of olivewood; the lintel and the doorposts were five-sided.
text.Rotherham: And, the entrance of the shrine, made he with doors, of wild olive-wood, the pillars and posts being a fifth.
text.KJV: And for the entering of the oracle he made doors of olive tree: the lintel and side posts were a fifth part of the wall.
text.NABRE: At the entrance of the inner sanctuary, doors of pine were made; the doorframes had five-sided posts.
text.DRB: And in the entrance of the oracle he made little doors of the timber of olivetrees, and five corner posts.

text.NRSV: He covered the two doors of olivewood with carvings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers; he overlaid them with gold, and spread gold on the cherubim and on the palm trees.
text.Rotherham: The two doors, also were of olive-wood, and he carved upon them carvings of cherubim, and palm-trees, and festoons of flowers, and overlaid them with gold,—yea he spread out, over the cherubim and over the palm-trees, the gold.
text.KJV: The two doors also were of olive tree; and he carved upon them carvings of cherubims and palm trees and open flowers, and overlaid them with gold, and spread gold upon the cherubims, and upon the palm trees.
text.NABRE: The two doors were of pine, with carved figures of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers. The doors were overlaid with gold, and the cherubim and the palm trees were also covered with beaten gold.
text.DRB: And two doors of olivetimber: and he graved in them pictures of Cherubs, and figures of Palm trees, and graven works standing out very much; and he covered them with gold: and he covered as well the Cherubs and the palmtrees, and the other things with gold.

text.NRSV: So also he made for the entrance to the nave doorposts of olivewood, four-sided,
text.Rotherham: So, also, made he for the entrance of the temple, with posts of wild olive-wood,—a fourth part.
text.KJV: So also made he for the door of the temple posts of olive tree, a fourth part of the wall.
text.NABRE: He did the same at the entrance to the nave, where the doorposts were of pine and were four-sided.
text.DRB: And he made in the entrance of the temple posts of olivetimber four square:

text.NRSV: and two doors of cypress wood; the two leaves of the one door were folding, and the two leaves of the other door were folding.
text.Rotherham: And, the two doors, were of fir wood,—the two leaves of the one door, were folding, and, the two leaves of the other door, were folding.
text.KJV: And the two doors were of fir tree: the two leaves of the one door were folding, and the two leaves of the other door were folding.
text.NABRE: The two doors were of fir wood, each door consisting of two panels hinged together;
text.DRB: and two doors of fir trees, one against another: and either door was double, & so opened with folding leaves.

text.NRSV: He carved cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, overlaying them with gold evenly applied upon the carved work.
text.Rotherham: And he carved cherubim, and palm-trees, and festoons of flowers, and overlaid them with gold, smoothed out on the carved work.
text.KJV: And he carved thereon cherubims and palm trees and open flowers: and covered them with gold fitted upon the carved work.
text.NABRE: and he carved cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, and plated them with gold.
text.DRB: And he graved Cherubs, and palmtrees, and engravings appearing very much: and he covered all with golden plates in square work by rule.

text.NRSV: He built the inner court with three courses of dressed stone to one course of cedar beams.
text.Rotherham: And he built the inner court, with three rows of hewn stone,—and a row of beams of cedar.
text.KJV: And he built the inner court with three rows of hewed stone, and a row of cedar beams.
text.NABRE: He walled off the inner court with three courses of hewn stones and one course of cedar beams.
text.DRB: And he built the inner court with three rows of stones polished, & one row of cedar timber.

text.NRSV: In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the Lord was laid, in the month of Ziv.
text.Rotherham: In the fourth year, was laid the foundation of the house of Yahweh,—in the month Zif;
text.KJV: In the fourth year was the foundation of the house of the Lord laid, in the month Zif:
text.NABRE: The foundations of the Lord ’s house were laid in the month of Ziv in the fourth year,
text.DRB: In the fourth year was the house of our Lord founded in the month of Zio:

text.NRSV: In the eleventh year, in the month of Bul, which is the eighth month, the house was finished in all its parts, and according to all its specifications. He was seven years in building it.
text.Rotherham: and, in the eleventh year, in the month Bul—the same, is the eighth month, was the house finished, as to all the particulars thereof, and as to all the appointments thereof,—so then he built it in seven years.
text.KJV: And in the eleventh year, in the month Bul, which is the eighth month, was the house finished throughout all the parts thereof, and according to all the fashion of it. So was he seven years in building it.
text.NABRE: and it was finished, in all particulars, exactly according to plan, in the month of Bul, the eighth month, in the eleventh year. Thus Solomon built it in seven years.
text.DRB: and in the eleventh year in the month Bul (that is the eighth month) the house was perfected in all the works thereof, and in all the implements thereof: and he was building it seven years.

text.NRSV: Solomon was building his own house thirteen years, and he finished his entire house.
text.Rotherham: But, his own house, was Solomon thirteen years in building,—so he finished all his house.
text.KJV: But Solomon was building his own house thirteen years, and he finished all his house.
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 To finish the building of his own house Solomon took thirteen years.
text.DRB: And his own house Salomon built in thirteen years, and brought it to perfection.

text.NRSV: He built the House of the Forest of the Lebanon one hundred cubits long, fifty cubits wide, and thirty cubits high, built on four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams on the pillars.
text.Rotherham: Yea he built the house of the forest of Lebanon, a hundred cubits, the length thereof, and, fifty cubits, the breadth thereof, and, thirty cubits, the height thereof,—upon four rows of pillars of cedar, with beams of cedar, upon the pillars;
text.KJV: He built also the house of the forest of Lebanon; the length thereof was an hundred cubits, and the breadth thereof fifty cubits, and the height thereof thirty cubits, upon four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams upon the pillars.
text.NABRE: He built the House of the Forest of Lebanon one hundred cubits long, fifty wide, and thirty high; it was supported by four rows of cedar columns, with cedar beams upon the columns.
text.DRB: He built also the house of the forest of Libanus of an hundred cubits in length, & fifty cubits in breadth, and thirty cubits in height: and four galleries between pillars of cedar: for he had cut cedar trees into pillars.

text.NRSV: It was roofed with cedar on the forty-five rafters, fifteen in each row, which were on the pillars.
text.Rotherham: and it was covered with cedar above upon the joists, that were on forty-five pillars,—fifteen in a row.
text.KJV: And it was covered with cedar above upon the beams, that lay on forty five pillars, fifteen in a row.
text.NABRE: Moreover, it had a ceiling of cedar above the rafters resting on the columns; these rafters numbered forty-five, fifteen to a row.
text.DRB: And he decked the whole vault with boards of cedar, which was held up with five & forty pillars, And one order had fifteen pillars,

text.NRSV: There were window frames in three rows, facing each other in the three rows.
text.Rotherham: And there were, window spaces, in three rows,—and light over against light, three times.
text.KJV: And there were windows in three rows, and light was against light in three ranks.
text.NABRE: There were lattices in three rows, each row facing the next,
text.DRB: set one against another,

text.NRSV: All the doorways and windows had square frames, and they faced each other in three rows.
text.Rotherham: And, all the openings and the posts, were square in their frame,—and light was over against light, three times.
text.KJV: And all the doors and posts were square, with the windows: and light was against light in three ranks.
text.NABRE: and all the openings and doorposts were squared with lintels, each facing across from the next.
text.DRB: and looked one over against another, with equal space between the pillars, & over the pillars square beams in all equal.

text.NRSV: He made the Hall of Pillars fifty cubits long and thirty cubits wide. There was a porch in front with pillars, and a canopy in front of them.
text.Rotherham: And, a porch of pillars, made he, fifty cubits, in length, and, thirty cubits, in breadth,—and, a porch, was on the one front of them, and pillars and threshold, were on the other front of them.
text.KJV: And he made a porch of pillars; the length thereof was fifty cubits, and the breadth thereof thirty cubits: and the porch was before them: and the other pillars and the thick beam were before them.
text.NABRE: He also made the Porch of Columns, fifty cubits long and thirty wide. The porch extended across the front, and there were columns with a canopy in front of them.
text.DRB: And the porch of the pillars he made of fifty cubits in length, and thirty cubits in breadth: and another porch before the greater porch: and pillars, and tops upon the pillars.

text.NRSV: He made the Hall of the Throne where he was to pronounce judgment, the Hall of Justice, paneled with cedar from floor to floor.
text.Rotherham: And, a porch for the throne, where he should judge, even the porch of judgment, made he, and it was wainscotted with cedar, from floor to ceiling.
text.KJV: Then he made a porch for the throne where he might judge, even the porch of judgment: and it was covered with cedar from one side of the floor to the other.
text.NABRE: He also made the Porch of the Throne where he gave judgment—that is, the Porch of Judgment; it was paneled with cedar from floor to ceiling beams.
text.DRB: He made also the porch of the throne, wherein the seat of judgment is; and covered it with cedar wood from the pavement unto the top.

text.NRSV: His own house where he did live, in the other court back of the hall, was of like workmanship. Solomon also made a house like this hall for Pharaoh’s daughter, whom he had taken in marriage.
text.Rotherham: And, his own house where he should dwell, in the other court within the porch, was, of like workmanship unto this,—a house also, made he, for the daughter of Pharaoh, whom Solomon had taken [to wife], in a similar porch.
text.KJV: And his house where he dwelt had another court within the porch, which was of the like work. Solomon made also an house for Pharaoh’s daughter, whom he had taken to wife, like unto this porch.
text.NABRE: The house in which he lived was in another court, set in deeper than the Porch and of the same construction. (Solomon made a house like this Porch for Pharaoh’s daughter, whom he had married.)
text.DRB: And the little house, where they sat in judgment, was in the midst of the porch of like work. He made also a house for the daughter of Pharao (which Salomon had taken to wife) of such work, as also this porch.

text.NRSV: All these were made of costly stones, cut according to measure, sawed with saws, back and front, from the foundation to the coping, and from outside to the great court.
text.Rotherham: All these, were of costly stones, after the dimensions of hewn stones, sawn with saws, within and without,—even from the foundation, unto the coping, and without as far as the great court.
text.KJV: All these were of costly stones, according to the measures of hewed stones, sawed with saws, within and without, even from the foundation unto the coping, and so on the outside toward the great court.
text.NABRE: All these buildings were of fine stones, hewn to size and trimmed front and back with a saw, from the foundation to the bonding course and outside as far as the great court.
text.DRB: All of chosen stones, which were sawed by a certain rule and measure both within and without: from the foundation to the top of the walls, and without unto the greater court.

text.NRSV: The foundation was of costly stones, huge stones, stones of eight and ten cubits.
text.Rotherham: And, the foundation, was of costly stones, large stones, stones of ten cubits, and stones of eight cubits.
text.KJV: And the foundation was of costly stones, even great stones, stones of ten cubits, and stones of eight cubits.
text.NABRE: The foundation was made of fine, large blocks, some ten cubits and some eight cubits.
text.DRB: And the foundations of chosen stones, great stones of ten or eight cubits.

text.NRSV: Above were costly stones, cut according to measure, and cedar.
text.Rotherham: And, above, were costly stones, after the dimensions of hewn stone, and cedar.
text.KJV: And above were costly stones, after the measures of hewed stones, and cedars.
text.NABRE: Above were fine stones hewn to size, and cedar wood.
text.DRB: And above there were hewed chosen stones of equal measure, and in like manner of cedar.

text.NRSV: The great court had three courses of dressed stone to one course of cedar beams all around; so had the inner court of the house of the Lord, and the vestibule of the house.
text.Rotherham: And, the great court round about, was of three rows of hewn stone, and a row of beams of cedar,—both to the inner court of the house of Yahweh, and to the porch of the house.
text.KJV: And the great court round about was with three rows of hewed stones, and a row of cedar beams, both for the inner court of the house of the Lord, and for the porch of the house.
text.NABRE: The great court had three courses of hewn stones all around and a course of cedar beams. So also were the inner court of the house of the Lord and its porch.
text.DRB: And the greater court round with three rews of hewed stones, and one rew of planed cedar, moreover also in the inner court of the house of our Lord, and in the porch of the house.

text.NRSV: Now King Solomon sent and brought Hiram from Tyre.
text.Rotherham: And King Solomon sent and fetched Hiram, out of Tyre:
text.KJV: And king Solomon sent and fetched Hiram out of Tyre.
text.NABRE: King Solomon brought Hiram from Tyre.
text.DRB: King Salomon also sent, and took Hiram from Tyre,

text.NRSV: He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali, whose father, a Tyrian, had been a worker in bronze; he was full of skill, intelligence, and knowledge in working any work in bronze. He came to King Solomon, and did all his work.
text.Rotherham: son of a widow woman, was he of the tribe of Naphtali, but, his father, was a man of Tyre, a worker in bronze, and he was filled with wisdom, and discernment, and knowledge, for executing all manner of work in bronze,—so he came unto King Solomon, and executed all his work.
text.KJV: He was a widow’s son of the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a worker in brass: and he was filled with wisdom, and understanding, and cunning to work all works in brass. And he came to king Solomon, and wrought all his work.
text.NABRE: He was a bronze worker, the son of a widow from the tribe of Naphtali; his father had been from Tyre. He was endowed with wisdom, understanding, and knowledge for doing any work in bronze. He came to King Solomon and did all his metal work.
text.DRB: the son of a widow woman of the tribe of Nephthali, his father, a Tyrian, an artificer in brass, and full of wisdom, and intelligence, and skill to make all work of brass. Who when he was come to king Salomon, made all his work.

text.NRSV: He cast two pillars of bronze. Eighteen cubits was the height of the one, and a cord of twelve cubits would encircle it; the second pillar was the same.
text.Rotherham: And he cast two pillars of bronze,—eighteen cubits, the height of each pillar, and, a line of twelve cubits, would surround the second pillar.
text.KJV: For he cast two pillars of brass, of eighteen cubits high apiece: and a line of twelve cubits did compass either of them about.
text.NABRE: He fashioned two bronze columns, each eighteen cubits high and twelve cubits in circumference.
text.DRB: And he cast two brazen pillars, of eighteen cubits in height one pillar: and a line of twelve cubits compassed both pillars.

text.NRSV: He also made two capitals of molten bronze, to set on the tops of the pillars; the height of the one capital was five cubits, and the height of the other capital was five cubits.
text.Rotherham: And, two capitals, made he, to set upon the tops of the pillars, of molten bronze,—five cubits, was the height of the one capital, and, five cubits, the height of the other capital;
text.KJV: And he made two chapiters of molten brass, to set upon the tops of the pillars: the height of the one chapiter was five cubits, and the height of the other chapiter was five cubits:
text.NABRE: He also made two capitals cast in bronze, to be placed on top of the columns, each of them five cubits high.
text.DRB: He made also two little heads, which should be put upon the heads of the pillars, cast of brass: five cubits high one little head, and five cubits the other little head:

text.NRSV: There were nets of checkerwork with wreaths of chainwork for the capitals on the tops of the pillars; seven for the one capital, and seven for the other capital.
text.Rotherham: with a frame of checker work, wreaths of chain work, for the capitals which were upon the top of the pillars,—seven for the one capital, and seven for the other capital.
text.KJV: And nets of checker work, and wreaths of chain work, for the chapiters which were upon the top of the pillars; seven for the one chapiter, and seven for the other chapiter.
text.NABRE: There were meshes made like netting and braid made like chains for the capitals on top of the columns, seven for each capital.
text.DRB: and as it were in manner of a net, & of chains knit one to the other with marvelous work. Both little heads of the pillars were cast: seven rews of little nets in one little head, & seven little nets in the other little head.

text.NRSV: He made the medium-sized pomegranates; there were two rows of them around each latticework to cover the capitals that were on the top of the pillars; he did the same with the other capital.
text.Rotherham: So he made the pillars,—and, two rows round about, upon the one frame of checker work, covering the capitals which were upon the top with pomegranates, thus also, did he for the other capital.
text.KJV: And he made the pillars, and two rows round about upon the one network, to cover the chapiters that were upon the top, with pomegranates: and so did he for the other chapiter.
text.NABRE: He also cast pomegranates, two rows around each netting to cover the capital on top of the columns.
text.DRB: And finished the pillars, and two rews round about every net, that they might cover the little heads, which were over the top of the pomegranates: in like manner did he also to the second little head.

text.NRSV: Now the capitals that were on the tops of the pillars in the vestibule were of lily-work, four cubits high.
text.Rotherham: And, the capitals which were upon the top of the pillars, were of lily work, in span, four cubits.
text.KJV: And the chapiters that were upon the top of the pillars were of lily work in the porch, four cubits.
text.NABRE: The capitals on top of the columns (in the porch) were made like lilies, four cubits high.
text.DRB: And the little heads, that were upon the heads of the pillars, were made as it were with lily work, in the porch, of four cubits.

text.NRSV: The capitals were on the two pillars and also above the rounded projection which was beside the latticework; there were two hundred pomegranates in rows all around on each capital.
text.Rotherham: And there were capitals upon the two pillars, above also, close by the belly, which was beside the checker work,—and there were two hundred pomegranates, in rows round about, upon either capital.
text.KJV: And the chapiters upon the two pillars had pomegranates also above, over against the belly which was by the network: and the pomegranates were two hundred in rows round about upon the other chapiter.
text.NABRE: And the capitals on the two columns, both above and adjoining the bulge where it crossed out of the netting, had two hundred pomegranates in rows around each capital.
text.DRB: And again other little heads in the top of the pillars above, according to the measure of the pillar against the little nets: and of the pomegranates were two hundred rews round about the second little head.

text.NRSV: He set up the pillars at the vestibule of the temple; he set up the pillar on the right and called it Jachin; and he set up the pillar on the left and called it Boaz.
text.Rotherham: So he reared the pillars for the porch of the temple,—yea he reared the right pillar, and called the name thereof Jachin, and reared the left pillar, and called the name thereof, Boaz.
text.KJV: And he set up the pillars in the porch of the temple: and he set up the right pillar, and called the name thereof Jachin: and he set up the left pillar, and called the name thereof Boaz.
text.NABRE: He set up the columns at the temple porch; one he set up to the south, and called it Jachin, and the other to the north, and called it Boaz.
text.DRB: And he set two pillars in the porch of the temple: & when he had erected the pillar on the right hand, he called the name thereof Iachin: in like manner he erected the second pillar, & called the name thereof Booz.

text.NRSV: On the tops of the pillars was lily-work. Thus the work of the pillars was finished.
text.Rotherham: And, upon the top of the pillars, was lily work,—which gave finish to the work of the pillars.
text.KJV: And upon the top of the pillars was lily work: so was the work of the pillars finished.
text.NABRE: The top of the columns was made like a lily. Thus the work on the columns was completed.
text.DRB: And upon the heads of the pillars he put a work in manner of a lily: and the work of the pillars was perfected.

text.NRSV: Then he made the molten sea; it was round, ten cubits from brim to brim, and five cubits high. A line of thirty cubits would encircle it completely.
text.Rotherham: And he made a molten sea,—ten cubits from the one brim to the other, it was round all about, and, five cubits, was the height thereof, and, a line of thirty cubits, would encompass it round about.
text.KJV: And he made a molten sea, ten cubits from the one brim to the other: it was round all about, and his height was five cubits: and a line of thirty cubits did compass it round about.
text.NABRE: Then he made the molten sea; it was made with a circular rim, and measured ten cubits across, five in height, and thirty in circumference.
text.DRB: He made also a sea of founder's work, of ten cubits from brim to brim, round in compass, the height thereof was of five cubits, and a cord of thirty cubits did compass it round about.

text.NRSV: Under its brim were panels all around, ten to a cubit, surrounding the sea everywhere; the panels were in two rows, cast when it was cast.
text.Rotherham: And, colocynths, were under the brim thereof round about, encompassing it, ten in a cubit, encircling the sea, round about,—two rows of colocynths, made in the casting thereof:
text.KJV: And under the brim of it round about there were knops compassing it, ten in a cubit, compassing the sea round about: the knops were cast in two rows, when it was cast.
text.NABRE: Under the brim, gourds encircled it for ten cubits around the compass of the sea; the gourds were in two rows and were cast in one mold with the sea.
text.DRB: And the graving under the brim compassed it, ten cubits going about the lavatory: there were two rews of chamfered furrowed gravings cast.

text.NRSV: It stood on twelve oxen, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south, and three facing east; the sea was set on them. The hindquarters of each were toward the inside.
text.Rotherham: standing upon twelve oxen, three looking northward, and three looking westward, and three looking southward, and three looking eastward, the sea, being upon them above,—all their hinder parts, being, inward.
text.KJV: It stood upon twelve oxen, three looking toward the north, and three looking toward the west, and three looking toward the south, and three looking toward the east: and the sea was set above upon them, and all their hinder parts were inward.
text.NABRE: This rested on twelve oxen, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south, and three facing east, with their haunches all toward the center; upon them was set the sea.
text.DRB: And it stood upon twelve oxen, of which three looked to the North, and three to the West, and three to the South, and three to the East, and the sea was over them: whose hinder parts were all hid inward.

text.NRSV: Its thickness was a handbreadth; its brim was made like the brim of a cup, like the flower of a lily; it held two thousand baths.
text.Rotherham: And, the thickness thereof, was a hand breadth, and, the brim thereof, was like the brim-work of a cup, of lily-blossoms,—two thousand baths, did it contain.
text.KJV: And it was an hand breadth thick, and the brim thereof was wrought like the brim of a cup, with flowers of lilies: it contained two thousand baths.
text.NABRE: It was a handbreadth thick, and its brim resembled that of a cup, being lily-shaped. Its capacity was two thousand baths.
text.DRB: And the thickness of the lavatory was of three ounces: and the brim thereof as it were the brim of a chalice, and the leaf of crisped lily: it contained two thousand bates.

text.NRSV: He also made the ten stands of bronze; each stand was four cubits long, four cubits wide, and three cubits high.
text.Rotherham: And he made ten stands of bronze,—four cubits, the length of each stand, and, four cubits, the breadth thereof, and, three cubits, the height thereof.
text.KJV: And he made ten bases of brass; four cubits was the length of one base, and four cubits the breadth thereof, and three cubits the height of it.
text.NABRE: He also made ten stands of bronze, each four cubits long, four wide, and three high.
text.DRB: And he made ten brazen feet, of four cubits in length every foot, and four cubits in breadth, and three cubits in height.

text.NRSV: This was the construction of the stands: they had panels, and the panels were between the frames.
text.Rotherham: And, this, was the work of the stand, there were, side walls, to them,—and, the side-walls, were between joining ledges;
text.KJV: And the work of the bases was on this manner: they had borders, and the borders were between the ledges:
text.NABRE: When these stands were constructed, panels were set within the framework.
text.DRB: And the very work itself of the feet, was entergraven: and entergravings between the jointures.

text.NRSV: On the panels that were between the frames were lions, oxen, and cherubim; on the frames, both above and below the lions and oxen, there were wreaths of beveled work.
text.Rotherham: and, upon the side-walls which were between the ledges, were lions, oxen, and cherubim, and, upon the ledges, was a pedestal above, and, beneath the lions and oxen, a wreath of hanging work.
text.KJV: And on the borders that were between the ledges were lions, oxen, and cherubims: and upon the ledges there was a base above: and beneath the lions and oxen were certain additions made of thin work.
text.NABRE: On the panels within the frames there were lions, oxen, and cherubim; and on the frames likewise, above and below the lions and oxen, there were wreaths in hammered relief.
text.DRB: And between the little crowns and the plaits, lions, and oxen, and Cherubs: and in the jointures likewise above: and under the lions & oxen as it were bands of brass hanging down.

text.NRSV: Each stand had four bronze wheels and axles of bronze; at the four corners were supports for a basin. The supports were cast with wreaths at the side of each.
text.Rotherham: And, four wheels of bronze, had each stand, with axles of bronze, and, the four steps thereof, had shoulder pieces,—under the laver, were the molten shoulder-pieces, over against each wreath.
text.KJV: And every base had four brasen wheels, and plates of brass: and the four corners thereof had undersetters: under the laver were undersetters molten, at the side of every addition.
text.NABRE: Each stand had four bronze wheels and bronze axles. The four legs of each stand had cast braces, which were under the basin; they had wreaths on each side.
text.DRB: And four wheels at every foot, and axletrees of brass: and at four sides as it were little shoulders under the lavatory cast, looking one against another.

text.NRSV: Its opening was within a crown whose height was one cubit; its opening was round, as a stand is made, a cubit and a half deep; on its opening there were carvings; its panels were four-sided, not round.
text.Rotherham: And, the mouth thereof, within the capital and upwards, was a cubit, and, the mouth thereof, was round, of pedestal work, a cubit and a half,—moreover also, upon the mouth thereof, were gravings, with their side-walls four-square, not round.
text.KJV: And the mouth of it within the chapiter and above was a cubit: but the mouth thereof was round after the work of the base, a cubit and an half: and also upon the mouth of it were gravings with their borders, foursquare, not round.
text.NABRE: The mouth of the basin was inside, and a cubit above, the crown, whose opening was round, made like a receptacle, a cubit and a half in depth. There was carved work at the opening, on panels that were square, not circular.
text.DRB: The mouth also of the lavatory was inward in the top of the head: and that which appeared outward, was of one cubit all round, and together it had one cubit and a half: and in the corners of the pillars were diverse engravings: and the middle enterpillars square not round.

text.NRSV: The four wheels were underneath the panels; the axles of the wheels were in the stand; and the height of a wheel was a cubit and a half.
text.Rotherham: And, four wheels, were beneath the side-walls, the axletrees of the wheels, being in the stands,—and, the height of each wheel, was a cubit and a half:
text.KJV: And under the borders were four wheels; and the axletrees of the wheels were joined to the base: and the height of a wheel was a cubit and half a cubit.
text.NABRE: The four wheels were below the paneling, and the axletrees of the wheels and the stand were of one piece. Each wheel was a cubit and a half high.
text.DRB: The four wheels also which were at the four corners of a foot, joined one to another under the foot: one wheel had in height a cubit and a half.

text.NRSV: The wheels were made like a chariot wheel; their axles, their rims, their spokes, and their hubs were all cast.
text.Rotherham: and, the work of the wheels, was like the work of a chariot-wheel; and, their axletrees, and their felloes, and their spokes, and their naves, the whole, were molten.
text.KJV: And the work of the wheels was like the work of a chariot wheel: their axletrees, and their naves, and their felloes, and their spokes, were all molten.
text.NABRE: The wheels were constructed like chariot wheels; their axletrees, rims, spokes, and hubs were all cast.
text.DRB: And they were such wheels as are accustomed to be made in a chariot: and their axletrees and spokes, and strakes, and naves, all cast.

text.NRSV: There were four supports at the four corners of each stand; the supports were of one piece with the stands.
text.Rotherham: And there were, four shoulder-pieces, at the four corners of each stand, of the stand itself, were the shoulder-pieces thereof.
text.KJV: And there were four undersetters to the four corners of one base: and the undersetters were of the very base itself.
text.NABRE: The four braces reached the four corners of each stand, and formed part of the stand.
text.DRB: For those four little shoulders also at every corner of one foot, were cast out of the foot and joined together.

text.NRSV: On the top of the stand there was a round band half a cubit high; on the top of the stand, its stays and its panels were of one piece with it.
text.Rotherham: And, in the top of the stand, half a cubit in height, was a circular piece round about,—and, on the top of the stand, the tenons thereof, and, the side-walls thereof, were of the same.
text.KJV: And in the top of the base was there a round compass of half a cubit high: and on the top of the base the ledges thereof and the borders thereof were of the same.
text.NABRE: At the top of the stand there was a raised collar half a cubit high, and the handles and panels on top of the stand formed part of it.
text.DRB: And in the top of the foot was a certain roundness of half a cubit, so wrought, that the lavatory might be put thereon, having the engravings thereof, and diverse carvings of it-self.

text.NRSV: On the surfaces of its stays and on its panels he carved cherubim, lions, and palm trees, where each had space, with wreaths all around.
text.Rotherham: Then carved he, upon the plates of the tenons thereof, and upon the side-walls thereof, cherubim, lions, and palm-trees,—according to the open space of each with a wreath round about.
text.KJV: For on the plates of the ledges thereof, and on the borders thereof, he graved cherubims, lions, and palm trees, according to the proportion of every one, and additions round about.
text.NABRE: On the flat ends of the handles and on the panels, wherever there was a bare space, cherubim, lions, and palm trees were carved, as well as wreaths all around.
text.DRB: He graved also in those ceilings, which were of brass, and in the corners, cherubs, and lions, and palmtrees, as it were in the similitude of a man standing, that they seemed not to be engraven, but put to round about.

text.NRSV: In this way he made the ten stands; all of them were cast alike, with the same measure and the same form.
text.Rotherham: Thus, made he the ten stands,—one mould, one measure, one shape, had they, all.
text.KJV: After this manner he made the ten bases: all of them had one casting, one measure, and one size.
text.NABRE: This was how he made the ten stands, all of the same casting, the same size, the same shape.
text.DRB: After this manner made he ten feet, of one casting and measure, and like graving.

text.NRSV: He made ten basins of bronze; each basin held forty baths, each basin measured four cubits; there was a basin on each of the ten stands.
text.Rotherham: Then made he ten lavers of bronze,—forty baths, would each laver contain, four cubits, was each laver, one laver, was on each of, the ten stands.
text.KJV: Then made he ten lavers of brass: one laver contained forty baths: and every laver was four cubits: and upon every one of the ten bases one laver.
text.NABRE: He made ten bronze basins, each four cubits in diameter with a capacity of forty baths, one basin atop each of the ten stands.
text.DRB: He made also ten lavatories of brass: one lavatory contained forty bates, and it was of four cubits: also at every foot, that is ten, he put so many lavatories.

text.NRSV: He set five of the stands on the south side of the house, and five on the north side of the house; he set the sea on the southeast corner of the house.
text.Rotherham: And he set the stands, five on the right side of the house, and five on the left side of the house,—but, the sea, he set on the right side of the house eastward, over against the south.
text.KJV: And he put five bases on the right side of the house, and five on the left side of the house: and he set the sea on the right side of the house eastward over against the south.
text.NABRE: He placed the stands, five on the south side of the house and five on the north. The sea he placed off to the southeast from the south side of the house.
text.DRB: And he set the ten feet, five on the right side of the temple, and five on the left: and the sea he put on the right side of the temple against the East toward the South.

text.NRSV: Hiram also made the pots, the shovels, and the basins. So Hiram finished all the work that he did for King Solomon on the house of the Lord:
text.Rotherham: And Hiram made lavers, and shovels, and bowls,—and Hiram ended the doing of all the work which he wrought for King Solomon, for the house of Yahweh:
text.KJV: And Hiram made the lavers, and the shovels, and the basons. So Hiram made an end of doing all the work that he made king Solomon for the house of the Lord:
text.NABRE: When Hiram had made the pots, shovels, and bowls, he finished all his work for King Solomon in the house of the Lord:
text.DRB: Hiram therefore made cauldrons, and shovels, & little pots, and perfected all the work of king Salomon in the temple of our Lord.

text.NRSV: the two pillars, the two bowls of the capitals that were on the tops of the pillars, the two latticeworks to cover the two bowls of the capitals that were on the tops of the pillars;
text.Rotherham: two pillars,—and the bowls of the capitals which were upon the top of the two pillars,—and the two frames of checker-work, covering the two bowls of the capitals, which were upon the top of the pillars;
text.KJV: The two pillars, and the two bowls of the chapiters that were on the top of the two pillars; and the two networks, to cover the two bowls of the chapiters which were upon the top of the pillars;
text.NABRE: two columns; two nodes for the capitals on top of the columns; two pieces of netting covering the two nodes for the capitals on top of the columns;
text.DRB: Two pillars, and two cords of the little heads upon the little heads of the pillars: and two little nets, to cover the two cords, that were over the heads of the pillars.

text.NRSV: the four hundred pomegranates for the two latticeworks, two rows of pomegranates for each latticework, to cover the two bowls of the capitals that were on the pillars;
text.Rotherham: and the four hundred pomegranates, for the two frames of checker-work,—two rows of pomegranates to each frame, for covering the two bowls of the capitals, which were on the face of the pillars;
text.KJV: And four hundred pomegranates for the two networks, even two rows of pomegranates for one network, to cover the two bowls of the chapiters that were upon the pillars;
text.NABRE: four hundred pomegranates in double rows on both pieces of netting that covered the two nodes of the capitals on top of the columns;
text.DRB: And four hundred pomegranates in the two nets: two rews of pomegranates in every net, to cover the cords of the little heads, which were upon the heads of the pillars.

text.NRSV: the ten stands, and the ten basins on the stands;
text.Rotherham: and the ten stands,—with the ten lavers upon the stands;
text.KJV: And the ten bases, and ten lavers on the bases;
text.NABRE: ten stands; ten basins on the stands;
text.DRB: And ten feet, and ten lavatories upon the feet.

text.NRSV: the one sea, and the twelve oxen underneath the sea.
text.Rotherham: and the one sea,—with the twelve oxen under the sea;
text.KJV: And one sea, and twelve oxen under the sea;
text.NABRE: one sea; twelve oxen supporting the sea;
text.DRB: And one sea, and twelve oxen under the sea.

text.NRSV: The pots, the shovels, and the basins, all these vessels that Hiram made for King Solomon for the house of the Lord were of burnished bronze.
text.Rotherham: and the pans, and the shovels, and the tossing bowls, and, all these vessels which Hiram made King Solomon, for the house of Yahweh, were of burnished bronze.
text.KJV: And the pots, and the shovels, and the basons: and all these vessels, which Hiram made to king Solomon for the house of the Lord, were of bright brass.
text.NABRE: pots, shovels, and bowls. All these articles which Hiram made for King Solomon in the house of the Lord were of burnished bronze.
text.DRB: And cauldrons, & shovels, and little pots. All the vessels that Hiram made to king Salomon in the house of our Lord, were of bright latten.

text.NRSV: In the plain of the Jordan the king cast them, in the clay ground between Succoth and Zarethan.
text.Rotherham: In the circuit of the Jordan, did the king cast them, in the clay ground,—between Succoth and Zarethan.
text.KJV: In the plain of Jordan did the king cast them, in the clay ground between Succoth and Zarthan.
text.NABRE: The king had them cast in the neighborhood of the Jordan, between Succoth and Zarethan, in thick clay molds.
text.DRB: In the champion country of Jordan did the king cast those things in a clay ground, between Socoth & Sarthan.

text.NRSV: Solomon left all the vessels unweighed, because there were so many of them; the weight of the bronze was not determined.
text.Rotherham: And Solomon let all the vessels be, because of their exceeding great multitude,—the weight of the bronze was not found out.
text.KJV: And Solomon left all the vessels unweighed, because they were exceeding many: neither was the weight of the brass found out.
text.NABRE: Solomon did not weigh all the articles because they were so numerous; the weight of the bronze, therefore, was not determined.
text.DRB: And Salomon placed all the vessels: but for the exceeding great multitude the brass could not be weighed.

text.NRSV: So Solomon made all the vessels that were in the house of the Lord: the golden altar, the golden table for the bread of the Presence,
text.Rotherham: And Solomon made all the vessels that were in the house of Yahweh,—the altar of gold, and the table of gold, whereon was the presence-bread;
text.KJV: And Solomon made all the vessels that pertained unto the house of the Lord: the altar of gold, and the table of gold, whereupon the shewbread was,
text.NABRE: Solomon made all the articles that were for the house of the Lord: the golden altar; the table on which the showbread lay;
text.DRB: And Salomon made all the vessels in the house of our Lord: an altar of gold, and a table, whereupon the loaves of proposition should be put, of gold:

text.NRSV: the lampstands of pure gold, five on the south side and five on the north, in front of the inner sanctuary; the flowers, the lamps, and the tongs, of gold;
text.Rotherham: and the lamp-holders—five on the right and five on the left, before the shrine, of pure gold,—with the blossoms, and the lamps, and the tongs, of gold;
text.KJV: And the candlesticks of pure gold, five on the right side, and five on the left, before the oracle, with the flowers, and the lamps, and the tongs of gold,
text.NABRE: the lampstands of pure gold, five to the right and five to the left before the inner sanctuary; their flowers, lamps, and tongs of gold;
text.DRB: and candlesticks of gold, five on the right hand, and five on the left against the oracle, of pure gold: and as it were little flowers, and lamps above of gold: and golden snuffers,

text.NRSV: the cups, snuffers, basins, dishes for incense, and firepans, of pure gold; the sockets for the doors of the innermost part of the house, the most holy place, and for the doors of the nave of the temple, of gold.
text.Rotherham: and the bowls, and the snuffers, and the tossing bowls, and the spoons, and the censers, of pure gold,—and the hinge-holes, for the doors of the inner house, the holy of holies, and for the doors of the house itself, the temple, of, gold.
text.KJV: And the bowls, and the snuffers, and the basons, and the spoons, and the censers of pure gold; and the hinges of gold, both for the doors of the inner house, the most holy place, and for the doors of the house, to wit, of the temple.
text.NABRE: basins, snuffers, bowls, cups, and firepans of pure gold; hinges of gold for the doors of the innermost part of the house, or holy of holies, and for the doors of the outer room, the nave.
text.DRB: and water pots, and fleshhooks, and phials, and mortars, and censers, of most pure gold: and the hinges of doors of the inner Sanctum Sanctorum, and of the doors of the house of the temple, were of gold.

text.NRSV: Thus all the work that King Solomon did on the house of the Lord was finished. Solomon brought in the things that his father David had dedicated, the silver, the gold, and the vessels, and stored them in the treasuries of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So was completed all the work which King Solomon made for the house of Yahweh, and Solomon brought in the hallowed things of David his father—the silver, and the gold, and the vessels, did he put in the treasuries of the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: So was ended all the work that king Solomon made for the house of the Lord. And Solomon brought in the things which David his father had dedicated; even the silver, and the gold, and the vessels, did he put among the treasures of the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: When all the work undertaken by King Solomon in the house of the Lord was completed, he brought in the votive offerings of his father David, and put the silver, gold, and other articles in the treasuries of the house of the Lord.
text.DRB: And Salomon perfected all the work that he did in the house of our Lord, and brought in the things that David his father had sanctified, silver and gold, and the vessels, and laid them in the treasures of the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes, the leaders of the ancestral houses of the Israelites, before King Solomon in Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord out of the city of David, which is Zion.
text.Rotherham: Then, did Solomon call together the elders of Israel, and all the heads of the tribes, chiefs of the fathers of the sons of Israel, unto King Solomon in Jerusalem,—that they might bring up the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, out of the city of David, the same is Zion.
text.KJV: Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel, and all the heads of the tribes, the chief of the fathers of the children of Israel, unto king Solomon in Jerusalem, that they might bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord out of the city of David, which is Zion.
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 Dedication of the Temple. Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes, the princes in the ancestral houses of the Israelites. They came to King Solomon in Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the Lord ’s covenant from the city of David (which is Zion).
text.DRB: Then were gathered together all the ancients of Israel with the princes of the tribes, and the heads of the families of the children of Israel to king Salomon into Jerusalem: that they might carry the Ark of the covenant of our Lord out of the city of David, that is, out of Sion.

text.NRSV: All the people of Israel assembled to King Solomon at the festival in the month Ethanim, which is the seventh month.
text.Rotherham: So all the men of Israel came together unto King Solomon, in the month of steady flowings, at the festival,—the same is the seventh month.
text.KJV: And all the men of Israel assembled themselves unto king Solomon at the feast in the month Ethanim, which is the seventh month.
text.NABRE: All the people of Israel assembled before King Solomon during the festival in the month of Ethanim (the seventh month).
text.DRB: And all Israel assembled to king Salomon in the month of Ethanim, on a solemn day, that is the seventh month.

text.NRSV: And all the elders of Israel came, and the priests carried the ark.
text.Rotherham: And all the elders of Israel came in,—and the priests took up the ark;
text.KJV: And all the elders of Israel came, and the priests took up the ark.
text.NABRE: When all the elders of Israel had arrived, the priests took up the ark;
text.DRB: And all the ancients of Israel came, and the Priests took the ark,

text.NRSV: So they brought up the ark of the Lord, the tent of meeting, and all the holy vessels that were in the tent; the priests and the Levites brought them up.
text.Rotherham: and they brought up the ark of Yahweh, and the Tent of Meeting, and all the hallowed vessels that were in the tent,—yea, the priests and the Levites, did bring them up.
text.KJV: And they brought up the ark of the Lord, and the tabernacle of the congregation, and all the holy vessels that were in the tabernacle, even those did the priests and the Levites bring up.
text.NABRE: and they brought up the ark of the Lord and the tent of meeting with all the sacred vessels that were in the tent. The priests and Levites brought them up.
text.DRB: and carried the ark of our Lord, and the tabernacle of covenant, and all the vessels of the Sanctuary, that were in the tabernacle: and the Priests and the Levites carried them.

text.NRSV: King Solomon and all the congregation of Israel, who had assembled before him, were with him before the ark, sacrificing so many sheep and oxen that they could not be counted or numbered.
text.Rotherham: And, King Solomon, and all the assembly of Israel who had assembled themselves unto him, were with him, before the ark,—sacrificing sheep and oxen, which could neither be recorded nor counted, for multitude.
text.KJV: And king Solomon, and all the congregation of Israel, that were assembled unto him, were with him before the ark, sacrificing sheep and oxen, that could not be told nor numbered for multitude.
text.NABRE: King Solomon and the entire community of Israel, gathered for the occasion before the ark, sacrificed sheep and oxen too many to number or count.
text.DRB: And King Salomon, and all the multitude of Israel which was assembled unto him, went with him before the ark, and they immolated sheep and oxen without estimation and number.

text.NRSV: Then the priests brought the ark of the covenant of the Lord to its place, in the inner sanctuary of the house, in the most holy place, underneath the wings of the cherubim.
text.Rotherham: So the priests brought in the ark of the covenant of Yahweh into its place, into the shrine of the house, into the holy of holies,—under the wings of the cherubim.
text.KJV: And the priests brought in the ark of the covenant of the Lord unto his place, into the oracle of the house, to the most holy place, even under the wings of the cherubims.
text.NABRE: The priests brought the ark of the covenant of the Lord to its place, the inner sanctuary of the house, the holy of holies, beneath the wings of the cherubim.
text.DRB: And the priests brought in the ark of the covenant of our Lord into his place, into the oracle of the temple, into Sanctum Sanctorum under the wings of the Cherubs.

text.NRSV: For the cherubim spread out their wings over the place of the ark, so that the cherubim made a covering above the ark and its poles.
text.Rotherham: For, the cherubim, were spreading forth their two wings, over the place of the ark,—and the cherubim made a covering over the ark and over the staves thereof, above.
text.KJV: For the cherubims spread forth their two wings over the place of the ark, and the cherubims covered the ark and the staves thereof above.
text.NABRE: The cherubim had their wings spread out over the place of the ark, sheltering the ark and its poles from above.
text.DRB: For the Cherubs spread their wings over the place of the ark, and covered the ark, and the bars thereof above.

text.NRSV: The poles were so long that the ends of the poles were seen from the holy place in front of the inner sanctuary; but they could not be seen from outside; they are there to this day.
text.Rotherham: And they drew out the staves, and the heads of the staves were seen from the holy place, in front of the shrine, although they were not seen on the outside,—and they have remained there until this day.
text.KJV: And they drew out the staves, that the ends of the staves were seen out in the holy place before the oracle, and they were not seen without: and there they are unto this day.
text.NABRE: The poles were so long that their ends could be seen from the holy place in front of the inner sanctuary. They cannot be seen from outside, but they remain there to this day.
text.DRB: And whereas the bars stood out, and the ends of them appeared without in the Sanctuary before the oracle, they appeared no farther outward, which also were there until this present day.

text.NRSV: There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets of stone that Moses had placed there at Horeb, where the Lord made a covenant with the Israelites, when they came out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: There was nothing in the ark, save the two tables of stone, which Moses deposited there in Horeb,—the tables of the covenant which Yahweh solemnised with the sons of Israel, when they came forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: There was nothing in the ark save the two tables of stone, which Moses put there at Horeb, when the Lord made a covenant with the children of Israel, when they came out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: There was nothing in the ark but the two stone tablets which Moses had put there at Horeb, when the Lord made a covenant with the Israelites after they went forth from the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And in the ark there was nothing else but two tables of stone, which Moyses put in it in Horeb, when our Lord made the covenant with the children of Israel, when they came out of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: And when the priests came out of the holy place, a cloud filled the house of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when the priests came forth out of the holy place, that, a cloud, filled the house of Yahweh;
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when the priests were come out of the holy place, that the cloud filled the house of the Lord,
text.NABRE: When the priests left the holy place, the cloud filled the house of the Lord
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when the Priests were gone out of the Sanctuary, a cloud filled the house of our Lord,

text.NRSV: so that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud; for the glory of the Lord filled the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: so that the priests could not stand to minister, because of the cloud,—for, the glory of Yahweh, filled, the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: So that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud: for the glory of the Lord had filled the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: so that the priests could no longer minister because of the cloud, since the glory of the Lord had filled the house of the Lord.
text.DRB: and the Priests could not stand and minister for the cloud: for the glory of our Lord had filled the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Solomon said, “The Lord has said that he would dwell in thick darkness.
text.Rotherham: Then, said Solomon,—Yahweh, said, that he would make his habitation in thick gloom:
text.KJV: Then spake Solomon, The Lord said that he would dwell in the thick darkness.
text.NABRE: Then Solomon said, “The Lord intends to dwell in the dark cloud;
text.DRB: Then said Salomon: Our Lord said that he would dwell in a cloud.

text.NRSV: I have built you an exalted house, a place for you to dwell in forever.”
text.Rotherham: I have, built, a house as a high abode for thee,—A settled place for thee to abide in, for ages.
text.KJV: I have surely built thee an house to dwell in, a settled place for thee to abide in for ever.
text.NABRE: I have indeed built you a princely house, the base for your enthronement forever.”
text.DRB: Building I have built a house for thy habitation, thy most firm throne for ever.

text.NRSV: Then the king turned around and blessed all the assembly of Israel, while all the assembly of Israel stood.
text.Rotherham: And the king turned about his face, and blessed all the convocation of Israel,—while all the convocation of Israel was standing;
text.KJV: And the king turned his face about, and blessed all the congregation of Israel: (and all the congregation of Israel stood;)
text.NABRE: The king turned and blessed the whole assembly of Israel, while the whole assembly of Israel stood.
text.DRB: And the king turned his face, and blessed all the Church of Israel: for all the Church of Israel stood.

text.NRSV: He said, “Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, who with his hand has fulfilled what he promised with his mouth to my father David, saying,
text.Rotherham: and he said—Blessed, be Yahweh, God of Israel, who spake with his mouth, unto David my father,—that which with his hand he hath fulfilled, saying:—
text.KJV: And he said, Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, which spake with his mouth unto David my father, and hath with his hand fulfilled it, saying,
text.NABRE: He said: “Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, who with his own mouth spoke a promise to David my father and by his hand fulfilled it, saying:
text.DRB: And Salomon said: Bless be our Lord the God of Israel, who spake by his mouth to David my father, & in his own hands hath perfected it, saying:

text.NRSV: ‘Since the day that I brought my people Israel out of Egypt, I have not chosen a city out of all the tribes of Israel in which to build a house, that my name might be there; but I chose David to be over my people Israel.’
text.Rotherham: Since the day that I brought forth my people Israel, out of Egypt, I had chosen no city, out of all the tribes of Israel, for building a house, where my Name might be,—but I have chosen Jerusalem, that my Name should be there, and I have chosen David, to be over my people Israel:
text.KJV: Since the day that I brought forth my people Israel out of Egypt, I chose no city out of all the tribes of Israel to build an house, that my name might be therein; but I chose David to be over my people Israel.
text.NABRE: Since the day I brought my people Israel out of Egypt, I have not chosen a city out of any tribe of Israel for the building of a house, that my name might be there; but I have chosen David to rule my people Israel.
text.DRB: Since the day that I brought my people Israel out of Ægypt, I chose no city of all the tribes of Israel, that a house might be built, and my name might be there: but I chose David to be over my people Israel.

text.NRSV: My father David had it in mind to build a house for the name of the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass, that it was near the heart of David my father,—to build a house for the Name of Yahweh, God of Israel.
text.KJV: And it was in the heart of David my father to build an house for the name of the Lord God of Israel.
text.NABRE: When David my father wished to build a house for the name of the Lord, the God of Israel,
text.DRB: And David my father would have built a house to the name of our Lord the God of Israel:

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to my father David, ‘You did well to consider building a house for my name;
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto David my father, Because it was near thy heart to build a house for my Name, thou didst well that it was near thy heart;
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto David my father, Whereas it was in thine heart to build an house unto my name, thou didst well that it was in thine heart.
text.NABRE: the Lord said to him: In wishing to build a house for my name, you did well.
text.DRB: and our Lord said to David my father: In that thou hast thought in thy heart to build a house to my name, thou hast done well, casting this same thing in thy mind.

text.NRSV: nevertheless you shall not build the house, but your son who shall be born to you shall build the house for my name.’
text.Rotherham: Only, thou thyself, must not build the house,—but, thine own son who hath proceeded out of thy loins, he, shall build the house, for my Name.
text.KJV: Nevertheless thou shalt not build the house; but thy son that shall come forth out of thy loins, he shall build the house unto my name.
text.NABRE: But it is not you who will build the house, but your son, who comes from your loins; he shall build the house for my name.
text.DRB: Nevertheless thou shalt not build me a house, but thy son, that shall come forth of thy reins, he shall build a house to my name.

text.NRSV: Now the Lord has upheld the promise that he made; for I have risen in the place of my father David; I sit on the throne of Israel, as the Lord promised, and I have built the house for the name of the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh hath established his word which he spake,—and I have been raised up in the room of David my father, and have taken my seat upon the throne of Israel, as spake Yahweh, and have built the house for the Name of Yahweh, God of Israel;
text.KJV: And the Lord hath performed his word that he spake, and I am risen up in the room of David my father, and sit on the throne of Israel, as the Lord promised, and have built an house for the name of the Lord God of Israel.
text.NABRE: Now the Lord has fulfilled the word he spoke: I have succeeded David my father, and I sit on the throne of Israel, as the Lord has spoken, and I have built this house for the name of the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath confirmed his word, which he spake: & I stand for David my father, & sit upon the throne of Israel, as our Lord hath spoken: and I have built a house to the name of our Lord the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: There I have provided a place for the ark, in which is the covenant of the Lord that he made with our ancestors, when he brought them out of the land of Egypt.”
text.Rotherham: and have appointed there a place for the ark, wherein is the covenant of Yahweh,—which he solemnised with our fathers, when he brought them forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And I have set there a place for the ark, wherein is the covenant of the Lord, which he made with our fathers, when he brought them out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: I have provided there a place for the ark in which is the covenant of the Lord that he made with our ancestors when he brought them out of the land of Egypt.”
text.DRB: And I have appointed there a place for the ark, wherein the covenant of our Lord is, which he made with our fathers, when they came out of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Then Solomon stood before the altar of the Lord in the presence of all the assembly of Israel, and spread out his hands to heaven.
text.Rotherham: And Solomon stood before the altar of Yahweh, in the presence of all the convocation of Israel,—and spread forth his hands towards the heavens;
text.KJV: And Solomon stood before the altar of the Lord in the presence of all the congregation of Israel, and spread forth his hands toward heaven:
text.NABRE: Solomon’s Prayer. Solomon stood before the altar of the Lord in the presence of the whole assembly of Israel, and stretching forth his hands toward heaven,
text.DRB: And Salomon stood before the altar of our Lord in the sight of the assembly of Israel, and extended his hands toward heaven,

text.NRSV: He said, “O Lord, God of Israel, there is no God like you in heaven above or on earth beneath, keeping covenant and steadfast love for your servants who walk before you with all their heart,
text.Rotherham: and said—O Yahweh, God of Israel! Not like thee, is there a God, in the heavens above, or upon the earth beneath,—keeping Covenant and Lovingkindness for thy servants who are walking before thee, with all their heart;
text.KJV: And he said, Lord God of Israel, there is no God like thee, in heaven above, or on earth beneath, who keepest covenant and mercy with thy servants that walk before thee with all their heart:
text.NABRE: he said, “ Lord, God of Israel, there is no God like you in heaven above or on earth below; you keep covenant and love toward your servants who walk before you with their whole heart,
text.DRB: and said: Lord God of Israel, there is no God like to thee in heaven above, and upon the earth beneath: which keepest covenant and mercy with thy servants, that walk before thee in all their heart.

text.NRSV: the covenant that you kept for your servant my father David as you declared to him; you promised with your mouth and have this day fulfilled with your hand.
text.Rotherham: who hast kept for thy servant David my father, that which thou didst promise him,—in that thou didst promise with thy mouth, and, with thy hand, hast fulfilled, as it is this day.
text.KJV: Who hast kept with thy servant David my father that thou promisedst him: thou spakest also with thy mouth, and hast fulfilled it with thine hand, as it is this day.
text.NABRE: the covenant that you kept toward your servant, David my father, what you promised him; your mouth has spoken and your hand has fulfilled this very day.
text.DRB: Which hast kept to thy servant David my father, the things that thou hast spoken to him: by mouth thou didst speak, and with thy hands thou hast accomplished, as this day proveth.

text.NRSV: Therefore, O Lord, God of Israel, keep for your servant my father David that which you promised him, saying, ‘There shall never fail you a successor before me to sit on the throne of Israel, if only your children look to their way, to walk before me as you have walked before me.’
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh, God of Israel, keep thou for thy servant David my father, that which thou didst promise him, saying, There shall not be cut off to thee a man from before me, to sit upon the throne of Israel,—if only, thy sons take heed to their way, by walking before me, as thou hast walked before me.
text.KJV: Therefore now, Lord God of Israel, keep with thy servant David my father that thou promisedst him, saying, There shall not fail thee a man in my sight to sit on the throne of Israel; so that thy children take heed to their way, that they walk before me as thou hast walked before me.
text.NABRE: And now, Lord, God of Israel, keep toward your servant, David my father, what you promised: There shall never be wanting someone from your line to sit before me on the throne of Israel, provided that your descendants keep to their way, walking before me as you have.
text.DRB: Now therefore Lord God of Israel, keep unto thy servant David my father the things which thou hast spoken to him, saying: There shall not be taken away of thee a man before me, which sitteth upon the throne of Israel: yet so if thy children shall keep their way, that they walk before me as thou hast walked in my sight.

text.NRSV: Therefore, O God of Israel, let your word be confirmed, which you promised to your servant my father David.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O God of Israel,—verified be thy word, I pray thee, which thou didst speak to thy servant, David my father.
text.KJV: And now, O God of Israel, let thy word, I pray thee, be verified, which thou spakest unto thy servant David my father.
text.NABRE: Now, God of Israel, may the words you spoke to your servant, David my father, be confirmed.
text.DRB: And now Lord God of Israel, let thy words be established, which thou hast spoken to thy servant David my father.

text.NRSV: “But will God indeed dwell on the earth? Even heaven and the highest heaven cannot contain you, much less this house that I have built!
text.Rotherham: But, in very deed, will God dwell, upon the earth? Lo! the heaven, yea the heaven of heavens, cannot contain thee, how much less this house which I have built?
text.KJV: But will God indeed dwell on the earth? behold, the heaven and heaven of heavens cannot contain thee; how much less this house that I have builded?
text.NABRE: “Is God indeed to dwell on earth? If the heavens and the highest heavens cannot contain you, how much less this house which I have built!
text.DRB: Is it then to be thought that indeed God dwelleth upon the earth? For if heaven, and the heavens of heavens cannot contain thee, how much more this house, which I have built?

text.NRSV: Regard your servant’s prayer and his plea, O Lord my God, heeding the cry and the prayer that your servant prays to you today;
text.Rotherham: Yet wilt thou turn unto the prayer of thy servant, and unto his supplication, O Yahweh my God,—to hearken unto the cry, and unto the prayer, wherewith thy servant doth pray before thee to-day;
text.KJV: Yet have thou respect unto the prayer of thy servant, and to his supplication, O Lord my God, to hearken unto the cry and to the prayer, which thy servant prayeth before thee to day:
text.NABRE: Regard kindly the prayer and petition of your servant, Lord, my God, and listen to the cry of supplication which I, your servant, utter before you this day.
text.DRB: But look toward the prayer of thy servant, & to his petitions, o Lord my God: hear the hymn and the prayer, which thy servant prayeth before thee this day:

text.NRSV: that your eyes may be open night and day toward this house, the place of which you said, ‘My name shall be there,’ that you may heed the prayer that your servant prays toward this place.
text.Rotherham: that thine eye may be opened toward this house, night and day, toward the place of which thou hast said, My Name shall be, there; hearkening unto the prayer which thy servant shall offer toward this place.
text.KJV: That thine eyes may be open toward this house night and day, even toward the place of which thou hast said, My name shall be there: that thou mayest hearken unto the prayer which thy servant shall make toward this place.
text.NABRE: May your eyes be open night and day toward this house, the place of which you said, My name shall be there; listen to the prayer your servant makes toward this place.
text.DRB: that thy eyes be opened upon this house night and day: upon the house, whereof thou saidst: My name shall be there: that thou hear the prayer, which thy servant prayeth in this place to thee.

text.NRSV: Hear the plea of your servant and of your people Israel when they pray toward this place; O hear in heaven your dwelling place; heed and forgive.
text.Rotherham: Wilt, thou, therefore, hearken unto the supplication of thy servant, and of thy people Israel, whensoever they shall pray toward this place,—yea wilt, thou thyself, hear, in thine own dwelling-place, in the heavens, and, when thou hearest, then wilt thou forgive?
text.KJV: And hearken thou to the supplication of thy servant, and of thy people Israel, when they shall pray toward this place: and hear thou in heaven thy dwelling place: and when thou hearest, forgive.
text.NABRE: Listen to the petition of your servant and of your people Israel which they offer toward this place. Listen, from the place of your enthronement, heaven, listen and forgive.
text.DRB: That thou hear the request of thy servant & of thy people Israel, whatsoever they shall pray for in this place, & thou shalt hear in the place of thy habitation in heaven: and when thou hast heard, thou shalt be merciful,

text.NRSV: “If someone sins against a neighbor and is given an oath to swear, and comes and swears the oath before your altar in this house,
text.Rotherham: When a man shall sin against his neighbour, and there shall be taken up against him an oath, to put him on oath,—and he shall come in and swear before thine altar, in this house,
text.KJV: If any man trespass against his neighbour, and an oath be laid upon him to cause him to swear, and the oath come before thine altar in this house:
text.NABRE: “If someone sins in some way against a neighbor and is required to take an oath sanctioned by a curse, and comes and takes the oath before your altar in this house,
text.DRB: If a man shall sin against his neighbour, & shall have any oath, wherewith he is held fast bound: & shall come because of the oath before thine altar into thy house,

text.NRSV: then hear in heaven, and act, and judge your servants, condemning the guilty by bringing their conduct on their own head, and vindicating the righteous by rewarding them according to their righteousness.
text.Rotherham: then wilt, thou thyself, hear in the heavens, and act, and judge thy servants, condemning the lawless, by setting his way upon his own head,—and justifying the righteous, by giving to him, according to his righteousness?
text.KJV: Then hear thou in heaven, and do, and judge thy servants, condemning the wicked, to bring his way upon his head; and justifying the righteous, to give him according to his righteousness.
text.NABRE: listen in heaven; act and judge your servants. Condemn the wicked, requiting their ways; acquit the just, rewarding their justice.
text.DRB: thou shalt hear in heaven: and shalt do, and judge thy servants, condemning the impious, and rendering his way upon his head, and justifying the just, and rewarding him according to his justice.

text.NRSV: “When your people Israel, having sinned against you, are defeated before an enemy but turn again to you, confess your name, pray and plead with you in this house,
text.Rotherham: When thy people Israel are smitten before an enemy, because they have been sinning against thee,—and they turn again unto thee, and confess thy Name, and pray and make supplication unto thee, in this house,
text.KJV: When thy people Israel be smitten down before the enemy, because they have sinned against thee, and shall turn again to thee, and confess thy name, and pray, and make supplication unto thee in this house:
text.NABRE: “When your people Israel are defeated by an enemy because they sinned against you, and then they return to you, praise your name, pray to you, and entreat you in this house,
text.DRB: If thy people Israel shall fly their enemies (because they will sin against thee) & doing penance and confessing to thy name, shall come, and pray, & beseech thee in this house;

text.NRSV: then hear in heaven, forgive the sin of your people Israel, and bring them again to the land that you gave to their ancestors.
text.Rotherham: then wilt, thou thyself, hear in the heavens, and forgive the sin of thy people Israel, and bring them back unto the soil, which thou gavest unto their fathers?
text.KJV: Then hear thou in heaven, and forgive the sin of thy people Israel, and bring them again unto the land which thou gavest unto their fathers.
text.NABRE: listen in heaven and forgive the sin of your people Israel, and bring them back to the land you gave their ancestors.
text.DRB: hear in heaven, & forgive the sin of thy people Israel, and thou shalt reduce them unto the land, which thou gavest to their fathers.

text.NRSV: “When heaven is shut up and there is no rain because they have sinned against you, and then they pray toward this place, confess your name, and turn from their sin, because you punish them,
text.Rotherham: When the heavens are shut up, and there is no rain, because they have been sinning against thee,—and they shall pray toward this place, and confess thy Name, and, from their sin, shall return, because thou hast been afflicting them,
text.KJV: When heaven is shut up, and there is no rain, because they have sinned against thee; if they pray toward this place, and confess thy name, and turn from their sin, when thou afflictest them:
text.NABRE: “When the heavens are closed, so that there is no rain, because they have sinned against you, but they pray toward this place and praise your name, and turn from their sin because you have afflicted them,
text.DRB: If the heaven shall be shut, and it rain not, because of their sins, and praying in this place, they do penance to thy name, and shall be converted from their sins through their affliction:

text.NRSV: then hear in heaven, and forgive the sin of your servants, your people Israel, when you teach them the good way in which they should walk; and grant rain on your land, which you have given to your people as an inheritance.
text.Rotherham: then wilt, thou thyself, hear in the heavens and forgive the sin of thy servants, and of thy people Israel, that thou mayest teach them the good way, wherein they should walk,—and give rain upon thy land, which thou hast given unto thy people, for an inheritance?
text.KJV: Then hear thou in heaven, and forgive the sin of thy servants, and of thy people Israel, that thou teach them the good way wherein they should walk, and give rain upon thy land, which thou hast given to thy people for an inheritance.
text.NABRE: listen in heaven and forgive the sin of your servants, your people Israel (for you teach them the good way in which they should walk). Give rain to this land of yours which you have given to your people as their heritage.
text.DRB: hear them in heaven, and forgive the sins of thy servants, and of thy people Israel: and shew them a good way wherein they may walk, and give rain upon thy land, which thou has given to thy people in possession.

text.NRSV: “If there is famine in the land, if there is plague, blight, mildew, locust, or caterpillar; if their enemy besieges them in any of their cities; whatever suffering, whatever sickness there is;
text.Rotherham: When there shall be, famine, in the land, when there shall be, pestilence, when there shall be, blasting, mildew locust, caterpillar, when their enemy shall besiege them in one of their own gates,—whatsoever plague, whatsoever sickness;
text.KJV: If there be in the land famine, if there be pestilence, blasting, mildew, locust, or if there be caterpiller; if their enemy besiege them in the land of their cities; whatsoever plague, whatsoever sickness there be;
text.NABRE: “If there is famine in the land or pestilence; or if blight comes, or mildew, or locusts, or caterpillars; if an enemy of your people presses upon them in the land and at their gates; whatever plague or sickness there may be;
text.DRB: If famine arise in the land, or pestilence, or corrupt air, or blasting, or locust, or rust, and their enemy afflict them besieging the gates, all plague, all infirmity,

text.NRSV: whatever prayer, whatever plea there is from any individual or from all your people Israel, all knowing the afflictions of their own hearts so that they stretch out their hands toward this house;
text.Rotherham: any prayer, any supplication which, any son of earth, may have, of all thy people Israel,—who will certainly know every man the plague of his own heart, and so he shall spread abroad his hands towards this house,
