text.NABRE: He then bought the mountain of Samaria from Shemer for two silver talents and built upon the mountain the city he named Samaria, after Shemer, the former owner.
text.DRB: And he bought the mount of Samaria of Somer for two talents of silver: & he built it, and he called the city which he had built by the name of Semer the lord of the mount of Samaria.

text.NRSV: Omri did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; he did more evil than all who were before him.
text.Rotherham: And Omri did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—and did more wickedly than all who were before him;
text.KJV: But Omri wrought evil in the eyes of the Lord, and did worse than all that were before him.
text.NABRE: But Omri did what was evil in the Lord ’s sight, more than any of his predecessors.
text.DRB: And Amri did evil in the sight of our Lord, & wrought wickedly above all, that were before him.

text.NRSV: For he walked in all the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and in the sins that he caused Israel to sin, provoking the Lord, the God of Israel, to anger with their idols.
text.Rotherham: yea he walked in all the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and in his sin wherewith he caused, Israel, to sin,—provoking to anger Yahweh, God of Israel, with their vanities.
text.KJV: For he walked in all the way of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, and in his sin wherewith he made Israel to sin, to provoke the Lord God of Israel to anger with their vanities.
text.NABRE: In every way he imitated the sinful conduct of Jeroboam, son of Nebat, and the sin he had caused Israel to commit, thus provoking the Lord, the God of Israel, to anger by their idols.
text.DRB: And he walked in all the way of Jeroboam the son of Nabat, and in his sins wherewith he made Israel to sin: that they might anger our Lord the God of Israel in their vanities.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Omri that he did, and the power that he showed, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Omri, what he did, and his might that he shewed, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.KJV: Now the rest of the acts of Omri which he did, and his might that he shewed, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Omri, what he did and his valor, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Amri, and the battles he made, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?

text.NRSV: Omri slept with his ancestors, and was buried in Samaria; and his son Ahab succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: So Omri slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria,—and, Ahab his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: So Omri slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria: and Ahab his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Omri rested with his ancestors; he was buried in Samaria, and Ahab his son succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And Amri slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria, and Achab his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-eighth year of King Asa of Judah, Ahab son of Omri began to reign over Israel; Ahab son of Omri reigned over Israel in Samaria twenty-two years.
text.Rotherham: Now, Ahab son of Omri, began to reign over Israel, in the thirty-eighth year of Asa king of Judah,—and Ahab son of Omri reigned over Israel, in Samaria, twenty-two years.
text.KJV: And in the thirty and eighth year of Asa king of Judah began Ahab the son of Omri to reign over Israel: and Ahab the son of Omri reigned over Israel in Samaria twenty and two years.
text.NABRE: Reign of Ahab. Ahab, son of Omri, became king of Israel in the thirty-eighth year of Asa, king of Judah. Ahab, son of Omri, reigned over Israel in Samaria for twenty-two years.
text.DRB: But Achab the son of Amri reigned over Israel the eight & thirtieth year of Asa the king of Juda. And Achab the son of Amri reigned over Israel in Samaria two and twenty years.

text.NRSV: Ahab son of Omri did evil in the sight of the Lord more than all who were before him.
text.Rotherham: And Ahab son of Omri did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—and did more wickedly than all that were before him.
text.KJV: And Ahab the son of Omri did evil in the sight of the Lord above all that were before him.
text.NABRE: Ahab, son of Omri, did what was evil in the Lord ’s sight more than any of his predecessors.
text.DRB: And Achab the son of Amri did evil in the sight of our Lord above all that were before him.

text.NRSV: And as if it had been a light thing for him to walk in the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, he took as his wife Jezebel daughter of King Ethbaal of the Sidonians, and went and served Baal, and worshiped him.
text.Rotherham: And it come to pass, as though it had been too light a thing that he should walk in the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, that he took to wife Jezebel, daughter of Ethbaal, king of the Zidonians, and went and served Baal, and bowed down to him.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as if it had been a light thing for him to walk in the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, that he took to wife Jezebel the daughter of Ethbaal king of the Zidonians, and went and served Baal, and worshipped him.
text.NABRE: It was not enough for him to follow the sins of Jeroboam, son of Nebat. He even married Jezebel, daughter of Ethbaal, king of the Sidonians, and began to serve Baal, and worship him.
text.DRB: Neither did it suffice him that he walked in the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nabat: besides he took to wife Jezabel the daughter of Ethbaul the king of the Sidonians. And he went, and served Baal, and adored him.

text.NRSV: He erected an altar for Baal in the house of Baal, which he built in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: And he reared an altar unto Baal,—in the house of Baal, which he built in Samaria.
text.KJV: And he reared up an altar for Baal in the house of Baal, which he had built in Samaria.
text.NABRE: Ahab set up an altar to Baal in the house of Baal which he built in Samaria,
text.DRB: And he set an altar to Baal in the temple of Baal, which he had built in Samaria,

text.NRSV: Ahab also made a sacred pole. Ahab did more to provoke the anger of the Lord, the God of Israel, than had all the kings of Israel who were before him.
text.Rotherham: And Ahab made the Sacred Stem,—and Ahab did yet more to provoke to anger Yahweh God of Israel than all the kings of Israel who were before him.
text.KJV: And Ahab made a grove; and Ahab did more to provoke the Lord God of Israel to anger than all the kings of Israel that were before him.
text.NABRE: and also made an asherah. Ahab did more to provoke the Lord, the God of Israel, to anger than any of the kings of Israel before him.
text.DRB: and he planted a grove: and Achab added in his work, provoking our Lord the God of Israel above all the kings of Israel, that were before him.

text.NRSV: In his days Hiel of Bethel built Jericho; he laid its foundation at the cost of Abiram his firstborn, and set up its gates at the cost of his youngest son Segub, according to the word of the Lord, which he spoke by Joshua son of Nun.
text.Rotherham: In his days, did Hiel the Bethelite build Jericho,—At the price of Abiram his firstborn, he laid its foundation, and, at the price of Segub his youngest, he set up its doors, according to the word of Yahweh which he spake through Joshua son of Nun.
text.KJV: In his days did Hiel the Beth–elite build Jericho: he laid the foundation thereof in Abiram his firstborn, and set up the gates thereof in his youngest son Segub, according to the word of the Lord, which he spake by Joshua the son of Nun.
text.NABRE: During his reign, Hiel from Bethel rebuilt Jericho. At the cost of Abiram, his firstborn son, he laid the foundation, and at the cost of Segub, his youngest son, he set up the gates, according to the word of the Lord spoken through Joshua, son of Nun.
text.DRB: In his days Hiel of Bethel built Jericho: in Abiram his first born he founded it, and in Segub his last he set up the gates thereof: according to the word of our Lord, which he spake in the hand of Josue the son of Nun.

text.NRSV: Now Elijah the Tishbite, of Tishbe in Gilead, said to Ahab, “As the Lord the God of Israel lives, before whom I stand, there shall be neither dew nor rain these years, except by my word.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah the Tishbite, of Tishbe in Gilead, unto Ahab, By the life of Yahweh, God of Israel, before whom I stand, there shall not, these two years, be either dew or rain,—save at the bidding of my word.
text.KJV: And Elijah the Tishbite, who was of the inhabitants of Gilead, said unto Ahab, As the Lord God of Israel liveth, before whom I stand, there shall not be dew nor rain these years, but according to my word.
text.NABRE: IV. The Story of Elijah Chapter 17 Elijah Proclaims a Drought. Elijah the Tishbite, from Tishbe in Gilead, said to Ahab: “As the Lord, the God of Israel, lives, whom I serve, during these years there shall be no dew or rain except at my word.”
text.DRB: And Elias the Thesbite of the inhabiters of Galaad said to Achab: Our Lord liveth the God of Israel, in whose sight I stand, if there shall be these years dew and rain, but according to the words of my mouth.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to him, saying,
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto him, saying:
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord came unto him, saying,
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to Elijah:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord came to him, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go from here and turn eastward, and hide yourself by the Wadi Cherith, which is east of the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: Get thee hence, and turn thee eastward,—and hide thyself in the ravine of Cherith, which faceth the Jordan;
text.KJV: Get thee hence, and turn thee eastward, and hide thyself by the brook Cherith, that is before Jordan.
text.NABRE: Leave here, go east and hide in the Wadi Cherith, east of the Jordan.
text.DRB: Depart from hence, and go against the East, and be hid in the Torrent Carith, which is against Jordan,

text.NRSV: You shall drink from the wadi, and I have commanded the ravens to feed you there.”
text.Rotherham: and it shall be that, of the torrent, shalt thou drink,—and, the ravens, have I commanded to sustain thee, there.
text.KJV: And it shall be, that thou shalt drink of the brook; and I have commanded the ravens to feed thee there.
text.NABRE: You shall drink of the wadi, and I have commanded ravens to feed you there.
text.DRB: and there thou shalt drink of the torrent: and I have commanded the ravens that they feed thee there.

text.NRSV: So he went and did according to the word of the Lord; he went and lived by the Wadi Cherith, which is east of the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: So he went and did, according to the word of Yahweh,—yea he went and dwelt in the ravine of Cherith, which faceth the Jordan.
text.KJV: So he went and did according unto the word of the Lord: for he went and dwelt by the brook Cherith, that is before Jordan.
text.NABRE: So he left and did as the Lord had commanded. He left and remained by the Wadi Cherith, east of the Jordan.
text.DRB: He therefore went, and did according to the word of our Lord: and when he was gone, he sat in the Torrent Carith, which is against Jordan.

text.NRSV: The ravens brought him bread and meat in the morning, and bread and meat in the evening; and he drank from the wadi.
text.Rotherham: And, the ravens, used to bring him bread and flesh, in the morning, and bread and flesh, in the evening,—and, of the torrent, used he to drink.
text.KJV: And the ravens brought him bread and flesh in the morning, and bread and flesh in the evening; and he drank of the brook.
text.NABRE: Ravens brought him bread and meat in the morning, and bread and meat in the evening, and he drank from the wadi.
text.DRB: The ravens also brought him bread and flesh in the morning, in like manner bread and flesh in the evening, and he drank of the torrent.

text.NRSV: But after a while the wadi dried up, because there was no rain in the land.
text.Rotherham: But it came to pass, at the end of [certain] days, that the torrent dried up,—because there had been no rain in the land.
text.KJV: And it came to pass after a while, that the brook dried up, because there had been no rain in the land.
text.NABRE: After some time, however, the wadi ran dry, because no rain had fallen in the land.
text.DRB: But after certain days the torrent was dried: for it had not rained upon the earth.

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to him, saying,
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto him, saying—
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord came unto him, saying,
text.NABRE: So the word of the Lord came to him:
text.DRB: Therefore the word of our Lord came to him, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go now to Zarephath, which belongs to Sidon, and live there; for I have commanded a widow there to feed you.”
text.Rotherham: Arise, get thee to Zarephath, which belongeth to Zidon, and dwell there,—Lo! I have commanded, there, a widow woman, to sustain thee.
text.KJV: Arise, get thee to Zarephath, which belongeth to Zidon, and dwell there: behold, I have commanded a widow woman there to sustain thee.
text.NABRE: Arise, go to Zarephath of Sidon and stay there. I have commanded a widow there to feed you.
text.DRB: Arise, and go into Sareptha of the Sidonians, and thou shalt tarry there: for I have commanded a widow woman there to feed thee.

text.NRSV: So he set out and went to Zarephath. When he came to the gate of the town, a widow was there gathering sticks; he called to her and said, “Bring me a little water in a vessel, so that I may drink.”
text.Rotherham: So he arose and went to Zarephath, and, when he came to the entrance of the city, lo! there, a widow woman gathering sticks,—and he called to her, and said, Fetch me, I pray thee, a little water in a vessel, that I may drink.
text.KJV: So he arose and went to Zarephath. And when he came to the gate of the city, behold, the widow woman was there gathering of sticks: and he called to her, and said, Fetch me, I pray thee, a little water in a vessel, that I may drink.
text.NABRE: He arose and went to Zarephath. When he arrived at the entrance of the city, a widow was there gathering sticks; he called out to her, “Please bring me a small cupful of water to drink.”
text.DRB: He arose and went into Sareptha. And when he was come to the gate of the city, the widow woman appeared to him gathering sticks, and he called her, and said to her: Give me a little water in a vessel, that I may drink.

text.NRSV: As she was going to bring it, he called to her and said, “Bring me a morsel of bread in your hand.”
text.Rotherham: And, as she went to fetch it, he called to her and said, Bring me, I pray thee, a morsel of bread, in thy hand.
text.KJV: And as she was going to fetch it, he called to her, and said, Bring me, I pray thee, a morsel of bread in thine hand.
text.NABRE: She left to get it, and he called out after her, “Please bring along a crust of bread.”
text.DRB: And when she went to fetch it, he cried after her saying: Bring me also, I beseech thee, a morsel of bread in thy hand.

text.NRSV: But she said, “As the Lord your God lives, I have nothing baked, only a handful of meal in a jar, and a little oil in a jug; I am now gathering a couple of sticks, so that I may go home and prepare it for myself and my son, that we may eat it, and die.”
text.Rotherham: And she said—By the life of Yahweh, thy God, verily I have not a cake, only a handful of meal in the jar, and a little oil in the cruse,—and lo! I have been gathering a couple of sticks, so I shall go in and make it ready for me and for my son, that we may eat it—and die!
text.KJV: And she said, As the Lord thy God liveth, I have not a cake, but an handful of meal in a barrel, and a little oil in a cruse: and, behold, I am gathering two sticks, that I may go in and dress it for me and my son, that we may eat it, and die.
text.NABRE: She said, “As the Lord, your God, lives, I have nothing baked; there is only a handful of flour in my jar and a little oil in my jug. Just now I was collecting a few sticks, to go in and prepare something for myself and my son; when we have eaten it, we shall die.”
text.DRB: who answered: Our Lord thy God liveth, I have no bread, but so much meal in a pot as a hand can hold, and a little oil in a vessel: behold I gather two sticks, that I may go in, and dress it for me & my son, that we may eat, and die.

text.NRSV: Elijah said to her, “Do not be afraid; go and do as you have said; but first make me a little cake of it and bring it to me, and afterwards make something for yourself and your son.
text.Rotherham: And Elijah said unto her—Do not fear, go in—make ready according to thy word,—howbeit, make me thereof a little cake, first, and bring it out to me, and, for thyself and thy son, make ready, afterwards.
text.KJV: And Elijah said unto her, Fear not; go and do as thou hast said: but make me thereof a little cake first, and bring it unto me, and after make for thee and for thy son.
text.NABRE: Elijah said to her, “Do not be afraid. Go and do as you have said. But first make me a little cake and bring it to me. Afterwards you can prepare something for yourself and your son.
text.DRB: To whom Elias said: fear not, but go, and do as thou hast said: but first make for me of the same meal a little hearth cake, & bring it to me: & for thyself & thy son thou shalt make afterward.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord the God of Israel: The jar of meal will not be emptied and the jug of oil will not fail until the day that the Lord sends rain on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: For, thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel, The jar of meal, shall not waste, and, the cruse of oil, shall not fail,—until the day that Yahweh giveth rain upon the face of the ground.
text.KJV: For thus saith the Lord God of Israel, The barrel of meal shall not waste, neither shall the cruse of oil fail, until the day that the Lord sendeth rain upon the earth.
text.NABRE: For the Lord, the God of Israel, says: The jar of flour shall not go empty, nor the jug of oil run dry, until the day when the Lord sends rain upon the earth.”
text.DRB: For thus saith our Lord the God of Israel: The pot of meal shall not fail, nor the vessel of oil be diminished until the day, wherein our Lord will give rain upon the face of the earth.

text.NRSV: She went and did as Elijah said, so that she as well as he and her household ate for many days.
text.Rotherham: So she went and made ready, according to the word of Elijah,—and did eat, she, and he, and her house, [certain] days:
text.KJV: And she went and did according to the saying of Elijah: and she, and he, and her house, did eat many days.
text.NABRE: She left and did as Elijah had said. She had enough to eat for a long time—he and she and her household.
text.DRB: Who went and did according to the word of Elias: and he did eat, and she, and her house: and from that day

text.NRSV: The jar of meal was not emptied, neither did the jug of oil fail, according to the word of the Lord that he spoke by Elijah.
text.Rotherham: The jar of meal, did not waste,—and, the cruse of oil, did not fail,—according to the word of Yahweh, which he spake through Elijah.
text.KJV: And the barrel of meal wasted not, neither did the cruse of oil fail, according to the word of the Lord, which he spake by Elijah.
text.NABRE: The jar of flour did not go empty, nor the jug of oil run dry, according to the word of the Lord spoken through Elijah.
text.DRB: the pot of meal failed not, and the vessel of oil was not diminished, according to the word of our Lord, which he spake in the hand of Elias.

text.NRSV: After this the son of the woman, the mistress of the house, became ill; his illness was so severe that there was no breath left in him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after these things, that the son of the woman, owner of the house, fell sick,—yea it came to pass that his sickness was very severe, until no, breath, was left in him.
text.KJV: And it came to pass after these things, that the son of the woman, the mistress of the house, fell sick; and his sickness was so sore, that there was no breath left in him.
text.NABRE: Some time later the son of the woman, the owner of the house, fell sick, and his sickness grew more severe until he stopped breathing.
text.DRB: And it came to pass after these things, the son of the woman, the good wife of the house, fell sick, & the sickness was very vehement, so that there remained no breath in him,

text.NRSV: She then said to Elijah, “What have you against me, O man of God? Have you come to me to bring my sin to remembrance, and to cause the death of my son?”
text.Rotherham: Then said she unto Elijah, What have I in common with thee, O man of God? Hast thou come unto me, to call to remembrance mine iniquity, and to cause the death of my son?
text.KJV: And she said unto Elijah, What have I to do with thee, O thou man of God? art thou come unto me to call my sin to remembrance, and to slay my son?
text.NABRE: So she said to Elijah, “Why have you done this to me, man of God? Have you come to me to call attention to my guilt and to kill my son?”
text.DRB: She therefore said to Elias: what is to me and thee thou man of God? Comest thou unto me, that mine iniquities might be remembered, and thou mightest kill my son?

text.NRSV: But he said to her, “Give me your son.” He took him from her bosom, carried him up into the upper chamber where he was lodging, and laid him on his own bed.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto her—Give me thy son. And he took him out of her bosom, and carried him unto the upper room, where, he, was staying, and laid him upon his own bed.
text.KJV: And he said unto her, Give me thy son. And he took him out of her bosom, and carried him up into a loft, where he abode, and laid him upon his own bed.
text.NABRE: Elijah said to her, “Give me your son.” Taking him from her lap, he carried him to the upper room where he was staying, and laid him on his own bed.
text.DRB: And Elias said to her: Give me thy son. And he took him from her bosom, and carried him into the upper chamber where himself abode, and laid him upon his bed.

text.NRSV: He cried out to the Lord, “O Lord my God, have you brought calamity even upon the widow with whom I am staying, by causing the death of her son?”
text.Rotherham: Then cried he unto Yahweh, and said,—O Yahweh, my God! Even upon the widow with whom I am sojourning, hast thou brought misfortune by causing the death of her son?
text.KJV: And he cried unto the Lord, and said, O Lord my God, hast thou also brought evil upon the widow with whom I sojourn, by slaying her son?
text.NABRE: He called out to the Lord: “ Lord, my God, will you afflict even the widow with whom I am staying by killing her son?”
text.DRB: And he cried to our Lord, and said: O Lord my God, what, the widow also with whom I am after a sort sustained, hast thou afflicted, that thou wouldest kill her son?

text.NRSV: Then he stretched himself upon the child three times, and cried out to the Lord, “O Lord my God, let this child’s life come into him again.”
text.Rotherham: And he stretched himself out upon the boy, three times, and cried unto Yahweh, and said,—O Yahweh, my God! Let the life of this boy, I pray thee, come again within him.
text.KJV: And he stretched himself upon the child three times, and cried unto the Lord, and said, O Lord my God, I pray thee, let this child’s soul come into him again.
text.NABRE: Then he stretched himself out upon the child three times and he called out to the Lord: “ Lord, my God, let the life breath return to the body of this child.”
text.DRB: And he stretched forth, and measured himself upon the child three times, and he cried to our Lord, and said: O Lord my God, let the soul of this child, I beseech thee, return into his body.

text.NRSV: The Lord listened to the voice of Elijah; the life of the child came into him again, and he revived.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh hearkened unto the voice of Elijah,—and the life of the boy came again within him, and he lived.
text.KJV: And the Lord heard the voice of Elijah; and the soul of the child came into him again, and he revived.
text.NABRE: The Lord heard the prayer of Elijah; the life breath returned to the child’s body and he lived.
text.DRB: And our Lord heard the voice of Elias: and the soul of the child returned into him, and he revived.

text.NRSV: Elijah took the child, brought him down from the upper chamber into the house, and gave him to his mother; then Elijah said, “See, your son is alive.”
text.Rotherham: Then Elijah took the boy, and carried him down out of the upper room, into the house, and gave him to his mother,—and Elijah said, See! thy son liveth.
text.KJV: And Elijah took the child, and brought him down out of the chamber into the house, and delivered him unto his mother: and Elijah said, See, thy son liveth.
text.NABRE: Taking the child, Elijah carried him down into the house from the upper room and gave him to his mother. Elijah said, “See! Your son is alive.”
text.DRB: And Elias took the child, and brought him down from the upper chamber into the lower house, and delivered him to his mother, and said to her: Behold thy son liveth.

text.NRSV: So the woman said to Elijah, “Now I know that you are a man of God, and that the word of the Lord in your mouth is truth.”
text.Rotherham: And the woman said unto Elijah, Now, then I know, that, a man of God, thou art,—and that, the word of Yahweh, is in thy mouth, of a truth.
text.KJV: And the woman said to Elijah, Now by this I know that thou art a man of God, and that the word of the Lord in thy mouth is truth.
text.NABRE: The woman said to Elijah, “Now indeed I know that you are a man of God, and it is truly the word of the Lord that you speak.”
text.DRB: And the woman said to Elias: Now, in this I have known that thou art a man of God, and the word of our Lord in thy mouth is true.

text.NRSV: After many days the word of the Lord came to Elijah, in the third year of the drought, saying, “Go, present yourself to Ahab; I will send rain on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after many days, that, the word of Yahweh, came unto Elijah, in the third year, saying,—Go, shew thyself unto Ahab, that I may send rain upon the face of the ground.
text.KJV: And it came to pass after many days, that the word of the Lord came to Elijah in the third year, saying, Go, shew thyself unto Ahab; and I will send rain upon the earth.
text.NABRE: Chapter 18 Elijah Ends the Drought. Long afterward, in the third year, the word of the Lord came to Elijah: Go, present yourself to Ahab, that I may send rain upon the earth.
text.DRB: After many days the word of our Lord came to Elias, the third year, saying: Go, and shew thyself to Achab, that I may give rain upon the face of the earth.

text.NRSV: So Elijah went to present himself to Ahab. Now the famine was severe in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: And Elijah went, to show himself unto Ahab. Now, the famine, was severe in Samaria.
text.KJV: And Elijah went to shew himself unto Ahab. And there was a sore famine in Samaria.
text.NABRE: So Elijah went to present himself to Ahab. Now the famine in Samaria was severe,
text.DRB: Elias therefore went to shew himself to Achab: and there was sore famine in Samaria.

text.NRSV: Ahab summoned Obadiah, who was in charge of the palace. (Now Obadiah revered the Lord greatly;
text.Rotherham: And Ahab had called for Obadiah, who was over his house. Now, Obadiah, was one who revered Yahweh, exceedingly.
text.KJV: And Ahab called Obadiah, which was the governor of his house. (Now Obadiah feared the Lord greatly:
text.NABRE: and Ahab had summoned Obadiah, master of his palace, who greatly revered the Lord.
text.DRB: And Achab called Abdias the governor of his house: And Abdias did fear our Lord very much.

text.NRSV: when Jezebel was killing off the prophets of the Lord, Obadiah took a hundred prophets, hid them fifty to a cave, and provided them with bread and water.)
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass, when Jezebel was cutting off the prophets of Yahweh, that Obadiah took a hundred prophets, and hid them by fifties in caves, and sustained them, with bread and water.
text.KJV: For it was so, when Jezebel cut off the prophets of the Lord, that Obadiah took an hundred prophets, and hid them by fifty in a cave, and fed them with bread and water.)
text.NABRE: When Jezebel was slaughtering the prophets of the Lord, Obadiah took a hundred prophets, hid them away by fifties in caves, and supplied them with food and water.
text.DRB: For when Jezabel killed the Prophets of our Lord, he took an hundred Prophets, and hid them by fifty and fifty in caves, and fed them with bread and water.

text.NRSV: Then Ahab said to Obadiah, “Go through the land to all the springs of water and to all the wadis; perhaps we may find grass to keep the horses and mules alive, and not lose some of the animals.”
text.Rotherham: So then Ahab said unto Obadiah, Go, and let us pass throughout the land, unto all the fountains of water, and unto all the ravines, peradventure we may find grass, and save alive horse and mule, and not have more of the beasts cut off.
text.KJV: And Ahab said unto Obadiah, Go into the land, unto all fountains of water, and unto all brooks: peradventure we may find grass to save the horses and mules alive, that we lose not all the beasts.
text.NABRE: Ahab said to Obadiah, “Go through the land to all sources of water and to all the wadies. We may find grass and keep the horses and mules alive, so that we shall not have to slaughter any of the beasts.”
text.DRB: Achab therefore said to Abdias: Go into the land to all the fountains of waters, and into all valleys, if perhaps we may find grass, and save the horses and mules, and the beasts may not utterly perish.

text.NRSV: So they divided the land between them to pass through it; Ahab went one way by himself, and Obadiah went another way by himself.
text.Rotherham: So they divided to them the land, to pass through it,—Ahab, went one way, by himself, and, Obadiah, went another way, by himself.
text.KJV: So they divided the land between them to pass throughout it: Ahab went one way by himself, and Obadiah went another way by himself.
text.NABRE: Dividing the land to explore between them, Ahab went one way by himself, Obadiah another way by himself.
text.DRB: And they divided the countries between them, that they might go circuit about them: Achab went one way, and Abdias another way severally.

text.NRSV: As Obadiah was on the way, Elijah met him; Obadiah recognized him, fell on his face, and said, “Is it you, my lord Elijah?”
text.Rotherham: And so it was, as Obadiah was on the road, that lo! Elijah, met him,—and he recognised him, and fell upon his face, and said—Now art, thou, my lord Elijah?
text.KJV: And as Obadiah was in the way, behold, Elijah met him: and he knew him, and fell on his face, and said, Art thou that my lord Elijah?
text.NABRE: As Obadiah was on his way, Elijah met him. Recognizing him, Obadiah fell prostrate and asked, “Is it you, my lord Elijah?”
text.DRB: And when Abdias was in the way, Elias met him: who when he knew him, fell on his face, and said: My lord, art not thou Elias?

text.NRSV: He answered him, “It is I. Go, tell your lord, ‘Elijah is here.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said to him—I, am! Go, say to thy lord: Here, is, Elijah!
text.KJV: And he answered him, I am: go, tell thy lord, Behold, Elijah is here.
text.NABRE: He said to him, “Yes. Go tell your lord, ‘Elijah is here!’”
text.DRB: To whom he answered: I am. Go, and tell thy lord: Elias is here.

text.NRSV: And he said, “How have I sinned, that you would hand your servant over to Ahab, to kill me?
text.Rotherham: And he said—How have I sinned,—that, thou, art delivering thy servant into the hand of Ahab, to put me to death?
text.KJV: And he said, What have I sinned, that thou wouldest deliver thy servant into the hand of Ahab, to slay me?
text.NABRE: But Obadiah said, “What sin has your servant committed, that you are handing me over to Ahab to be killed?
text.DRB: And he said: What have I sinned, that thou deliverest me thy servant into the hand of Achab, that he may kill me?

text.NRSV: As the Lord your God lives, there is no nation or kingdom to which my lord has not sent to seek you; and when they would say, ‘He is not here,’ he would require an oath of the kingdom or nation, that they had not found you.
text.Rotherham: By the life of Yahweh thy God, there is no nation or kingdom, whither my lord hath not sent to seek thee, and, when they have said—Nay! he hath taken an oath of the kingdom or the nation, that no one could find thee;
text.KJV: As the Lord thy God liveth, there is no nation or kingdom, whither my lord hath not sent to seek thee: and when they said, He is not there; he took an oath of the kingdom and nation, that they found thee not.
text.NABRE: As the Lord, your God, lives, there is no nation or kingdom where my lord has not sent in search of you. When they replied, ‘He is not here,’ he made each kingdom and nation swear they could not find you.
text.DRB: Our Lord thy God liveth, there is no nation or Kingdom, whither my lord hath not sent to seek thee; and all answering: He is not here; he adjured all kingdoms and nations, for that thou wast not found.

text.NRSV: But now you say, ‘Go, tell your lord, “Elijah is here.”’
text.Rotherham: and, now, thou, art saying,—Go say to thy lord, Here, is, Elijah;
text.KJV: And now thou sayest, Go, tell thy lord, Behold, Elijah is here.
text.NABRE: And now you say, ‘Go tell your lord: Elijah is here!’
text.DRB: And now thou sayest to me: Go, and tell thy lord, Elias is here.

text.NRSV: As soon as I have gone from you, the spirit of the Lord will carry you I know not where; so, when I come and tell Ahab and he cannot find you, he will kill me, although I your servant have revered the Lord from my youth.
text.Rotherham: so shall it come to pass, as soon as, I, go from thee, that, the spirit of Yahweh, shall carry thee away—whither I know not, and so, when I go in to tell Ahab, and he cannot find thee, then will he slay me,—and yet, thy servant, hath revered Yahweh, from my youth.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, as soon as I am gone from thee, that the Spirit of the Lord shall carry thee whither I know not; and so when I come and tell Ahab, and he cannot find thee, he shall slay me: but I thy servant fear the Lord from my youth.
text.NABRE: After I leave you, the spirit of the Lord will carry you to some place I do not know, and when I go to inform Ahab and he does not find you, he will kill me—though your servant has revered the Lord from his youth!
text.DRB: And when I am departed from thee, the Spirit of our Lord will carry thee into a place, that I know not: and I entering in shall tell Achab, and not finding thee, he will kill me: and thy servant feareth our Lord from his infancy.

text.NRSV: Has it not been told my lord what I did when Jezebel killed the prophets of the Lord, how I hid a hundred of the Lord’s prophets fifty to a cave, and provided them with bread and water?
text.Rotherham: Was it not told my lord, what I did when Jezebel was slaying the prophets of Yahweh,—how I hid, of the prophets of Yahweh, a hundred men—by fifties—in caves, and sustained them with bread and water?
text.KJV: Was it not told my lord what I did when Jezebel slew the prophets of the Lord, how I hid an hundred men of the Lord’s prophets by fifty in a cave, and fed them with bread and water?
text.NABRE: Have you not been told, my lord, what I did when Jezebel was murdering the prophets of the Lord —that I hid a hundred of the prophets of the Lord, fifty each in caves, and supplied them with food and water?
text.DRB: Hath it not been told thee my lord, what I did when Jezabel killed the Prophets of our Lord, that I hid of the Prophets of our Lord an hundred men, by fifty and fifty in caves, and fed them with bread and water?

text.NRSV: Yet now you say, ‘Go, tell your lord, “Elijah is here”’; he will surely kill me.”
text.Rotherham: and yet, now, thou, art saying, Go, tell thy lord—Here, is, Elijah; and so he will slay me!
text.KJV: And now thou sayest, Go, tell thy lord, Behold, Elijah is here: and he shall slay me.
text.NABRE: And now you say, ‘Go tell your lord: Elijah is here!’ He will kill me!”
text.DRB: And now thou sayst: Go, and tell thy lord: Elias is here: that he may kill me?

text.NRSV: Elijah said, “As the Lord of hosts lives, before whom I stand, I will surely present myself to him today.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah—By the life of Yahweh of hosts, before whom I stand, to-day, will I show myself unto him.
text.KJV: And Elijah said, As the Lord of hosts liveth, before whom I stand, I will surely shew myself unto him to day.
text.NABRE: Elijah answered, “As the Lord of hosts lives, whom I serve, I will present myself to him today.”
text.DRB: And Elias said: The Lord of hosts liveth, before whose face I stand, this day will I appear to him.

text.NRSV: So Obadiah went to meet Ahab, and told him; and Ahab went to meet Elijah.
text.Rotherham: So Obadiah went to meet Ahab, and told him,—and, Ahab, went to meet, Elijah.
text.KJV: So Obadiah went to meet Ahab, and told him: and Ahab went to meet Elijah.
text.NABRE: So Obadiah went to meet Ahab and informed him, and Ahab came to meet Elijah.
text.DRB: Abdias therefore went to meet Achab, and told him: and Achab came to meet Elias.

text.NRSV: When Ahab saw Elijah, Ahab said to him, “Is it you, you troubler of Israel?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Ahab saw Elijah, that Ahab said unto him, Now, art, thou, troubling Israel?
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Ahab saw Elijah, that Ahab said unto him, Art thou he that troubleth Israel?
text.NABRE: When Ahab saw Elijah, he said to him, “Is it you, you disturber of Israel?”
text.DRB: And when he had seen him, he said: Art thou he that doest trouble Israel?

text.NRSV: He answered, “I have not troubled Israel; but you have, and your father’s house, because you have forsaken the commandments of the Lord and followed the Baals.
text.Rotherham: And he said—I have not troubled Israel, but, thou and the house of thy father,—in that ye have forsaken the commandments of Yahweh, and followed Baal.
text.KJV: And he answered, I have not troubled Israel; but thou, and thy father’s house, in that ye have forsaken the commandments of the Lord, and thou hast followed Baalim.
text.NABRE: He answered, “It is not I who disturb Israel, but you and your father’s house, by forsaking the commands of the Lord and you by following the Baals.
text.DRB: And he said: not I have troubled Israel, but thou, and the house of thy father, who have forsaken the commandments of our Lord, & have followed Baalim.

text.NRSV: Now therefore summon all Israel to me at Mount Carmel, with the four hundred fifty prophets of Baal and the four hundred prophets of Asherah, who eat at Jezebel’s table.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, send, gather unto me all Israel, unto Mount Carmel,—and the prophets of Baal, four hundred and fifty, and the prophets of the Sacred Stem, four hundred, who do eat at the table of Jezebel.
text.KJV: Now therefore send, and gather to me all Israel unto mount Carmel, and the prophets of Baal four hundred and fifty, and the prophets of the groves four hundred, which eat at Jezebel’s table.
text.NABRE: Now summon all Israel to me on Mount Carmel, as well as the four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal and the four hundred prophets of Asherah who eat at Jezebel’s table.”
text.DRB: Nevertheless send now, and gather unto me all Israel in the mount of Carmel, and the Prophets of Baal four hundred fifty, and the Prophets of the groves four hundred, which eat of Jezabel's table.

text.NRSV: So Ahab sent to all the Israelites, and assembled the prophets at Mount Carmel.
text.Rotherham: So Ahab sent among all the sons of Israel,—and gathered the prophets unto Mount Carmel.
text.KJV: So Ahab sent unto all the children of Israel, and gathered the prophets together unto mount Carmel.
text.NABRE: So Ahab summoned all the Israelites and had the prophets gather on Mount Carmel.
text.DRB: Achab sent to all the children of Israel, and gathered together the Prophets in the mount of Carmel.

text.NRSV: Elijah then came near to all the people, and said, “How long will you go limping with two different opinions? If the Lord is God, follow him; but if Baal, then follow him.” The people did not answer him a word.
text.Rotherham: And Elijah drew near unto all the people, and said—How long are ye limping on the two divided opinions? If, Yahweh, be GOD, follow, him, but, if, Baal, follow, him. But the people answered him, not a word.
text.KJV: And Elijah came unto all the people, and said, How long halt ye between two opinions? if the Lord be God, follow him: but if Baal, then follow him. And the people answered him not a word.
text.NABRE: Elijah approached all the people and said, “How long will you straddle the issue? If the Lord is God, follow him; if Baal, follow him.” But the people did not answer him.
text.DRB: And Elias coming to all the people, said: How long halt you on two sides? If our Lord be God, follow him: but if Baal, follow him. And the people did not answer him a word.

text.NRSV: Then Elijah said to the people, “I, even I only, am left a prophet of the Lord; but Baal’s prophets are four hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah unto the people—I, am left, Yahweh’s, only, prophet,—but, the prophets of Baal, are four hundred and fifty men.
text.KJV: Then said Elijah unto the people, I, even I only, remain a prophet of the Lord; but Baal’s prophets are four hundred and fifty men.
text.NABRE: So Elijah said to the people, “I am the only remaining prophet of the Lord, and there are four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal.
text.DRB: And Elias said again to the people: I only remain a Prophet of our Lord: and the Prophets of Baal are four hundred and fifty men.

text.NRSV: Let two bulls be given to us; let them choose one bull for themselves, cut it in pieces, and lay it on the wood, but put no fire to it; I will prepare the other bull and lay it on the wood, but put no fire to it.
text.Rotherham: Let there be given us, therefore, two bullocks, and let them choose for themselves one bullock, and cut it in pieces, and lay it upon the wood, but, fire, shall they not put,—then, I, will make ready the other bullock, and place upon the wood, but, fire, will I not put.
text.KJV: Let them therefore give us two bullocks; and let them choose one bullock for themselves, and cut it in pieces, and lay it on wood, and put no fire under: and I will dress the other bullock, and lay it on wood, and put no fire under:
text.NABRE: Give us two young bulls. Let them choose one, cut it into pieces, and place it on the wood, but start no fire. I shall prepare the other and place it on the wood, but shall start no fire.
text.DRB: Let two oxen be given us, and let them choose to themselves one oxe, and cutting it into pieces let them lay it upon wood, but put no fire under: and I will dress the other oxe, and will lay it upon wood, and put no fire under.

text.NRSV: Then you call on the name of your god and I will call on the name of the Lord; the god who answers by fire is indeed God.” All the people answered, “Well spoken!”
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye call on the name of your god, and, I, will call on the name of Yahweh, and it shall be, the God that respondeth by fire, he, is GOD. And all the people responded—Well spoken!
text.KJV: And call ye on the name of your gods, and I will call on the name of the Lord: and the God that answereth by fire, let him be God. And all the people answered and said, It is well spoken.
text.NABRE: You shall call upon the name of your gods, and I will call upon the name of the Lord. The God who answers with fire is God.” All the people answered, “We agree!”
text.DRB: Invocate ye the names of your gods, & I will invocate the name of my Lord: and the God that shall hear by fire, let the same be God. And all the people answering said, A very good proposition.

text.NRSV: Then Elijah said to the prophets of Baal, “Choose for yourselves one bull and prepare it first, for you are many; then call on the name of your god, but put no fire to it.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah to the prophets of Baal—Choose for yourselves one bullock, and make ye ready, first, for, ye, are many,—and call ye on the name of your god, but, fire, shall ye not put.
text.KJV: And Elijah said unto the prophets of Baal, Choose you one bullock for yourselves, and dress it first; for ye are many; and call on the name of your gods, but put no fire under.
text.NABRE: Elijah then said to the prophets of Baal, “Choose one young bull and prepare it first, for there are more of you. Call upon your gods, but do not start the fire.”
text.DRB: Elias therefore said to the Prophets of Baal: Choose you one oxe, and make it first, because you are many: and invocate the names of your gods, and put no fire under.

text.NRSV: So they took the bull that was given them, prepared it, and called on the name of Baal from morning until noon, saying, “O Baal, answer us!” But there was no voice, and no answer. They limped about the altar that they had made.
text.Rotherham: So they took the bullock which was given them, and made ready, and called on the name of Baal—from the morning even until the noon, saying—O Baal! answer us. But them was no voice, nor any that answered. And they leaped about by the altar which had been made.
text.KJV: And they took the bullock which was given them, and they dressed it, and called on the name of Baal from morning even until noon, saying, O Baal, hear us. But there was no voice, nor any that answered. And they leaped upon the altar which was made.
text.NABRE: Taking the young bull that was turned over to them, they prepared it and called upon Baal from morning to noon, saying, “Baal, answer us!” But there was no sound, and no one answering. And they hopped around the altar they had prepared.
text.DRB: Who when they had taken the oxe, which he gave them, they dressed it: and they invocated the name of Baal from morning until midday, saying: Baal hear us. And there was no voice, nor any that answered: and they leaped over the altar, that they had made.

text.NRSV: At noon Elijah mocked them, saying, “Cry aloud! Surely he is a god; either he is meditating, or he has wandered away, or he is on a journey, or perhaps he is asleep and must be awakened.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at noon, that Elijah mocked them, and said—Cry with a loud voice, for, a god, he is, either he hath, a meditation, or an occasion to retire, or he hath, a journey,—peradventure, he, sleepeth, and must be awaked.
text.KJV: And it came to pass at noon, that Elijah mocked them, and said, Cry aloud: for he is a god; either he is talking, or he is pursuing, or he is in a journey, or peradventure he sleepeth, and must be awaked.
text.NABRE: When it was noon, Elijah taunted them: “Call louder, for he is a god; he may be busy doing his business, or may be on a journey. Perhaps he is asleep and must be awakened.”
text.DRB: And when it was now midday, Elias jested at them, saying: Cry with a louder voice: for he is God, and perhaps he speaketh, or is in his inn, or in the way, or at the least he sleepeth, that he must be waked.

text.NRSV: Then they cried aloud, and, as was their custom, they cut themselves with swords and lances until the blood gushed out over them.
text.Rotherham: And they cried, with a loud voice, and cut themselves, after their custom, with swords, and with lances,—until the blood gushed out upon them.
text.KJV: And they cried aloud, and cut themselves after their manner with knives and lancets, till the blood gushed out upon them.
text.NABRE: They called out louder and slashed themselves with swords and spears according to their ritual until blood gushed over them.
text.DRB: They cried therefore with a loud voice, and cut themselves after their rite with knives and lancets, till they were all imbrued with blood.

text.NRSV: As midday passed, they raved on until the time of the offering of the oblation, but there was no voice, no answer, and no response.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when noon was passed, that although they prophesied until the offering up of the evening gift, yet was there no voice nor any that answered, nor any that hearkened.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when midday was past, and they prophesied until the time of the offering of the evening sacrifice, that there was neither voice, nor any to answer, nor any that regarded.
text.NABRE: Noon passed and they remained in a prophetic state until the time for offering sacrifice. But there was no sound, no one answering, no one listening.
text.DRB: And after the midday was past, and whilst they prophesied, the time was come, when they used to offer sacrifice, neither voice was heard, nor any did answer, nor attend them praying:

text.NRSV: Then Elijah said to all the people, “Come closer to me”; and all the people came closer to him. First he repaired the altar of the Lord that had been thrown down;
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah unto all the people: Draw near unto, me. And all the people drew near unto, him. Then repaired he the broken-down altar of Yahweh; yea Elijah took twelve stones, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Jacob,—
text.KJV: And Elijah said unto all the people, Come near unto me. And all the people came near unto him. And he repaired the altar of the Lord that was broken down.
text.NABRE: Then Elijah said to all the people, “Come here to me.” When they drew near to him, he repaired the altar of the Lord which had been destroyed.
text.DRB: Elias said to all the people: Come unto me. And the people coming to him, he repaired the altar of our Lord, that was destroyed.

text.NRSV: Elijah took twelve stones, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Jacob, to whom the word of the Lord came, saying, “Israel shall be your name”;
text.Rotherham: unto whom the word of Yahweh came, saying—Israel, shall be thy name;
text.KJV: And Elijah took twelve stones, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Jacob, unto whom the word of the Lord came, saying, Israel shall be thy name:
text.NABRE: He took twelve stones, for the number of tribes of the sons of Jacob, to whom the Lord had said: Israel shall be your name.
text.DRB: And he took twelve stones according to the number of the tribes of the children of Jacob, to whom the word of our Lord came, saying: Israel shall be thy name.

text.NRSV: with the stones he built an altar in the name of the Lord. Then he made a trench around the altar, large enough to contain two measures of seed.
text.Rotherham: and built, with the stones, an altar, in the name of Yahweh,—and he made a trench, as large as would contain two measures of seed, round about the altar;
text.KJV: And with the stones he built an altar in the name of the Lord: and he made a trench about the altar, as great as would contain two measures of seed.
text.NABRE: He built the stones into an altar to the name of the Lord, and made a trench around the altar large enough for two measures of grain.
text.DRB: And he built of the stones an altar in the name of our Lord: and he made a water gutter, as it were by two furrows round about the altar,

text.NRSV: Next he put the wood in order, cut the bull in pieces, and laid it on the wood.
text.Rotherham: and he put in order the wood,—and cut in pieces the bullock, and laid upon the wood. Then said he—Fill four pitchers with water, and pour out upon the ascending-sacrifice, and upon the wood. And they did so.
text.KJV: And he put the wood in order, and cut the bullock in pieces, and laid him on the wood, and said, Fill four barrels with water, and pour it on the burnt sacrifice, and on the wood.
text.NABRE: When he had arranged the wood, he cut up the young bull and laid it on the wood.
text.DRB: And he laid the wood in order, and divided the oxe in joints, and laid it upon the wood,

text.NRSV: He said, “Fill four jars with water and pour it on the burnt offering and on the wood.” Then he said, “Do it a second time”; and they did it a second time. Then he said, “Do it a third time”; and they did it a third time,
text.Rotherham: And he said—Do it the second time. And they did it the second time. And he said—Do it the third time. And they did it the third time.
text.KJV: And he said, Do it the second time. And they did it the second time. And he said, Do it the third time. And they did it the third time.
text.NABRE: He said, “Fill four jars with water and pour it over the burnt offering and over the wood.” “Do it again,” he said, and they did it again. “Do it a third time,” he said, and they did it a third time.
text.DRB: and said: Fill four buckets with water, and pour upon the holocaust, and upon the wood. And again he said: Do it also the second time. Who having done it the second time, he said: The third time also do the same. And they did so the third time,

text.NRSV: so that the water ran all around the altar, and filled the trench also with water.
text.Rotherham: And the water ran round about the altar,—moreover also, the trench, filled he with water.
text.KJV: And the water ran round about the altar; and he filled the trench also with water.
text.NABRE: The water flowed around the altar; even the trench was filled with the water.
text.DRB: and the waters ran about the altar, and the trough of the conduit was filled.

text.NRSV: At the time of the offering of the oblation, the prophet Elijah came near and said, “O Lord, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, let it be known this day that you are God in Israel, that I am your servant, and that I have done all these things at your bidding.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the time of the offering of the gift, that Elijah the prophet drew near, and said, O Yahweh, God of Abraham, Isaac and Israel! To-day, let it be known—That, thou, art God in Israel, and that, I, am thy servant,—and that, by thy word, have I done all these things.
text.KJV: And it came to pass at the time of the offering of the evening sacrifice, that Elijah the prophet came near, and said, Lord God of Abraham, Isaac, and of Israel, let it be known this day that thou art God in Israel, and that I am thy servant, and that I have done all these things at thy word.
text.NABRE: At the time for offering sacrifice, Elijah the prophet came forward and said, “ Lord, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, let it be known this day that you are God in Israel and that I am your servant and have done all these things at your command.
text.DRB: And when it was now time that the holocaust should be offered, Elias the Prophet coming said: Lord God of Abraham, and Isaac, and Israel, shew this day that thou art the God of Israel, and I thy servant, and that according to thy commandment I have done all these things.

text.NRSV: Answer me, O Lord, answer me, so that this people may know that you, O Lord, are God, and that you have turned their hearts back.”
text.Rotherham: Answer me, O Yahweh! answer me, That this people may know, that, thou—Yahweh, art GOD,—so shalt, thou thyself, have turned their heart back again.
text.KJV: Hear me, O Lord, hear me, that this people may know that thou art the Lord God, and that thou hast turned their heart back again.
text.NABRE: Answer me, Lord! Answer me, that this people may know that you, Lord, are God and that you have turned their hearts back to you.”
text.DRB: Hear me Lord, hear me that this people may learn, that thou art our Lord God, and thou hast converted their heart again.

text.NRSV: Then the fire of the Lord fell and consumed the burnt offering, the wood, the stones, and the dust, and even licked up the water that was in the trench.
text.Rotherham: Then fell fire from Yahweh, and consumed the ascending-sacrifice, and the wood, and the stones, and the dust,—even the water that was in the trench, it licked up.
text.KJV: Then the fire of the Lord fell, and consumed the burnt sacrifice, and the wood, and the stones, and the dust, and licked up the water that was in the trench.
text.NABRE: The Lord ’s fire came down and devoured the burnt offering, wood, stones, and dust, and lapped up the water in the trench.
text.DRB: And the fire of our Lord fell, and devoured the holocaust, and the wood, and the stones, licking also the dust, and the water, that was in the water gutter.

text.NRSV: When all the people saw it, they fell on their faces and said, “The Lord indeed is God; the Lord indeed is God.”
text.Rotherham: And, when all the people saw it, they fell upon their faces,—and said, Yahweh, he, is GOD, Yahweh, he, is GOD.
text.KJV: And when all the people saw it, they fell on their faces: and they said, The Lord, he is the God; the Lord, he is the God.
text.NABRE: Seeing this, all the people fell prostrate and said, “The Lord is God! The Lord is God!”
text.DRB: Which when all the people had seen, they fell on their face, and said: Our Lord he is God, our Lord he is God.

text.NRSV: Elijah said to them, “Seize the prophets of Baal; do not let one of them escape.” Then they seized them; and Elijah brought them down to the Wadi Kishon, and killed them there.
text.Rotherham: And Elijah said unto them—Seize ye the prophets of Baal—let not, a man, of them escape. So they seized them. And Elijah took them down unto the ravine of Kishon, and slaughtered them there.
text.KJV: And Elijah said unto them, Take the prophets of Baal; let not one of them escape. And they took them: and Elijah brought them down to the brook Kishon, and slew them there.
text.NABRE: Then Elijah said to them, “Seize the prophets of Baal. Let none of them escape!” They seized them, and Elijah brought them down to the Wadi Kishon and there he slaughtered them.
text.DRB: And Elias said to them: Apprehend the Prophets of Baal, and let not one escape of them. Whom when they had taken, Elias brought them to the Torrent Cison, and killed them there.

text.NRSV: Elijah said to Ahab, “Go up, eat and drink; for there is a sound of rushing rain.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah unto Ahab, Go up, eat and drink,—for there is a sound of a downpour of rain.
text.KJV: And Elijah said unto Ahab, Get thee up, eat and drink; for there is a sound of abundance of rain.
text.NABRE: Elijah then said to Ahab, “Go up, eat and drink, for there is the sound of a heavy rain.”
text.DRB: And Elias said to Achab: Go up, eat and drink: because there is sound of much rain.

text.NRSV: So Ahab went up to eat and to drink. Elijah went up to the top of Carmel; there he bowed himself down upon the earth and put his face between his knees.
text.Rotherham: So Ahab went up, to eat and to drink,—but, Elijah, went up to the top of Carmel, and bowed himself to the earth, and put his face between his knees.
text.KJV: So Ahab went up to eat and to drink. And Elijah went up to the top of Carmel; and he cast himself down upon the earth, and put his face between his knees,
text.NABRE: So Ahab went up to eat and drink, while Elijah went up to the top of Carmel, crouched down to the earth, and put his head between his knees.
text.DRB: Achab went up to eat and drink: and Elias went up into the top of Carmel, and flat on the earth put his face between his knees,

text.NRSV: He said to his servant, “Go up now, look toward the sea.” He went up and looked, and said, “There is nothing.” Then he said, “Go again seven times.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto his young man—Go up, I pray thee, and look about in the direction of the sea. So he went up, and looked about, and said—There is, nothing whatever. Then said he—Go again, seven times.
text.KJV: And said to his servant, Go up now, look toward the sea. And he went up, and looked, and said, There is nothing. And he said, Go again seven times.
text.NABRE: He said to his servant, “Go up and look out to sea.” He went up and looked, but reported, “There is nothing.” Seven times he said, “Go look again!”
text.DRB: and he said to his servant: Go up, and look toward the sea. Who when he was gone up, and had looked, he said: There is nothing. And again he said to him: Return seven times.

text.NRSV: At the seventh time he said, “Look, a little cloud no bigger than a person’s hand is rising out of the sea.” Then he said, “Go say to Ahab, ‘Harness your chariot and go down before the rain stops you.’”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the seventh time, that he said, Lo! a little cloud, like a man’s hand coming up from the sea. Then he said—Go up, say unto Ahab—Harness, and get thee down, lest the rain shut thee in.
text.KJV: And it came to pass at the seventh time, that he said, Behold, there ariseth a little cloud out of the sea, like a man’s hand. And he said, Go up, say unto Ahab, Prepare thy chariot, and get thee down, that the rain stop thee not.
text.NABRE: And the seventh time the youth reported, “There is a cloud as small as a man’s hand rising from the sea.” Elijah said, “Go and say to Ahab, ‘Harness up and go down the mountain before the rain stops you.’”
text.DRB: And in the seventh time: Behold a little cloud as it were a man's foot came up from the sea. Who said: Go up and say to Achab: Yoke thy chariot and go down, lest the rain prevent thee.

text.NRSV: In a little while the heavens grew black with clouds and wind; there was a heavy rain. Ahab rode off and went to Jezreel.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, meanwhile, that, the heavens, had enshrouded themselves with clouds and wind, and there came a great rain, and Ahab rode, and went to Jezreel;
text.KJV: And it came to pass in the mean while, that the heaven was black with clouds and wind, and there was a great rain. And Ahab rode, and went to Jezreel.
text.NABRE: All at once the sky grew dark with clouds and wind, and a heavy rain fell. Ahab mounted his chariot and headed for Jezreel.
text.DRB: And when he turned himself hither and thither, behold the heavens were darkened, and clouds, and wind, and there fell great rain. Achab therefore going up went into Jezrahel:

text.NRSV: But the hand of the Lord was on Elijah; he girded up his loins and ran in front of Ahab to the entrance of Jezreel.
text.Rotherham: but, the hand of Yahweh, came upon Elijah, so he girded his loins,—and ran before Ahab, until thou enterest Jezreel.
text.KJV: And the hand of the Lord was on Elijah; and he girded up his loins, and ran before Ahab to the entrance of Jezreel.
text.NABRE: But the hand of the Lord was on Elijah. He girded up his clothing and ran before Ahab as far as the approaches to Jezreel.
text.DRB: and the hand of our Lord was made upon Elias, and his loins girded he ran before Achab, until he came into Jezrahel.

text.NRSV: Ahab told Jezebel all that Elijah had done, and how he had killed all the prophets with the sword.
text.Rotherham: And Ahab told Jezebel, all that Elijah had done,—and withal, how he had slain all the prophets with the sword.
text.KJV: And Ahab told Jezebel all that Elijah had done, and withal how he had slain all the prophets with the sword.
text.NABRE: Chapter 19 Flight to Horeb. Ahab told Jezebel all that Elijah had done—that he had murdered all the prophets by the sword.
text.DRB: And Achab told Jezabel all things that Elias had done, and how he had killed all the prophets with the sword.

text.NRSV: Then Jezebel sent a messenger to Elijah, saying, “So may the gods do to me, and more also, if I do not make your life like the life of one of them by this time tomorrow.”
text.Rotherham: Then sent Jezebel a messenger unto Elijah, saying,—So, let the gods do, and, so, let them add, if, by this time to-morrow, I make not thy life as the life of one of them.
text.KJV: Then Jezebel sent a messenger unto Elijah, saying, So let the gods do to me, and more also, if I make not thy life as the life of one of them by to morrow about this time.
text.NABRE: Jezebel then sent a messenger to Elijah and said, “May the gods do thus to me and more, if by this time tomorrow I have not done with your life what was done to each of them.”
text.DRB: And Jezabel sent a message to Elias, saying: These things do the gods to me, and these add they, if this hour tomorrow I make not thy soul as the soul of one of them.

text.NRSV: Then he was afraid; he got up and fled for his life, and came to Beer-sheba, which belongs to Judah; he left his servant there.
text.Rotherham: And, when he saw [that], he arose, and went for his life, and came to Beer-sheba, which belongeth to Judah,—and left his servant there.
text.KJV: And when he saw that, he arose, and went for his life, and came to Beer–sheba, which belongeth to Judah, and left his servant there.
text.NABRE: Elijah was afraid and fled for his life, going to Beer-sheba of Judah. He left his servant there
text.DRB: Elias therefore was afraid, and rising he went whither soever his will carried him: and he came into Bersabee of Juda, and left his servant there,

text.NRSV: But he himself went a day’s journey into the wilderness, and came and sat down under a solitary broom tree. He asked that he might die: “It is enough; now, O Lord, take away my life, for I am no better than my ancestors.”
text.Rotherham: But, he himself, went into the wilderness a day’s journey, and came and sat down under a certain shrub,—and asked for his life, that he might die, and said—Enough, now, O Yahweh! take away my life, for, no better, am, I, than, my fathers.
text.KJV: But he himself went a day’s journey into the wilderness, and came and sat down under a juniper tree: and he requested for himself that he might die; and said, It is enough; now, O Lord, take away my life; for I am not better than my fathers.
text.NABRE: and went a day’s journey into the wilderness, until he came to a solitary broom tree and sat beneath it. He prayed for death: “Enough, Lord! Take my life, for I am no better than my ancestors.”
text.DRB: and went forward into the desert, one day's journey. And when he was come, and sat under a juniper tree, he desired for his soul to die, and said: It sufficeth me Lord, take my soul: for I am not better than my fathers.

text.NRSV: He lay down under the broom tree and fell asleep. Suddenly an angel touched him and said to him, “Get up and eat.”
text.Rotherham: And, as he lay, and slept, under a certain shrub, lo! a messenger, touching him, who said to him—Rise, eat.
text.KJV: And as he lay and slept under a juniper tree, behold, then an angel touched him, and said unto him, Arise and eat.
text.NABRE: He lay down and fell asleep under the solitary broom tree, but suddenly a messenger touched him and said, “Get up and eat!”
text.DRB: And he cast himself down, & slept in the shadow of the juniper tree: & behold an Angel of our Lord touched him, and said to him: Arise, and eat.

text.NRSV: He looked, and there at his head was a cake baked on hot stones, and a jar of water. He ate and drank, and lay down again.
text.Rotherham: So he looked about, and lo! at his head, a cake baked on hot stones, and a cruse of water,—and he did eat and drink, and then went back and lay down.
text.KJV: And he looked, and, behold, there was a cake baken on the coals, and a cruse of water at his head. And he did eat and drink, and laid him down again.
text.NABRE: He looked and there at his head was a hearth cake and a jug of water. After he ate and drank, he lay down again,
text.DRB: He looked, and behold at his head hearth-baked bread, and a vessel of water: he therefore did eat, & drink, & he slept again.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord came a second time, touched him, and said, “Get up and eat, otherwise the journey will be too much for you.”
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh came again a second time, and touched him, and said—Rise, eat,—because, too much for thee, is the journey.
text.KJV: And the angel of the Lord came again the second time, and touched him, and said, Arise and eat; because the journey is too great for thee.
text.NABRE: but the angel of the Lord came back a second time, touched him, and said, “Get up and eat or the journey will be too much for you!”
text.DRB: And the Angel of our lord returned the second time, & touched him, & said to him: Arise, eat: for thou hast yet a great way to go.

text.NRSV: He got up, and ate and drank; then he went in the strength of that food forty days and forty nights to Horeb the mount of God.
text.Rotherham: So he arose, and did eat and drink,—and journeyed, in the strength of that eating, forty days and forty nights, as far as the mountain of God—Horeb.
text.KJV: And he arose, and did eat and drink, and went in the strength of that meat forty days and forty nights unto Horeb the mount of God.
text.NABRE: He got up, ate, and drank; then strengthened by that food, he walked forty days and forty nights to the mountain of God, Horeb.
text.DRB: Who when he was risen, did eat & drink, and walked in the strength of that meat forty days, & forty nights, unto the mount of God, Horeb.

text.NRSV: At that place he came to a cave, and spent the night there. Then the word of the Lord came to him, saying, “What are you doing here, Elijah?”
text.Rotherham: And he entered, there, into a cave, and lodged there,—and lo! the word of Yahweh [came] unto him, and said to him, What doest thou here, Elijah?
text.KJV: And he came thither unto a cave, and lodged there; and, behold, the word of the Lord came to him, and he said unto him, What doest thou here, Elijah?
text.NABRE: There he came to a cave, where he took shelter. But the word of the Lord came to him: Why are you here, Elijah?
text.DRB: And when he was come thither, he tarried in a cave, and behold the word of our Lord unto him, and he said to him: What doest thou here Elias?

text.NRSV: He answered, “I have been very zealous for the Lord, the God of hosts; for the Israelites have forsaken your covenant, thrown down your altars, and killed your prophets with the sword. I alone am left, and they are seeking my life, to take it away.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Very jealous, have I been, for Yahweh, God of hosts, because the sons of Israel have forsaken thy covenant, thine altars, have they thrown down, thy prophets, also have they slain with the sword,—and, I alone, am left, and they have sought my life, to take it.
text.KJV: And he said, I have been very jealous for the Lord God of hosts: for the children of Israel have forsaken thy covenant, thrown down thine altars, and slain thy prophets with the sword; and I, even I only, am left; and they seek my life, to take it away.
text.NABRE: He answered: “I have been most zealous for the Lord, the God of hosts, but the Israelites have forsaken your covenant. They have destroyed your altars and murdered your prophets by the sword. I alone remain, and they seek to take my life.”
text.DRB: But he answered: With zeal have I been zealous for our Lord the God of hosts, because the children of Israel have forsaken thy covenant: thy altars have they destroyed, and thy Prophets they have slain with the sword, and I alone am left, and they seek my life to take it away.

text.NRSV: He said, “Go out and stand on the mountain before the Lord, for the Lord is about to pass by.” Now there was a great wind, so strong that it was splitting mountains and breaking rocks in pieces before the Lord, but the Lord was not in the wind; and after the wind an earthquake, but the Lord was not in the earthquake;
text.Rotherham: And he said—Go forth to-morrow, and stand in the mountain, before Yahweh. And lo! Yahweh, passing by, and a great and strong wind—rending the mountains, and breaking in pieces the crags, before Yahweh, Not, in the wind, was, Yahweh,—And, after the wind, an, earthquake, Not, in the earthquake, was, Yahweh;
text.KJV: And he said, Go forth, and stand upon the mount before the Lord. And, behold, the Lord passed by, and a great and strong wind rent the mountains, and brake in pieces the rocks before the Lord; but the Lord was not in the wind: and after the wind an earthquake; but the Lord was not in the earthquake:
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said: Go out and stand on the mountain before the Lord; the Lord will pass by. There was a strong and violent wind rending the mountains and crushing rocks before the Lord —but the Lord was not in the wind; after the wind, an earthquake—but the Lord was not in the earthquake;
text.DRB: And he said to him: Come forth, and stand in the mount before our Lord: and behold our Lord passeth, and a great wind and strong, overthrowing mountains and breaking rocks before our Lord: not in the wind is our Lord, and after the wind an earth quake: not in the earth quake is our Lord,

text.NRSV: and after the earthquake a fire, but the Lord was not in the fire; and after the fire a sound of sheer silence.
text.Rotherham: And, after the earthquake, a, fire, Not, in the fire, was, Yahweh,—And, after the fire, the voice of a gentle whisper.
text.KJV: And after the earthquake a fire; but the Lord was not in the fire: and after the fire a still small voice.
text.NABRE: after the earthquake, fire—but the Lord was not in the fire; after the fire, a light silent sound.
text.DRB: and after the earth quake fire: not in the fire is our Lord, & after the fire a whistling of a gentle wind.

text.NRSV: When Elijah heard it, he wrapped his face in his mantle and went out and stood at the entrance of the cave. Then there came a voice to him that said, “What are you doing here, Elijah?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Elijah heard it, he wrapped his face in his mantle, and went forth, and stood at the entrance of the cave,—and lo! unto him, a voice; and it said—What doest thou here, Elijah?
text.KJV: And it was so, when Elijah heard it, that he wrapped his face in his mantle, and went out, and stood in the entering in of the cave. And, behold, there came a voice unto him, and said, What doest thou here, Elijah?
text.NABRE: When he heard this, Elijah hid his face in his cloak and went out and stood at the entrance of the cave. A voice said to him, Why are you here, Elijah?
text.DRB: Which when Elias had heard, he covered his face with his mantle, and coming forth stood in the door of the cave, and behold a voice unto him, saying: What doest thou here Elias?

text.NRSV: He answered, “I have been very zealous for the Lord, the God of hosts; for the Israelites have forsaken your covenant, thrown down your altars, and killed your prophets with the sword. I alone am left, and they are seeking my life, to take it away.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Very jealous, have I been, for Yahweh, God of hosts, because the sons of Israel have forsaken thy covenant, thine altars, have they thrown down, thy prophets also, have they slain with the sword,—and, I alone, am left, and they rove sought my life, to take it.
text.KJV: And he said, I have been very jealous for the Lord God of hosts: because the children of Israel have forsaken thy covenant, thrown down thine altars, and slain thy prophets with the sword; and I, even I only, am left; and they seek my life, to take it away.
text.NABRE: He replied, “I have been most zealous for the Lord, the God of hosts, but the Israelites have forsaken your covenant. They have destroyed your altars and murdered your prophets by the sword. I alone remain, and they seek to take my life.”
text.DRB: And he answered: With zeal have I been zealous for our Lord the God of hosts: because the children of Israel have forsaken thy covenant: thine altars they have destroyed, and thy Prophets they have slain with the sword, and I alone am left, and they seek my life to take it away.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to him, “Go, return on your way to the wilderness of Damascus; when you arrive, you shall anoint Hazael as king over Aram.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto him, Go, return to thy way, towards the wilderness of Damascus,—and, when thou enterest, then shalt thou anoint Hazael to be king over Syria;
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto him, Go, return on thy way to the wilderness of Damascus: and when thou comest, anoint Hazael to be king over Syria:
text.NABRE: The Lord said to him: Go back! Take the desert road to Damascus. When you arrive, you shall anoint Hazael as king of Aram.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him: Go, and return into thy way by the desert of Damascus: and when thou art come thither thou shalt anoint Hazael king over Syria,

text.NRSV: Also you shall anoint Jehu son of Nimshi as king over Israel; and you shall anoint Elisha son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah as prophet in your place.
text.Rotherham: And, Jehu son of Minshi, shalt thou anoint to be king over Israel,—and, Elisha son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah, shalt thou anoint to be prophet in thy stead:
text.KJV: And Jehu the son of Nimshi shalt thou anoint to be king over Israel: and Elisha the son of Shaphat of Abel–meholah shalt thou anoint to be prophet in thy room.
text.NABRE: You shall also anoint Jehu, son of Nimshi, as king of Israel, and Elisha, son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah, as prophet to succeed you.
text.DRB: and Jehu the son of Namsi thou shalt anoint king over Israel: and Eliseus the son of Saphat, which is of Abelmeula, thou shalt anoint prophet for thee.

text.NRSV: Whoever escapes from the sword of Hazael, Jehu shall kill; and whoever escapes from the sword of Jehu, Elisha shall kill.
text.Rotherham: Then shall it come to pass, that—him that escapeth the sword of Hazael, shall, Jehu, slay,—and, him that escapeth the sword of Jehu, shall, Elisha, slay;
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, that him that escapeth the sword of Hazael shall Jehu slay: and him that escapeth from the sword of Jehu shall Elisha slay.
text.NABRE: Anyone who escapes the sword of Hazael, Jehu will kill. Anyone who escapes the sword of Jehu, Elisha will kill.
text.DRB: And it shall be, whosoever shall escape the sword of Hazael, him Jehu shall kill: and whosoever shall escape the sword of Jehu, him shall Eliseus kill.

text.NRSV: Yet I will leave seven thousand in Israel, all the knees that have not bowed to Baal, and every mouth that has not kissed him.”
text.Rotherham: Yet will I leave remaining in Israel, seven thousand,—all knees which have not bowed to Baal, and all mouths which have not kissed to him.
text.KJV: Yet I have left me seven thousand in Israel, all the knees which have not bowed unto Baal, and every mouth which hath not kissed him.
text.NABRE: But I will spare seven thousand in Israel—every knee that has not bent to Baal, every mouth that has not kissed him.
text.DRB: And I will leave me in Israel seven thousand men, whose knees have not been bowed before Baal, and every mouth, that hath not adored him kissing his hands.

text.NRSV: So he set out from there, and found Elisha son of Shaphat, who was plowing. There were twelve yoke of oxen ahead of him, and he was with the twelfth. Elijah passed by him and threw his mantle over him.
text.Rotherham: So he departed from thence, and found Elisha son of Shaphat, as he was plowing, with twelve yoke of oxen before him, he being with the twelfth,—so Elijah crossed over unto him, and cast his mantle towards him;
text.KJV: So he departed thence, and found Elisha the son of Shaphat, who was plowing with twelve yoke of oxen before him, and he with the twelfth: and Elijah passed by him, and cast his mantle upon him.
text.NABRE: Elijah set out, and came upon Elisha, son of Shaphat, as he was plowing with twelve yoke of oxen; he was following the twelfth. Elijah went over to him and threw his cloak on him.
