text.NABRE: So Micaiah said: “I see all Israel scattered on the mountains, like sheep without a shepherd, And the Lord saying, These have no master! Let each of them go back home in peace.”
text.DRB: And he said: I saw all Israel dispersed in the mountains, as sheep not having a shepherd, and our Lord said: These have no master, let every man return into his house in peace.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “Did I not tell you that he would not prophesy anything favorable about me, but only disaster?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king of Israel, unto Jehoshaphat,—Did I not say unto thee, He will not be moved to prophesy concerning me anything good, only calamity.
text.KJV: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat, Did I not tell thee that he would prophesy no good concerning me, but evil?
text.NABRE: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “Did I not tell you, he does not prophesy good about me, but only evil?”
text.DRB: (Therefore the king of Israel said to Josaphat: Did I not tell thee, that he doth not Prophesy me good, but always evil?)

text.NRSV: Then Micaiah said, “Therefore hear the word of the Lord: I saw the Lord sitting on his throne, with all the host of heaven standing beside him to the right and to the left of him.
text.Rotherham: Then he said, Therefore, hear thou the word of Yahweh,—I saw Yahweh, sitting upon his throne, and all the host of the heavens, standing by him, on his right hand, and on his left.
text.KJV: And he said, Hear thou therefore the word of the Lord: I saw the Lord sitting on his throne, and all the host of heaven standing by him on his right hand and on his left.
text.NABRE: Micaiah continued: “Therefore hear the word of the Lord: I saw the Lord seated on his throne, with the whole host of heaven standing to his right and to his left.
text.DRB: But he adding, said: Therefore hear the word of our Lord: I saw our Lord sitting upon his throne, and all the host of heaven assisting him on the right hand and on the left:

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, ‘Who will entice Ahab, so that he may go up and fall at Ramoth-gilead?’ Then one said one thing, and another said another,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said, Who will persuade Ahab, that he may go up and fall, at Ramoth-gilead? And, one, said in this manner, and, another, said in that manner.
text.KJV: And the Lord said, Who shall persuade Ahab, that he may go up and fall at Ramoth–gilead? And one said on this manner, and another said on that manner.
text.NABRE: The Lord asked: Who will deceive Ahab, so that he will go up and fall on Ramoth-gilead? And one said this, another that,
text.DRB: and our Lord said: Who shall deceive Achab the king of Israel, that he may go up, and fall in Ramoth Galaad? And one saith these manner of words, and another otherwise.

text.NRSV: until a spirit came forward and stood before the Lord, saying, ‘I will entice him.’
text.Rotherham: Then came forth a spirit, and stood before Yahweh, and said—I, will persuade him.
text.KJV: And there came forth a spirit, and stood before the Lord, and said, I will persuade him.
text.NABRE: until this spirit came forth and stood before the Lord, saying, ‘I will deceive him.’ The Lord asked: How?
text.DRB: And there came forth a spirit, and stood before our Lord, and said: I will deceive him. To whom our Lord spake: Wherein?

text.NRSV: ‘How?’ the Lord asked him. He replied, ‘I will go out and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets.’ Then the Lord said, ‘You are to entice him, and you shall succeed; go out and do it.’
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto him—Wherewith? And he said—I will go forth, and become a spirit of falsehood in the mouth of all his prophets,—So he said,—Thou mayest persuade, yea and prevail, go forth, and do so.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto him, Wherewith? And he said, I will go forth, and I will be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And he said, Thou shalt persuade him, and prevail also: go forth, and do so.
text.NABRE: He answered, ‘I will go forth and become a lying spirit in the mouths of all his prophets.’ The Lord replied: You shall succeed in deceiving him. Go forth and do this.
text.DRB: And he said: I will go forth, and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And our Lord said: Thou shalt deceive, and shalt prevail: go forth, and do so.

text.NRSV: So you see, the Lord has put a lying spirit in the mouth of all these your prophets; the Lord has decreed disaster for you.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! Yahweh hath suffered a spirit of falsehood to be put into the mouth of all these thy prophets. But, Yahweh himself, hath spoken concerning thee, calamity.
text.KJV: Now therefore, behold, the Lord hath put a lying spirit in the mouth of all these thy prophets, and the Lord hath spoken evil concerning thee.
text.NABRE: So now, the Lord has put a lying spirit in the mouths of all these prophets of yours; the Lord himself has decreed evil against you.”
text.DRB: Now therefore behold our Lord hath given the spirit of lying in the mouth of all thy prophets, that are here, and our Lord hath spoken evil against thee.

text.NRSV: Then Zedekiah son of Chenaanah came up to Micaiah, struck him on the cheek, and said, “Which way did the spirit of the Lord go from me to speak to you?”
text.Rotherham: Then drew near Zedekiah, son of Chenaanah, and smote Micaiah on the cheek,—and said—Where then passed the Spirit of Yahweh, from me, to speak unto thee?
text.KJV: But Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah went near, and smote Micaiah on the cheek, and said, Which way went the Spirit of the Lord from me to speak unto thee?
text.NABRE: Thereupon Zedekiah, son of Chenaanah, came up and struck Micaiah on the cheek, saying, “Has the spirit of the Lord, then, left me to speak with you?”
text.DRB: And Sedecias the son of Chanaana came, and smote Micheas on the cheek, and said: Hath the Spirit of our Lord left me, and hath it spoken to thee?

text.NRSV: Micaiah replied, “You will find out on that day when you go in to hide in an inner chamber.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Micaiah, Lo! thou art about to see, on that day,—when thou enterest a chamber within a chamber, to hide thyself.
text.KJV: And Micaiah said, Behold, thou shalt see in that day, when thou shalt go into an inner chamber to hide thyself.
text.NABRE: Micaiah said, “You shall find out on the day you go into an inner room to hide.”
text.DRB: And Micheas said: Thou shalt see in that day, when thou shalt enter into thy chamber, within the chamber to be hid.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel then ordered, “Take Micaiah, and return him to Amon the governor of the city and to Joash the king’s son,
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said, Take Micaiah, and carry him back unto Amon captain of the city,—and unto Joash son of the king;
text.KJV: And the king of Israel said, Take Micaiah, and carry him back unto Amon the governor of the city, and to Joash the king’s son;
text.NABRE: The king of Israel then said, “Seize Micaiah and take him back to Amon, prefect of the city, and to Joash, the king’s son,
text.DRB: And the king of Israel said: Take Micheas, & let him tarry with Amon the governor of the city, and with Joas the son of Amelech,

text.NRSV: and say, ‘Thus says the king: Put this fellow in prison, and feed him on reduced rations of bread and water until I come in peace.’”
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt say—Thus, saith the king, Put this man into the prison,—and let him eat the bread of oppression, with the water of oppression, until I enter in peace.
text.KJV: And say, Thus saith the king, Put this fellow in the prison, and feed him with bread of affliction and with water of affliction, until I come in peace.
text.NABRE: and say, ‘This is the king’s order: Put this man in prison and feed him scanty rations of bread and water until I come back in safety.’”
text.DRB: and tell them: Thus saith the king: Cast this man into prison, and feed him with bread of tribulation, and water of distress, till I return in peace.

text.NRSV: Micaiah said, “If you return in peace, the Lord has not spoken by me.” And he said, “Hear, you peoples, all of you!”
text.Rotherham: Then said Micaiah, If thou, return, in peace, Yahweh hath not spoken by me. And he said, Hear, ye peoples, all of you!
text.KJV: And Micaiah said, If thou return at all in peace, the Lord hath not spoken by me. And he said, Hearken, O people, every one of you.
text.NABRE: But Micaiah said, “If you return in safety, the Lord has not spoken through me.” (He also said, “Hear, O peoples, all of you.”)
text.DRB: And Micheas said: If thou return in peace, our Lord hath not spoken in me. And he said: Hear all ye peoples.

text.NRSV: So the king of Israel and King Jehoshaphat of Judah went up to Ramoth-gilead.
text.Rotherham: So the king of Israel went up, with Jehoshaphat king of Judah, unto Ramoth-gilead.
text.KJV: So the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah went up to Ramoth–gilead.
text.NABRE: Ahab at Ramoth-gilead. The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, went up to Ramoth-gilead,
text.DRB: Therefore the king of Israel went up, and Josaphat the king of Juda into Ramoth Galaad.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “I will disguise myself and go into battle, but you wear your robes.” So the king of Israel disguised himself and went into the battle.
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat—I am about to disguise myself, and enter into the battle, thou, therefore, put on thy robes. So the king of Israel disguised himself, and entered into the battle.
text.KJV: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat, I will disguise myself, and enter into the battle; but put thou on thy robes. And the king of Israel disguised himself, and went into the battle.
text.NABRE: and the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “I will disguise myself and go into battle, but you put on your own robes.” So the king of Israel disguised himself and entered the battle.
text.DRB: The king of Israel therefore said to Josaphat: Take armour, and go into the battle, and put on thine own garments. Moreover the king of Israel changed his habit, and went into the battle.

text.NRSV: Now the king of Aram had commanded the thirty-two captains of his chariots, “Fight with no one small or great, but only with the king of Israel.”
text.Rotherham: Now, the king of Syria, had commanded the captains of chariots which he had, thirty and two, saying, Ye shall not fight with small or great,—save with the king of Israel alone.
text.KJV: But the king of Syria commanded his thirty and two captains that had rule over his chariots, saying, Fight neither with small nor great, save only with the king of Israel.
text.NABRE: In the meantime the king of Aram had given his thirty-two chariot commanders the order, “Do not fight with anyone, great or small, except the king of Israel alone.”
text.DRB: And the king of Syria had commanded the princes of his chariots thirty and two, saying: You shall not fight against any lesser, or greater, but against the king of Israel only.

text.NRSV: When the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat, they said, “It is surely the king of Israel.” So they turned to fight against him; and Jehoshaphat cried out.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the chariot-captains saw Jehoshaphat, that, they, said: Surely it is, the king of Israel! But, when they turned aside against him to fight, Jehoshaphat cried out.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat, that they said, Surely it is the king of Israel. And they turned aside to fight against him: and Jehoshaphat cried out.
text.NABRE: When the chariot commanders saw Jehoshaphat, they cried out, “There is the king of Israel!” and wheeled to fight him. But Jehoshaphat cried out,
text.DRB: When therefore the Princes of the chariots had seen Josaphat, they suspected that he was the king of Israel, & making a violent assault they fought against him: and Josaphat cried out.

text.NRSV: When the captains of the chariots saw that it was not the king of Israel, they turned back from pursuing him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the chariot-captains saw that it was, not the king of Israel, that they turned back, from pursuing him.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when the captains of the chariots perceived that it was not the king of Israel, that they turned back from pursuing him.
text.NABRE: and the chariot commanders, seeing that he was not the king of Israel, turned away from him.
text.DRB: And the Princes of the chariots perceived that he was not the king of Israel, and they ceased from him.

text.NRSV: But a certain man drew his bow and unknowingly struck the king of Israel between the scale armor and the breastplate; so he said to the driver of his chariot, “Turn around, and carry me out of the battle, for I am wounded.”
text.Rotherham: But, a certain man, drawing a bow in his innocence, smote the king of Israel, between the shoulder-joints and the coat of mail,—wherefore he said to his charioteer—Turn thy hand, and convey me out of the host, for I am sore wounded.
text.KJV: And a certain man drew a bow at a venture, and smote the king of Israel between the joints of the harness: wherefore he said unto the driver of his chariot, Turn thine hand, and carry me out of the host; for I am wounded.
text.NABRE: But someone drew his bow at random, and hit the king of Israel between the joints of his breastplate. He ordered his charioteer, “Rein about and take me out of the ranks, for I am wounded.”
text.DRB: And a certain man bent his bow; directing the arrow at all adventure, and by chance he struck the king of Israel between the lungs and the stomach. But he said to his coacher: Turn thy hand, and carry me out of the army, because I am grievously wounded.

text.NRSV: The battle raged that day, and the king was propped up in his chariot facing the Arameans, until at evening he died; the blood from the wound had flowed into the bottom of the chariot.
text.Rotherham: But the battle increased that day, and, the king, was propped up in the chariot, before the Syrians,—and died in the evening, and so the blood of the wound ran out into the hollow of the chariot.
text.KJV: And the battle increased that day: and the king was stayed up in his chariot against the Syrians, and died at even: and the blood ran out of the wound into the midst of the chariot.
text.NABRE: The battle grew fierce during the day, and the king, who was propped up in his chariot facing the Arameans, died in the evening. The blood from his wound flowed to the bottom of the chariot.
text.DRB: The battle therefore was fought that day, and the king of Israel stood in his chariot against the Syrians, and he died in the evening: and the blood of the wound ran into the midst of the chariot,

text.NRSV: Then about sunset a shout went through the army, “Every man to his city, and every man to his country!”
text.Rotherham: And a loud cry went through the host, at the going in of the sun, saying—Every man to his own city! and every man to his own land!
text.KJV: And there went a proclamation throughout the host about the going down of the sun, saying, Every man to his city, and every man to his own country.
text.NABRE: At sunset a cry went through the army, “Every man to his city, every man to his land!”
text.DRB: and the herald sounded in all the army before the sun set, saying: Every man return into his city, and into his country.

text.NRSV: So the king died, and was brought to Samaria; they buried the king in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: So the king died, and was brought into Samaria,—and they buried the king, in Samaria.
text.KJV: So the king died, and was brought to Samaria; and they buried the king in Samaria.
text.NABRE: And so the king died, and came back to Samaria, and they buried him there.
text.DRB: And the king died, and was carried into Samaria: and they buried the king in Samaria,

text.NRSV: They washed the chariot by the pool of Samaria; the dogs licked up his blood, and the prostitutes washed themselves in it, according to the word of the Lord that he had spoken.
text.Rotherham: And, when the chariot was washed out at the pool of Samaria, the dogs lapped up his blood, also, the harlots, bathed [there],—according to the word of Yahweh which he had spoken.
text.KJV: And one washed the chariot in the pool of Samaria; and the dogs licked up his blood; and they washed his armour; according unto the word of the Lord which he spake.
text.NABRE: When they washed out the chariot at the pool of Samaria, the dogs licked up his blood and prostitutes bathed there, as the Lord had prophesied.
text.DRB: & washed his chariot in the pool of Samaria, and the dogs licked his blood, and they washed the reins of the bridle, according to the word of our Lord which he had spoken.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Ahab, and all that he did, and the ivory house that he built, and all the cities that he built, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Ahab, and all that he did, and the house of ivory that he built, and all the cities that he built, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.KJV: Now the rest of the acts of Ahab, and all that he did, and the ivory house which he made, and all the cities that he built, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Ahab, with all that he did, including the ivory house he built and all the cities he built, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Achab, and all that he did, and the house of ivory that he built, and of all the cities that he built, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?

text.NRSV: So Ahab slept with his ancestors; and his son Ahaziah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: So Ahab slept with his fathers,—and, Ahaziah his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: So Ahab slept with his fathers; and Ahaziah his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Ahab rested with his ancestors, and his son Ahaziah succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: Achab therefore slept with his fathers, and Ochozias his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat son of Asa began to reign over Judah in the fourth year of King Ahab of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, Jehoshaphat son of Asa, began to reign over Judah,—in the fourth year of Ahab, king of Israel.
text.KJV: And Jehoshaphat the son of Asa began to reign over Judah in the fourth year of Ahab king of Israel.
text.NABRE: Reign of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat, son of Asa, became king of Judah in the fourth year of Ahab, king of Israel.
text.DRB: But Josaphat the son of Asa began to reign over Juda the fourth year of Achab the king of Israel.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat was thirty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-five years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Azubah daughter of Shilhi.
text.Rotherham: Jehoshaphat, was thirty-five years old when he began to reign, and, twenty-five years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and, the name of his mother, was Azubah, daughter of Shilhi.
text.KJV: Jehoshaphat was thirty and five years old when he began to reign; and he reigned twenty and five years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Azubah the daughter of Shilhi.
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat was thirty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned twenty-five years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Azubah, daughter of Shilhi.
text.DRB: Five and thirty years old was he when he began to reign, and five and twenty years he reigned in Jerusalem: the name of his mother was Azuba the daughter of Salai.

text.NRSV: He walked in all the way of his father Asa; he did not turn aside from it, doing what was right in the sight of the Lord; yet the high places were not taken away, and the people still sacrificed and offered incense on the high places.
text.Rotherham: And he walked in all the way of Asa his father, he turned not aside therefrom,—doing that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh: nevertheless, the high places, were not taken away,—still were the people offering sacrifices and burning incense in the high places.
text.KJV: And he walked in all the ways of Asa his father; he turned not aside from it, doing that which was right in the eyes of the Lord: nevertheless the high places were not taken away; for the people offered and burnt incense yet in the high places.
text.NABRE: He walked in the way of Asa his father unceasingly, doing what was right in the Lord ’s sight.
text.DRB: And he walked in all the way of Asa his father, and he declined not from it: and he did that which was right in the sight of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat also made peace with the king of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoshaphat made peace with the king of Israel.
text.KJV: And Jehoshaphat made peace with the king of Israel.
text.NABRE: Nevertheless, the high places did not disappear, and the people still sacrificed on the high places and burned incense there.
text.DRB: But yet he took not away the excelses: for as yet the people did sacrifice, and burnt incense in the excelses.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, and his power that he showed, and how he warred, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jehoshaphat, and his might that he shewed, and how he warred, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the kings of Judah?
text.KJV: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, and his might that he shewed, and how he warred, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat also made peace with the king of Israel.
text.DRB: And Josaphat had peace with the king of Israel.

text.NRSV: The remnant of the male temple prostitutes who were still left in the days of his father Asa, he exterminated from the land.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, the rest of the male devotees who remained in the days of Asa his father, he consumed out of the land.
text.KJV: And the remnant of the sodomites, which remained in the days of his father Asa, he took out of the land.
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, with his valor, what he did and how he fought, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Josaphat, and his works, which he did, and his battles, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?

text.NRSV: There was no king in Edom; a deputy was king.
text.Rotherham: And, king, was there none in Edom, a prefect, was king.
text.KJV: There was then no king in Edom: a deputy was king.
text.NABRE: He removed from the land the rest of the pagan priests who had remained in the reign of Asa his father.
text.DRB: Yea and the remnant of the effeminate, which remained in the days of Asa his father, he took out of the land.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat made ships of Tarshish to go to Ophir for gold; but they did not go, for the ships were wrecked at Ezion-geber.
text.Rotherham: Jehoshaphat, made ships of Tarshish, to go to Ophir, for gold; but they went not,—for ships had been broken to pieces in Ezion-geber.
text.KJV: Jehoshaphat made ships of Tharshish to go to Ophir for gold: but they went not; for the ships were broken at Ezion–geber.
text.NABRE: There was no king in Edom, but an appointed regent.
text.DRB: Neither was there then a king appointed in Edom.

text.NRSV: Then Ahaziah son of Ahab said to Jehoshaphat, “Let my servants go with your servants in the ships,” but Jehoshaphat was not willing.
text.Rotherham: Then, said Ahaziah son of Ahab, unto Jehoshaphat, Let, my servants, go with, thy servants, in the ships,—but Jehoshaphat did not consent.
text.KJV: Then said Ahaziah the son of Ahab unto Jehoshaphat, Let my servants go with thy servants in the ships. But Jehoshaphat would not.
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat made Tarshish ships to go to Ophir for gold; but in fact the ships did not go, because they were wrecked at Ezion-geber.
text.DRB: But king Josaphat had made navies on the sea, which should sail into Ophir for gold: and they could not go, because they were broken in Asiongaber.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat slept with his ancestors and was buried with his ancestors in the city of his father David; his son Jehoram succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: So Jehoshaphat slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers, in the city of David his father,—and, Jehoram his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: And Jehoshaphat slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David his father: and Jehoram his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: That was the time when Ahaziah, son of Ahab, had said to Jehoshaphat, “Let my servants accompany your servants in the ships.” But Jehoshaphat would not agree.
text.DRB: Then said Ochozias the son of Achab to Josaphat: Let my servants go with thy servants in the ships. And Josaphat would not.

text.NRSV: Ahaziah son of Ahab began to reign over Israel in Samaria in the seventeenth year of King Jehoshaphat of Judah; he reigned two years over Israel.
text.Rotherham: Ahaziah son of Ahab, began to reign over Israel, in Samaria, in the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah,—and reigned over Israel, two years.
text.KJV: Ahaziah the son of Ahab began to reign over Israel in Samaria the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, and reigned two years over Israel.
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat rested with his ancestors; he was buried with his ancestors in the City of David his father, and his son Jehoram succeeded him as king. Reign of Ahaziah.
text.DRB: And Josaphat slept with his fathers, and was buried with them in the City of David his father: and Joram his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, and walked in the way of his father and mother, and in the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—and went in the way of his father, and in the way of his mother, and in the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused, Israel, to sin.
text.KJV: And he did evil in the sight of the Lord, and walked in the way of his father, and in the way of his mother, and in the way of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who made Israel to sin:
text.NABRE: Ahaziah, son of Ahab, became king over Israel in Samaria in the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah; he reigned two years over Israel.
text.DRB: And Ochozias the son of Achab began to reign over Israel in Samaria, in the seventeenth year of Josaphat the king of Juda, and he reigned over Israel two years.

text.NRSV: He served Baal and worshiped him, and provoked the Lord, the God of Israel, to anger, just as his father had done.
text.Rotherham: Yea he served Baal, and bowed down to him,—and provoked to anger Yahweh, God of Israel, according to all that his father had done.
text.KJV: For he served Baal, and worshipped him, and provoked to anger the Lord God of Israel, according to all that his father had done.
text.NABRE: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, walking in the way of his father, his mother, and Jeroboam, son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin.
text.DRB: And he did evil in the sight of our Lord, and walked in the way of his father and his mother, and in the way of Jeroboam the son of Nabat, who made Israel to sin.

4.22: Solomon’s provisions for each day were thirty kors of fine flour, sixty kors of meal,
4.23: ten fatted oxen, twenty pasture-fed oxen, and a hundred sheep, not counting harts, gazelles, roebucks, and fatted fowl.
4.24: He had dominion over all the land west of the River, from Tiphsah to Gaza, and all its kings, and he had peace on all his borders round about.
4.25: Thus Judah and Israel lived in security, everyone under their own vine and fig tree from Dan to Beer-sheba, as long as Solomon lived.
4.27: The governors, one for each month, provided food for King Solomon and for all the guests at King Solomon’s table. They left nothing unprovided.
4.28: For the chariot horses and draft animals also, each brought his quota of barley and straw to the required place.
4.30: Solomon’s wisdom surpassed that of all the peoples of the East and all the wisdom of Egypt.
4.31: He was wiser than anyone else—wiser than Ethan the Ezrahite, or Heman, Chalcol, and Darda, the musicians—and his fame spread throughout the neighboring peoples.
4.32: Solomon also uttered three thousand proverbs, and his songs numbered a thousand and five.
4.33: He spoke of plants, from the cedar on Lebanon to the hyssop growing out of the wall, and he spoke about beasts, birds, reptiles, and fishes.
